1256144980.title: Cancellation Inquiry SOP (DSC2S) - VCN
1256144980.author: Former user (Deleted)
1256144980.body:
Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN

Last Updated:




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Cancellation Inquiry. 

Suppliers enter these tickets when they:

  • can’t find the PO in their open orders
  • don’t see a way to generate the shipping labels

These issues are caused due to the PO having a Cancelled status.


Overview

Add text here.




Resources & Tools



EU


Resource/Tool 

Use Case

EU SLAs 

To check the SLA for RPs for EU POs. 

NA SLAs

To check the SLA for RPs for NA POs. 

Utilized to access all templates applicable to this workflow. 





Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

Credit Strip

A credit strip is what we are seeking for reimbursement. It will show the final action taken, closed or deducted or charged back (deduction reversed), the dates, who processed it and the associated voucher #.










#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow


mediumfalse

Unless designated with greenlocationNA Specific or magentalocationEU Specific, process should be presumed as being globalized.



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

  • You can see the Customer Request Type, PO Number and the reason for the cancellation request (Visual 1).

Visual 1:



Confirm Current PO Status


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the PO

  1. From the Order tab, scroll down to Products In This Order. (Visual 1.1)
  2. Find the relevant PO number. This will show a Canceled status if the cancellation has already been confirmed.

This will also be visible in the Product tab under Service History. (Visual 1.2)


Tip 1

To review the PO status in Partner Home:

  1. Open the Partner Home portal
  2. Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.3)
  3. Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders
  4. Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO.  The PO status will be listed under Fulfillment Status. (Visual 1.4)


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

Visual 1.3:

Visual 1.4:

2

Confirm Who Initiated Cancellation

To confirm who initiated the cancellation, search for the PO in the Order Trail, from the Order tab. (Visual 2.1)

  • Customer initiated the cancellation (Visual 2.2)
  • Wayfair initiated the cancellation (Visual 2.3)
  • Supplier initiated the cancellation (Visual 2.4)

 Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

Visual 2.4:



PO Needs to be Cancelled


mediuminfo

Item is Discontinued

  • If the item is discontinued on the website, cancellation should be processed and partial cancellations that the supplier cancelled in full should be routed to a new PO. 


Supplier received cancellation request, but can’t approve it because the cancellation alert isn’t available


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review OrderReview order and confirm it is not shipped

2

Confirm customer wants PO cancelled

Confirm customer wants the PO canceled and didn’t call in to request the cancellation be reversed

IF: Alert was Auto-Denied

Run the cancellation wizard


IF: Cancellation Denied and can be Approved

Cancel the PO via bulk cancellation and respond to the supplier with the Cancellation Denied and can be Approved template.


Cancellation Alert Clarification

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Confirm/Deny Pending Cancellation template.


IF: Order was Marked Shipped

Run WIMS, incomplete shipment, refund customer, and advise supplier we can’t cancel but the customer was refunded


IF: Customer Requested Cancellation be Reversed

Redrop the PO to the supplier, canceling this order and sending them a new one.



3Send TemplateRespond utilizing the appropriate script. 



PO Not Cancelled


This section explains how to address the suppliers cancellation queries before the order has been cancelled. 

Depending on the Supplier's query, move to the relevant tab to provide them with the correct expectations:

  1. How to Action Cancellation Request
  2. Cancellation Request is Denied 



How to Action Cancellation Request


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Update Supplier

If a customer has requested to cancel an order, but the Supplier has not actioned this request, the status will appear as Pending Cancellation/Cancellation Requested. 

If the Supplier can cancel the order, use the Confirm_Pending_Cancellation template to provide them with instructions on how to do so. 

If the Supplier is unable to cancel the order, use the Deny_Pending_Cancellation template to advise them of the next steps and expectations for shipping the order. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




Cancellation Request is Denied


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Update Supplier 


If the Supplier has denied the customer's cancellation request, this will show as denied in the Order Trail.

Use the Cancellation_Denied template to update the supplier of the next steps. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.







Supplier Requests I cancel the PO



Review Reason for Cancellation


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Reason for Cancellation


Item Discontinuation
  • Confirm if item is showing discontinued on the website
  • Check if the item is available from another supplier
    • If available from another supplier reroute
    • If not available from another supplier proceed with cancelling discontinued item if showing as discontinued on website. Proceed as item is out of stock if it is not showing as discontinued


PO Transmission Error
  • Go to and navigate to PO Data Issues tab


Regulatory Compliance
  • Go to and navigate to Supplier Self Service Updates > Unable to Ship Product Compliance or Service Area Issue tab


SCT Alternative Resolution

If an alternative resolution is confirmed in the SCT ticket but the part or unit did not cancel, Global DS C2S should cancel as requested.


Other
  • If details are not provided on the reason for cancellation request please utilize the cancellation push back template
  • Items is out of stock follow order delays item out of stock process
  • PO was cancelled internally due to stock follow the PO reroute policy
  • PO not received, follow PO resend policy
  • Supplier is closing/going out of business/no longer operating, follow item discontinued SOP



2

Determine Item Eligibility for Cancellation

Determine if item is eligible for cancellation (can utilize the existing box in step 1) if eligibility cannot be determined please utilize the ticket help channel.


3

Utilize Template

Update ticket with the Cancellation Pushback template and put the ticket in Waiting for Supplier. If the supplier is non responsive on the ticket's rebound, close with no further action.




20+ POs to be Cancelled

magentalocationEU Specific

#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Validate Cancellation Reason 

If the SU is requesting the PO(s) to be canceled, they must provide valid reasons. If this has not been provided, ask the Supplier to confirm.

  1. Utilize the Cancellation Pushback template to request the supplier confirm the reasons for requiring PO cancellation.
  2. Edit the template to suit the Suppliers situation. (if SU has not replied after bounce back close ticket).

2

Create a Mass Error Ticket

  1. If more than 20 POs for a single SU require cancellation with valid reasons by the SU then enter a Mass Error ticket. (Visual 2.1)
  2. Request within the Mass Error ticket that notes are added to the order regarding reasons for cancellation - Reason for cancelation is based on what SU has advised eg. Incorrect part numbers, discontinued no ETA on restock date, issues with stock no restock date. 
  3. Link the tickets, by expanding the Linked Tickets dropdown and selecting Add. (Visual 2.2) 
  4. Enter the Issue ID for the Customer Information Needed ticket and select Validate.
  5. Change the Relationship to Relates to and select Link Ticket (Visual 2.3)

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

3

Cancel the POs

Cancel POs using the Bulk Cancellation Tool 


4

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Needs PO Cancelled template.


5

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab





How to Cancel an Order



Bulk Cancellation Tool


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Access the Bulk Cancellation Tool
  1. Stay on the “Initiate Cancellation” tab and choose “Purchase order numbers”
  2. Enter the PO number(s)
  3. Choose reason from the drop down menu
  4. Mark “Yes” for the cancellation has been confirmed mark the checkboxes next to both “Send Customer Email” and “Send Supplier Email”
  5. Click the “Initiate Cancellations” button
  6. This will run the cancellation and take you to the “Cancellation Results” tab where the request will show as “Queued”

After a few minutes, if you refresh the page the status will update to show the order as “Canceled”




Cancellation Wizard


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

View the Order

Open the order page


2

Click on "Order Tab"Under ticket details, choose the “Order Tab”

3

Click the Order Number

Click the order number to open the order page


4

Open the Cancellation Wizard
  1. At the top of the order page, locate and open the cancellation wizard
  2. Items that can be canceled will show with the status of “Eligible”
  3. Verify that the PO listed in the ticket matches the eligible item
  4. Select the quantity and hit continue
  5. Continue with sending the confirmation email to the current email address
  6. Choose the reason for cancellation as “OPS - Order Processing Issue/Wayfair Decision to Cancel, and hit continue





Cancellation Exceptions



Supplier Cancelled, We Did Not


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Why Cancellation Did Not Occur

If there is a glitch and the PO shows as open with no pending cancellation and the customer requested cancellation:

  1. Run the cancellation wizard again and follow the prompts to cancel.
  2. PO should automatically confirm as canceled.
    1. If it does not, you can confirm it as the supplier has confirmed the PO is canceled.


The supplier is reporting the item is out of stock:

  1. Refer to the Backorder Notification section and fill or kill section.
  2. Cancel if appropriate or direct the supplier to backorder.


If a supplier is requesting to cancel a sample PO:

  1. Proceed with fulfilling their request and canceling the PO. 



Special Order Cancellation Process (MTO)


  • Made to order items can be canceled prior to the “point of no return” during production
  • This point is based on the supplier’s production schedule, so best practice is to contact the supplier and verify if an item is still eligible to be canceled.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

IF: Item can be cancelled

If the item can still be canceled, continue the cancellation process as normal


2

IF: Item cannot be cancelled

If the item cannot be canceled, deny the cancellation request






Clarity on Credit Strips


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Replacement Part Cancellation Credit

This is a credit strip for a Replacement Part Cancellation Credit. This one is Closed and no longer active because its in Blue. If it was still open pending action from the Supplier Claims team it would be White and say "New" or "Final Submit". (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

New - Pending Credit Strip

This credit strip is one that is New - Pending action from the Supplier Claims Team. The credit investigation and info would be addressed in the GI Ticket entered by the supplier. The credit strip shows the amount of money we are attempting to recover from the supplier and what the status of that credit is once the investigation is complete, resolution has been provided to the SU and the ticket is closed. (Visual 2)

Visual 2:



Cancellation Queries


mediuminfo

Suppliers will often reach out regarding an order that has been cancelled and inquire about the reason for the cancellation.  We can provide information around who confirmed the cancellation and if it is a valid cancellation. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the request in the ticket and confirm the status of the PO

We can view the status of a PO on the Order Tab or Product Tab in ServiceHub

If a PO has been partially cancelled, we may need to redrop any active items to create a new PO for fulfillment


2

Confirm who cancelled an order in the Order Notes
  • On the Order Tab, scroll to the bottom of the Order Trail and increase the Items per page to the maximum number
  • Use Ctrl + F to search for the PO number in the Order Trail
  • The Order Notes will show whether the cancellation was confirmed by the Supplier, Wayfair, or processed automatically 

3

Send Template

Send the appropriate template to the supplier, and close the ticket out under a Done status or transfer to Finance as necessary 




PO is Returned/Refunded and Needs to be Cancelled


mediuminfo

Wizard has been run on an unshipped order to process a refund to the customer.  Suppliers may not be aware of this action, but they will be unable to process an order so will open a ticket to request assistance with fulfillment.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the order to check if the PO has been refunded via Shipping View

Navigate to the Legacy Order View and select the Shipping View tab, then locate the PO to check the refund status (Visual 1)

Visual 1:


2

Check the PO status to see if it is marked ‘Shipped’ or ‘Pending Ship Confirmation’


IF: Pending Ship Confirmation

If ‘Pending Ship Confirmation’, delete return line in Shipping View and cancel PO via Bulk Cancellation Tool


IF: Shipped

If ‘Shipped’, no additional action is necessary



3

When status changes fail to apply, this is a tech issue currently ongoing
  1. This Section applies to all POs
    1. In the "Returned/Replaced" status 
    2. The SU requests a status update to "Shipped" or "Canceled"
    3. A replacement PO has been issued against the original
    4. The system does not allow changes due to a technical Issue


  1. Unable to action SU request
    1. You have followed all other steps in this SOP to resolve the SUs query and the tech issue is stopping the resolution required.


4

Add Example

Add example PO and ticket reported in to this Sheet (Visual 4)

Visual 4:

5Respond to SU

Send the appropriate template based on the ship status to the supplier.

The template options include:

  1. Returned/Refunded but NOT Shipped 
  2. Returned/Refunded but HAS Shipped
  3. Unable to Cancel PO (Returned/Refunded PO)


6Close Ticket

Close the ticket out under a Done resolution.




Replacement Part Cancellation


mediuminfo

Part information tickets pause the SLA for the SU eg. 10 days to ship part - if the SU raises a PIN ticket and RPOPS Take 5 days to resolve this ticket the SU has 15 day SLA for this part.

(SLA Extended by X days to resolution on first open and closed date, if multiple tickets on the same part the SLA will not be paused only on the first ticket created).


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Identify the Reason for Cancellation Outreach from SU

SLA - SU breached SLA (SLAs are geo specific):

  • EU SLAs 
  • NA SLAs 

2

EU and NA Investigation

EU Investigation:

  1. Check the order notes to see if an RPOPS ticket was generated.
    1. The categories are EU SLA breach - SP & EU SLA breach - LP.
  2. If a ticket was generated, the part was cancelled due to an SLA breach; respond to SU with RP_Cancellation_Full_Unit template. 
  3. If a ticket was not generated, proceed to step 3.


NA Investigation:

  1. Check if the order notes to see if an RPOPS ticket was generated
    1. The categories are SLA breach - SP & SLA breach - LP.
  2. If a ticket was generated, the part was cancelled due to an SLA breach; respond to SU with RP_Cancellation_Full_Unit template. 
  3. If a ticket was not generated, check to see if the order notes show that the parts were cancelled & the item was replaced automatically.
    1. If not, proceed to step 3.

Visual 2:

3

Customer Driven Cancellation

The customer decided to cancel the replacement part (i.e. chose an alternative resolution).

    1. If no RPOPS ticket was created or no SLA breached, you can assume the customer just decided they preferred a different resolution.
      1. You can advise the SU to dispute deduction requests for any associated costs on the deduction page; respond to SU with CT_Cancelled_RP_Request template.
    2. If an RPOPS ticket was entered, proceed to step 4.

4

SU Driven Cancellation

The supplier used a workflow to cancel the replacement part. 

  1. Check the order notes to see if the supplier entered an RPOPS ticket to cancel the replacement part; respond to SU with SU_Driven_Part_Cancellation template.
    1. Ticket categories = Part Information needed/Order Clarification Needed/Problem with an Order/Whole Unit Needed/Item Discontinued/Past warranty).
  2. If the SU entered a “Whole unit needed” or “item Discontinued” ticket, the part is automatically cancelled & RPOPS issue an alternative resolution; respond to SU with SU_Driven_Part_Cancellation template.
  3. If a “customer clarification”/”Problem with an order”/”Part information needed” ticket was entered, please read through the ticket to see if the supplier advised to cancel the part or if RPOPS advised the part needed to be cancelled; respond to SU with SU_Driven_Part_Cancellation template.

5SU Liability

SU is liable for all alternative resolutions in EU for the following; 

  1. if the SU is reaching out questioning why a replacement part is being cancelled and replaced; respond to SU with SU_Disputes_Full_Unit - Post_Ship template.
  2. When opening the order if the replacement part has been cancelled due to the new process you will see the below on the order notes; “If a supplier advises that they shipped a cancel part & a whole unit replacement has already been ordered, please advise them that the replacement unit cannot be cancelled & need to be fulfilled. If they push back on the associated costs, advise them to dispute any associated deduction requests in Partner Home.“ Respond to SU with SU_Disputes_Full_Unit - Post_Ship template.



PO Mis-Shipment: Wayfair Cancellation Error


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Order

Review the order to check if/how cancellation was confirmed

  1. If PO was canceled by Wayfair in error and supplier is unable to retrieve the item, transfer to Finance.

2

Send Template

Send Template: Incorrect Cancellation by Wayfair




Miscellaneous Processes



PO Cancelled without Supplier Confirmation


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Order Trail

If the PO was backordered with a new ship date of more than 72 hours, this results in any cancellation requests being approved, even if the PO is MTO (Made to Order). 

You can identify if the PO has been backordered from the Order Trail. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Send Template

Use the Cancelled_via_BO template to update the supplier of the next steps.


3

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




Supplier Charged for Shipping


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Supplier Charged for Shipping
  • If the PO is registered/label created prior to cancellation, we are charged by the carrier for this
  • If the supplier still ships the order after the cancellation is approved by the supplier via the alert, Wayfair still receives invoices from the carrier. Per Wayfair policy, we are not responsible for costs on canceled orders



Order Shipped After Cancellation Confirmed


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Supplier Ships Order after Confirming Cancellation

If the supplier confirms an order canceled on Partner Home, but still ships the order, the supplier is responsible for all costs related to the order




Requesting Payment

magentalargeEU Specific

#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Update Supplier

If the Supplier requests that then cancellation be reversed or the shipping status is changed, use the Cancelled_Payment_Request template to update them of the next steps. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




Incorrect Pricing


The wholesale price on the PO doesn’t match SU records; often due to missed promotions, inaccurate price updates, or upload errors.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Ticket
  1. The SU has requested cancellation of the PO due to incorrect pricing, as the listed price is lower than the correct wholesale cost they should receive.
  2.  Navigate to for next steps in reviewing and resolving SU queries.





Resolve Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Select Template

Use the relevant template to update the supplier of the action taken. 

Templates for each scenario can be found . 

Ensure you make any necessary edits to the templates so these are tailored to the Supplier's specific query. 



2

Editing Comment (Optional)
  1. If you need to make any edits to your comment after this is added, click the pencil icon (Visual 2.1).
  2. Select Change Status > Done (Visual 2.2).


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

1256144848.title: Cannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label SOP (DSC2S) - VCN
1256144848.author: Jenn Gill
1256144848.body:
Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN 

Last Updated:  




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Cannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label. 

Suppliers enter these tickets when they need help printing their labels. This may be driven by various tech issues which are explained below. 

These tickets may also be entered by the Supplier when they have queries about their labels. 


Overview

Print Label Failure tickets are submitted for a number of reasons - select the most relevant one from the options. When an error occurs, the specific error message can be found in the order notes as well as in the ticket directly in the "Reason for failure" section.



Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool

Use Case











Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition











Before You Start


General Concepts

Guidance

Error is not showing

Go to NA > Common Label Print Errors > Error Isn't Showing expand.

Suppliers push back on creating their own labels for swatch requests

Proceed with cancellation;  excessive time should not be spent on swatch label requests. 

Reroute and Changing Ship Method

Must be requested by the supplier or confirmed in ticket help as necessary prior to being actioned.

API Registration Failures

API registration must be from the warehouse we sent the PO to. If suppliers are shipping from a different warehouse they must register in Partner Home.





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Review Ticket



Review the Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type and PO Number. (Visual 1)


These tickets are created in two ways: 

  1. Generated by the system when a label fails to print – see System Generated
  2. Entered manually by the Supplier – see Supplier Entered 

Visual 1:

2

Review the Description

Review the ticket to confirm the Supplier's query. 

If they are experiencing an error printing their labels, move to the appropriate regional tab.

If they are requesting a different number of labels, need help with a replacement part, or have a query regarding manifests or commercial invoices, move to the Additional Queries tab (this is specific to the EU space at this time). 

How to Identify a Replacement Part

A replacement part is indicated by a PO number with '-R-' and a number following it. 

For example UK123456789-R-12345.





System Generated


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

If an error prevents the Supplier from printing their label, this may automatically generate a ticket.

The reason for the label failure will appear on these tickets as highlighted. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:



Supplier Entered


The supplier might enter a ticket manually for the following reasons: 

  1. They experienced an error printing their label and the system did not automatically create a ticket 
  2. They require a different amount of labels to what they printed
  3. They are unable to print a label for a replacement part order
  4. They have a query regarding manifests or commercial invoices
  5. The customer has listed a PO Box or APO address which cannot be shipped to; the PO will need to be cancelled 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Supplier entered tickets will contain a description like this example below where the description is not auto-populated like the system generated and instead the Supplier has personally described the issue they are facing printing labels. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Access the Order Trail

In cases where an error prevented the label from printing, you will be able to confirm the error type in the Order Trail. 

These error notes will appear as shown in Visual 2.1.

The Order Trail is a record of all the actions taken place on an order and any notes left internally. 

  1. Select the Order tab.
  2. Scroll towards the bottom until you reach the Order Trail. (Visual 2.2)


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Confirm the Request

Review the ticket to confirm the Supplier's query. 

If they are experiencing an error printing their labels, move to the following tab. 

If they are requesting a different number of labels, need help with a replacement part, or have a query regarding manifests or commercial invoices, move to the Additional Queries tab. 

How to Identify a Replacement Part

A replacement part is indicated by a PO number with '-R-' and a number following it. 

For example UK123456789-R-12345.







NA



Common Label Print Errors


This section explains how to troubleshoot common label printing errors.

Based on the error message appearing in the ticket or the Order Trail, navigate to the relevant section below: 


Shipping Address


The email is missing from the shipping address


On occasion a PO will fail to register as the email address is missing from the shipping info. When this occurs you will not receive an automatic ticket and you will not see an error on the order.


Labels cannot be created for VI or PR


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Navigate to "Run a Wizard"

Navigate to the legacy view by selecting ‘Run Wizard,’ (Visual 1.1) and choosing ‘More Wizards’ in ServiceHub (Visual 1.2)

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Copy the Customer's Email Address

Copy CT's email address from under "Billing Info" or "Account Info" then click the pencil icon in the "Shipping Contact Information" box. (Visual 2)   

Visual 2:

3

Paste the Email Address

Paste the email into the ‘Email’ box, and click submit. (Visual 3)

Visual 3:

4

Log into Partner Home

Log into Partner Home as the Supplier, and navigate to Dropship Orders.

  1. Search for the PO
  2. Check the box next to the PO
  3. Click the Refresh button
  4. Register order


5Send Template

Attach the labels, update ticket with General Label Print Error Resolved template, and mark the ticket as done




Update CT Address

An error in the CTs name and address can sometimes prevent labels from printing. 

The below are some error messages that may appear if there is an issue with a customer's name/address:

  • NoSuchMethodError:com.google.gson.JsonParser.parseString(Ljava/lang/String;)Lcom/google/gson/JsonElement;
  • An error occurred when printing shipping labels
  • An error occurred. Try again later. Number is bigger than the 15 characters at shipmentRequest.shipment.shipper.phone.number,Number is bigger than the 15 characters at shipmentRequest.shipment.shipFrom.phone.number
  • NoSuchMethodError:com.google.gson.JsonParser.parseString(Ljava/lang/String;)Lcom/google/gson/JsonElement;
  • An error occurred when printing shipping labels
  • An error occurred. Try again later. City "Hemingford Grey Huntingdon" is too long (limit: 40 characters) at city


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Navigate to "Run a Wizard"

From the “Run a Wizard” button, select “More Wizards” to navigate to the Order View. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Select the POSelect the appropriate PO. (Visual 2)

Visual 2:

3

Edit the Address

Edit address so it follows the correct format (name, street, and city on separate lines). (Visual 3)

Visual 3:

4

Verify Address is Correct

Double check the address is correct, and submit without sending an email update to the customer. (Visual 4)

Visual 4:

5Log in to Partner Home

Log into Partner Home as the supplier, and approve the address change request via the Order Alerts page. (Visual 5)

Visual 5:

6Refresh the PO

Refresh the PO


7Generate Documents

Generate the documents


8Send Template

Attach the labels, update ticket with General Label Print Error Resolved Template, and mark the ticket as done





Ship Speed


There is an error because the shipping method we currently have assigned is not valid for the area the PO is being shipped to/from and usually only occurs with “Express” (or next day) delivery


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Change Ship Speed
  1. To change ship speed, go to order view and run the “shipping or service upgrades”  
  2. Identify which item(s) need to be adjusted
  3. Check the box of the item(s) that need a ship speed change on the right. 
  4. Click Continue at the bottom
  5. Select a downgraded ship speed. 
    1. Example: If the order is set to ship Express, select Expedited.
  6. If additional charges are applied to the customers total, select Allow Discount.
  7. Select “Wrong Ship Speed/Method” from the drop down.
  8. Click “Allow Discount as Percentage (%)”
  9. Enter “100” in the Discount % box
  10. Click Continue 
  11. Select “Do Not Send Email to Customer”
  12. The first change we make may not correct the error, so we don’t want to email the customer just yet
  13. Click Submit

2Confirm Ship Speed Change Alert

From here, we will need to confirm the ship speed change alert we generated by running the Shop Speed Change Wizard. The easiest way to pull up this alert is to access the suppliers alerts through System Weather.

  1. Click the name of the supplier to pull up their Order Alerts
  2. Click the Ship Speed Changes tab
  3. Select “Yes” for “Change Speed?”
  4. Click Save Changes

3Refresh the PO

Once the alert has been confirmed, the PO will need to be refreshed.

  1. Refresh PO in Partner Home
  2. Pull up PO in Dropship Orders via Partner Home

STANDARD_OVERNIGHT is not supported for the destination


There is an error because the shipping method we currently have assigned is not valid for the area the PO is being shipped to/from and usually only occurs with “Express” (or next day) delivery


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Change Ship Speed
  1. To change ship speed, go to order view and run the “shipping or service upgrades”  
  2. Identify which item(s) need to be adjusted
  3. Check the box of the item(s) that need a ship speed change on the right. 
  4. Click Continue at the bottom
  5. Select a downgraded ship speed. 
    1. Example: If the order is set to ship Express, select Expedited.
  6. If additional charges are applied to the customers total, select Allow Discount.
  7. Select “Wrong Ship Speed/Method” from the drop down.
  8. Click “Allow Discount as Percentage (%)”
  9. Enter “100” in the Discount % box
  10. Click Continue 
  11. Select “Do Not Send Email to Customer”
  12. The first change we make may not correct the error, so we don’t want to email the customer just yet
  13. Click Submit

2

Confirm Ship Speed Change Alert

 From here, we will need to confirm the ship speed change alert we generated by running the Shop Speed Change Wizard. The easiest way to pull up this alert is to access the suppliers alerts through System Weather.

  1. Click the name of the supplier to pull up their Order Alerts
  2. Click the Ship Speed Changes tab
  3. Select “Yes” for “Change Speed?”
  4. Click Save Changes

3

Refresh the PO

Once the alert has been confirmed, the PO will need to be refreshed

  1. Refresh PO in Partner Home
  2. Pull up PO in Dropship Orders via Partner Home
  3. Check the boxes of the PO
  4. Click the Refresh button
  5. Register the PO

IF: Labels are Generated

5IF: Same Error is Generated

Request Onshore Support


If you require support from the In House team, you can request onshore support via the ticket you are working.

The In House team will take over ownership of your ticket and remove this from your queue. 

Always update your current ticket with an internal comment to confirm the steps you have taken to try to resolve the issue before requesting support. 

You may also request specific language support from the In House team if the Supplier reopens a ticket with an update in German, Polish or Mandarin with questions requiring an in-depth response. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Add Manual Trigger

Navigate to the Templates drop down menu.

Select the Manual Trigger (POS AOPS) category. (Visual 1.1)

Choose the Onshore Support Required template. (Visual 1.2)

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Add Escalation Template

The template will automatically populate in the comment section. (Visual 2)

Template

Dropship Order Fulfillment Support Needed  

SOP Followed: [Provide details]  

Steps Taken: [Provide details]  

Any Other Comments: [Provide details]


Do not make any edits to the first sentence: Dropship Order Fulfillment Support Needed.

This is required to trigger the automatic escalation process. 

Remove [Provide details] from each line.


Visual 2:

3

Complete Template

Complete the template by filling in:

  • A link to the SOP followed.
  • Steps you took, if any.
  • Any additional comments (Tip 1)

Select Post Internally to ensure that the comment is not visible to suppliers. (Visual 3)

Once the comment is added, the automation will be triggered to escalate the ticket. This will move the ticket out of your queue and assign it to an In House agent. 

An external comment will be automatically added to the ticket, informing the Supplier of the next steps. 

Template

Hi Team, 

Thank you for reaching out regarding issues on {{Customer Request type raised on initial ticket}} topics. 

We are reviewing this and will provide an update as soon as possible.

This ticket will remain open to provide the next steps.

We appreciate your patience and understanding.  

Kind Regards,

You do not need to take any further action. (Tip 2)

Tip 1:

Check Current Label Print Status


This section outlines how to confirm the current status of the order to identify if what action is required to help resolve the Supplier's issue. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Log into Partner Home

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.1)

Visual 1.1:

2

Locate relevant PO

Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. 

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. (Visual 2.1)



Visual 2.1:

3

Confirm order status

If the Fulfilment Status is New or Processing, move to . (Visual 3.1)

If the status shows Awaiting Pickup, move to . 

If the status shows In Transit, move to . 

If the status shows Cancellation Requested/Cancelled, move to . 

If the status shows Delivered/Returned, move to . 






Visual 3.1:

4

New / Processing

A New status indicates that the order has not been acknowledged by the Supplier. (Tip 1)

Once the order has been acknowledged by the Supplier, the status will change to Processing. 

Processing indicates that the Supplier has seen the order, all details are correct and they can process this be the Original Ship By Date. 

For both of these statuses, no label has been generated at this point. 

Move to the Verify Logistics Setup tab.

Tip 1:

If the New status is showing for a Replacement Part, it is not possible to print the label until the order has been acknowledged. 

See the Replacement Parts section in the Additional Queries tab for steps to resolve this issue. 



5

Awaiting Pickup

This indicates the label has already been printed.

If the Supplier has indicated in the ticket that they were able to print their labels but they require a different amount, move to the Edit Label Quantity section in the Additional Queries tab. 

Download the label from the Transportation Event History tool. (Tip 2)

Search using the PO number and click Submit. 

Under the Label Print Information you will see: 

  • Tracking Number
  • Amount of Labels
  • Carrier (LabelService (ID))
  • The Label (Label Path)

Click on the Label Print hyperlink. (Visual 5.2)

This will open a new tab where you can review the label to ensure it is correct. 

If the label count or the Must Ship By Date are incorrect, refresh the PO multiple times until the status reverts to processing and follow the steps in the Print Label tab

If the label is in order, save with the PO Number as the file title. (Visual 5.3)

Attach to the ticket for the Supplier's reference. (Visual 5.4)

Use the SU_Already_Printed_Label template to advise the Supplier of the next steps. 

Move to Step 2 of the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Tip 2:

The Transportation Event History can also be accessed via ServiceHub under the ticket description. (Visual 5.1)



Visual 5.1:

Visual 5.2:

Visual 5.3:

Visual 5.4:

6

In Transit

This indicates the label may no longer be needed. (Tip 3)

To confirm the order was shipped correctly, check the tracking for scans.

Click onto the PO result to open the PO details. (Visual 6.2)

Confirm which carrier was used and the tracking number. (Visual 6.3) 

Based on this, follow the Validating Tracking for Orders guide to confirm if there are scans on the tracking. 

Tracking has Valid Scans

Use the PO_Shipped_No_Label_Needed template to advise the Supplier of the next steps. 

Move to Step 2 of the Resolve Ticket tab. 


Tracking has No Scans

Update the ticket to confirm with the Supplier if the order has truly shipped. 

If the Supplier confirms they have shipped the order, follow the steps outlined in Tracking has Valid Scans. 

If the Supplier states this is still at the warehouse, unmark the order as shipped:

From the Product tab, select Edit Shipping/Destination Info. (Visual 6.4)

This will open a new tab with the shipping details. 

Change the Status to Pending Ship Confirmation. This will remove the ship date. 

Click remove beside the tracking number. 

Select Update. (Visual 6.5)

Advise the Supplier that order has been marked as processing and new labels have been provided. Advise them to avoid prematurely sending ASNs to prevent the order being shipped in error. 

Move to the Print Label tab to generate the new labels for the Supplier. 


Tip 3:

The order may have been marked shipped in error if the Supplier provided the Advanced Shipping Notification (ASN) too soon. 

ASNs are visible from the Order Trail. (Visual 6.1)


Visual 6.1:

Visual 6.2:

Visual 6.3:

Visual 6.4:

Visual 6.5:

7

Cancellation Requested / Cancelled

This indicates the label is no longer needed. 

Use the PO_Cancelled_Label_Failure or Labels_Pending_Cancellation template to advise the Supplier of the next steps. 

Move to Step 2 of the Resolve Ticket tab. 


8

Delivered / Returned

Delivered indicates that the product has been received. This may also occur in cases where UPS has returned the item to sender, making it appear delivered in error. (Tip 4)

The Returned status may also appear in cases where the item has been returned to sender. 

In both of these cases, we are unable to take any actions to change the status of these orders. 

If the Supplier advises the item has been Returned to Sender, enter a Customer Information Needed ticket to request Customer Service replace/refund the order. (Visual 8.1; Tip 5 and Tip 6)

Use the Item_RTS template to advise the Supplier of the next steps.

Move to Step 2 of the Resolve Ticket tab.

Tip 4:

UPS may return the item if the labels are not generated with the information necessary for delivery. 

The Supplier may enter a ticket for support with labels in these cases. 


Tip 5:

From the Order tab, check if the B2B banner is visible. (Visual 8.2) 

If the banner is visible, enter a B2B Support > Service Inquiry ticket. (Visual 8.3) 


Tip 6:

Utilise the following template when entering the ticket: 

Hi team, 

  • PO / Item number: 
  • SU Ticket number:
  • Issue: 
  • Action required:

Thank you

Verify Logistics Setup


This section outlines how to use the Logistics Matrix to identify which Carriers a Supplier is setup to work with. 

When making any changes to the Shipping Information, you should only choose a Carrier that the Supplier is already setup to ship with. 

No T0 or T1 agent should make any edits to the Logistics matrix, Only our Logistics teams can facilitate changes to the matrix, the Matrix is a View Only section for Click to Ship.



#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Logistics Matrix

From the Supplier tab, select Transportation Logistics. (Visual 1.1)

This will open the Logistics tab of Manage Supplier. 

Based on the order's destination, view the section for delivery to the relevant country. (Visual 1.2) 

Confirm the logistics set up for that country matches the Carrier listed in the Destination section of the Product View tab. (Tip 1) 

Tip 1:

The Shipping Information must always match the relevant Carrier a Supplier is setup to ship with in the Logistics Matrix. 

If this is not the case, the label will not print. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Update Shipping Info

From the Product tab, select Edit Shipping/Destination Info. (Visual 2.1)

This will open a new tab with the shipping details. 

Ensure the Delivery Type and Carrier Information is filled in. 

If it is not, update the details. (Visual 2.2, Warning 1)

If this appears to have been the issue, or if there are no obvious error messages, move to the Print Label tab. 

If there appears to be an error preventing the label from printing, move to the Common Label Print Errors tab to resolve this. 


Warning 1

Please ensure you do not edit the logistics matrix only the ship via section. If the Logistics matrix is restricted send ticket to T1 for further support.

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Update Delivery Type

  1. From the Product tab, select Edit Shipping/Destination Info. (Visual 1.1)
  2. This will open a new tab with the shipping details. 
  3. The Delivery Type should appear with UK - SP - Next day before noon as the Delivery Type.

  4. Edit the Delivery Type to 'UK - Small Parcel Courier'. 

    IF  'UK - Small Parcel Courier' is not appearing select “Ground”.


  5. This will clear the Carrier and Third Party Carrier fields - reselect Evri for each of these. 
  6. Select Update. (Visual 1.2)

Select Submit.

Country/Postcode is not covered


This section explains how to resolve errors with the reason code: Country/Postcode is not covered. 

This error occurs when there are conflicting dimensions or weight in Partner Home.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Product Dimensions

From the Order tab, select the relevant PO to navigate to the Product tab, and view the Carton Dimensions. (Visual 1.1)

Identify which carrier is assigned to the PO in the Destination section. 

Compare the Carton Dimensions to the max weight and dimensions of the carrier assigned using smalllinkMax Weights & Dims for EU Carriershttps://wayfaircorp.atlassian.net/wiki/x/KYLeSg (Tip 1 & Tip 2)

If the ship class appears to be incorrectly assigned based on the dimensions, enter a Ship Via Change ticket to request that this is updated for the product. (Tip 3)

Move to to upgrade the PO in order to print the labels. 

Tip 1:

A product will be White Glove (2-man) if:

  • Any single box exceeds 40kg; OR
  • The longest side of any box exceeds 320cm; OR
  • The girth of any box exceeds 400cm

A product will be LTL (1-man) if:

  • Any single box exceeds 30kg; OR
  • The longest side of any box exceeds 175cm; OR
  • The girth of any box exceeds 300cm

A product will be Small Parcel (SP) if:

  • Any single box does not exceed 30kg; AND
  • The longest side of each box doesn't exceed 175cm; AND
  • Girth of each box doesn't exceed 300cm; AND
  • Carton count of 10 or lower


Tip 2:

The girth is calculated using the formula:

  • Girth = Longest Side + ( 2 * (Middle Side + Shortest Side))


Tip 3:

Link the tickets, by expanding the Linked Tickets dropdown and selecting Add. (Visual 1.4)

Enter the Issue ID for the EULS ticket and select Validate.

Change the Relationship to Relates to and select Link Ticket (Visual 1.5)

This error usually occurs if the weight for an XDP order is showing as below 1kg in weight. This can be resolved by editing the weight when printing the labels. 

This error may also appear on replacement parts orders and the same resolution logic would apply.

Actions

Visuals

1

Generate the Shipping Docs

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. 

Select the check box beside the PO. 

Click Print Documents. (Visual 1.1)

This will open a new tab. 

Confirm the following: 

  • All documents selected to print
  • Warehouse (Tip 1)
  • Pickup Date
  • Confirm Labels quantity 

Select Edit Shipping Details. (Visual 1.2) 

Input the amount of labels required in Piece Count. 

Divide the Total Weight by the Piece Count to get the Unit Weight. This must be over 1kg in order for the label to print. 

Select Done Editing. (Visual 1.3) 

Select Generate Documents.  (Visual 1.2) 

Close the window. 

Move to Step 3 of the Print Label tab.

Scroll to the relevant PO and select View Carton Dims. (Visual 1.1)

While in admin view in Partner Home, select Save in the bottom right-hand corner of the page. (Visual 1.2)

Use the red banner to log in as the Supplier, which should reload the save Shipping Dimensions page. 

Select Save again. 

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Reindex the PO

If you are logged in as a Supplier in Partner Home, type Switch Back to Admin View in the red banner. (Visual 2.1; Tip 1)

Navigate to the Re-Indexing Tool. 

Paste the PO number into the text box in the Request Purchase Order Data Re-Indexing section. (Visual 2.2; Tip 2)

Select Submit. 

You should now be able to print the label as per the steps outlined in the Print Label tab. 

If this does not resolve the issue, move to the Request Onshore Support tab. 

Tip 1:

This tool can only be accessed when in Admin View and will not worked when logged in as a Supplier to Partner home. 


Tip 2:

If you need to reindex multiple POs, these can be entered together separated by a comma. 

This tool can be used to easily add commas. 


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:



Labels Printed in Wrong Format / Supplier Unable to Open Downloaded Files


This section explains how to resolve errors where the labels are generating in the incorrect format, or where the Supplier advises they are unable to open the label files they have downloaded. 

You should only provide labels in PDF format (.pdf) unless otherwise specified by the Supplier.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open label print settings

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.1)

Select the person icon in the top right-hand corner. (Visual 1.2)

Navigate to Account > Supplier Account. 

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Review the Supplier's label print settings

The Label Paper Type will depend on the printer type used by the Supplier: (Visual 2.1) 

  • Laser: Laser printer, which will generate .pdf files
  • Thermal: Thermal printer, which will generate .zpl files. 

The Page size can be dependent on the Supplier's preference. 

If the Supplier advises they need .zpl files, update the following settings: 

  • Label Paper Type: Thermal
  • Carton License Plate Label Type: Thermal
  • Thermal Format Type: ZPLII

You can also guide the Supplier on how to change their own settings if needed using the steps outlined. 

If the Supplier still requires a specific label, follow the steps outlined in the Print Label tab. 

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:



UPS account number that was provided as the payment method is missing or invalid




#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Update the Ticket

Update the ticket raised by the supplier with the template:

mediumfalse

Hi {SU Name}/Team,

Thank you for reaching out about this label printing query.

After reviewing the PO, we can see the label failed due to a technical for UPS label prints. Our engineers are working on this and aim to resolve the issue as soon as possible.

I have noted this PO number having the issue and in the meantime I will request manual UPS labels are created as a workaround. 

Once the UPS labels are created I will provide a further update on this ticket so that this order can ship without further delays. 

Kind Regards,



2

Request the Labels

To request these labels, check the shipping weights and dims and box count as this is required in the ticket entry for shipping labels.

  1. Navigate to Manage Product (Visual 2.1) selecting shipping weights and dims as show (Visual 2.2) 

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:


3

Request Manual Shipping Labels for UPS

Request a manual shipping labels for UPS here. 

  1. Follow above noted link and fill in overall order number (Visual 3.1) 
  2. Then PO number as shown below.
  3. Scrolling down you will see where to input the collection date select the following business day. (Visual 3.2) 
  4. Leave preferred delivery date blank 
  5. Collection address and contact is for the Warehouse ie. where is the goods shipping from. 
  6. Delivery address is the customer name and delivery address retrieved from the order also. 
  7. Click Create ticket. 

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:


4

Link SC- Ticket to Open POS Ticket

  1. Link the tickets, by expanding the Linked Tickets dropdown and selecting Add. (Visual 4.1)
  2. Enter the Issue ID for the CAT ticket and select Validate.
  3. Change the Relationship to Relates to and select Link Ticket (Visual 4.2)
  4. A successfully linked ticket will generate in the "Issue Links" section. (Visual 4.3)

Visual 4.1:

Visual 4.2:

Visual 4.3:

5

Update and Close Ticket

Once the labels are provided on the SC ticket, update and close the POS Ticket with the below template:

mediumfalse

Hi {SU Name}/Team,

Thank you for reaching out about this label printing query.

Please find the manually created labels attached to this ticket. Please go ahead and ship these with UPS - collection has been booked for tomorrow or can be included in your typical UPS collections. 

 As noted we have flagged this issue to engineering. 

Please ship this PO in accordance with its must-ship-by date. Should this PO be late for shipment please delay it on Partner Home. Please find the instructions on how to do so here: https://partners.wayfair.com/help/2/article/1581.

Thanks again for reaching out. This ticket is now marked as complete. If you need further support with this issue, please comment to re-open. 

Please let us know how we did; an email is on its way for you to rate your experience and the service received on this ticket. Your feedback is extremely important to us and will help us improve the service we provide to you, our partners.

Kind Regards,

The given origin or destination postal code is not in a format that conforms to the specified country's postal system


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Tag the Ticket

Tag ticket affected with “Related Tech issue: EUCC-2682”. 

If the ticket is now closed, reference  to check for any existing open tech issues before tagging.


2

Review Warehouse Address

Check for blank spaces in warehouse address and remove and save if there are any (Visual 2.1)

Visual 2.1:

3

Move Ticket Onshore

If this fails to resolve the issue send ticket onshore T1.




Other Common Errors

Based on the failure reason provided in the ticket, review the truesmallCommon Label Printing Errorshttps://infohub.corp.wayfair.com/x/ZoTcAQfor guidance on how to resolve the issue. 

If you identify your error on this SOP, but are unsure of how to follow the resolution path, reach out in your Slack channel (#euc2s-cannot-print-shipping-docs-vcn-5972403) to ask your POC, Ingrid Compton, for support. 

Check the truesmallBugs Reporthttps://infohub.corp.wayfair.com/x/X4wvN to confirm if the issue has already been reported. 

If the error has not been reported, see the Request Onshore Support tab for next steps. 



Print Label


This section explains how to print the label, packing slip and manifest from Partner Home once any technical issues have been resolved. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Refresh the PO

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.1)

Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. 

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. 

Select the check box beside the PO. 

Click More Actions > Refresh Orders. (Visual 1.2; Tip 1)

Tip 1:

This is necessary to finalise any changes made to the order before printing the label. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Generate the Shipping Docs

Select the check box beside the PO. 

Click Print Documents. (Visual 2.1)

This will open a new tab. 

Confirm the following: (Visual 2.2) 

  • All documents selected to print
  • Warehouse (Tip 2)
  • Pickup Date
  • Confirm Labels quantity (Tip 3)

Select Generate Documents. (Visual 2.2) 

Close the window. 

Tip 2:

This is only required for Suppliers who ship out of multiple warehouses. 


Tip 3:

If the Supplier advises a different amount of labels are required, select Edit Shipping Labels. (Visual 2.2)

Input the amount of labels required in Piece Count. 

Divide the Total Weight by the Piece Count to get the Unit Weight.

Select Done Editing. (Visual 2.3) 


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

3

Save the labels

The Status should now appear as Awaiting Pickup. (Visual 3.1)

A green pop-up will appear at the bottom of the screen. (Visual 3.2)

Select Download Documents to save the labels. (Tip 4)

These should be saved using the PO as the title for the file. 

Attach to your ticket for the Supplier's reference. (Visual 3.3)

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab to update the Supplier. 

Tip 4:

Orders shipping from the UK on the Freight Network will generate two separate shipping labels - one for the first mile carrier and one for the final mile carrier. 

Orders shipping from the EU on the Freight Network will only generate one box label. The Supplier will also need to generate a pallet label. This Help Center article can be shared with them to explain the process.

The FN_Register_Pallet template can be used in cases where you are providing the Supplier with a pallet label. 

More information on the Freight Network can be found here.

Visual 3.2:

Visual 3.3:



Additional Queries



Edit Label Quantity


This section outlines the steps to take when the Supplier is able to print their labels but they are requesting a different label quantity. 

This is a Supplier education issue as this is something they can self-service through Partner Home.

You must still resolve the issue for the Supplier and provide them with the amount of labels requested. You should also educate them on the correct process to follow going forward. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Locate the PO

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.1)

Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. 

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. (Visual 1.2)



Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Generate the Shipping Docs

Select the check box beside the PO. 

Click Print Documents. (Visual 2.1)

This will open a new tab. 

Confirm the following: (Visual 2.2) 

  • All documents selected to print
  • Warehouse (Tip 1)
  • Pickup Date

To change the amount of labels, select Edit Shipping Labels. (Visual 2.2)

Input the amount of labels required in Piece Count. 

Divide the Total Weight by the Piece Count to get the Unit Weight.

Select Done Editing. (Visual 2.3) 

Select Generate Documents. (Visual 2.2) 

Close the window. 

Tip 1:

This is only required for Suppliers who ship out of multiple warehouses. 


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

3

Save the labels

A green pop-up will appear at the bottom of the screen. (Visual 3.1)

Select Download Documents to save the labels. (Tip 2)

These should be saved using the PO as the title for the file. 

Attach to your ticket for the Supplier's reference. (Visual 3.2)

Use the additional_labels template to update the supplier of the next steps. 

Move to Step 2 of the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Tip 2:

Orders shipping from the UK on the Freight Network will generate two separate shipping labels - one for the first mile carrier and one for the final mile carrier. 

Orders shipping from the EU on the Freight Network will only generate one box label. The Supplier will also need to generate a pallet label. This Help Center article can be shared with them to explain the process.

The FN_Register_Pallet template can be used in cases where you are providing the Supplier with a pallet label. 

More information on the Freight Network can be found here.

Replacement Part Labels


This section outlines the steps to take when the Supplier requests labels for a replacement part. 

They are generally unable to print these labels due to two issues: 

  1. The part has not been confirmed on Partner Home
  2. The Supplier has not provided the weight of the part

This is a Supplier education issue as this is something they can self-service through Partner Home.

You must still resolve the issue for the Supplier and provide them with the requested. You should also educate them on the correct process to follow going forward. 

If the below steps do not resolve the issue and there is a tech issue involved, move to the Common Label Print Errors tab to troubleshoot this. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Locate the PO

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.1)

Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. 

Select the Replacement Parts tab.

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. (Visual 1.2)

If the Fulfilment Status is showing as New, the part has not been confirmed. Move to .

If the Fulfilment Status is showing as Processing, move to .

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Confirm the Part

Click onto the PO result to open the PO details. (Visual 2.1)

This will open a new window with the PO details. 

At the top of the screen, select Yes, I Accept to confirm the part. 

Move to to generate the labels for the Supplier.

In cases where the part was not confirmed, use the RPO_Labels_Not_Confirmed template when updating the Supplier. 

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Generate the Shipping Docs

Select Print Documents. (Visual 3.1)

This will open a new tab. 

Confirm the following: (Visual 3.2) 

  • All documents selected to print
  • Warehouse (Tip 1)
  • Pickup Date

Select Continue. 

Confirm the carton count and weight. (Tip 2)

Select Generate Documents. (Visual 3.2) 

Close the window. 

Tip 1:

This is only required for Suppliers who ship out of multiple warehouses. 


Tip 2:

If the Supplier has not advised on the weight, use an estimate and advise the Supplier of this using the RPO_Labels_No_Weight template.


Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

Visual 3.3:

4

Save the labels

A green pop-up will appear at the bottom of the screen. (Visual 4.1)

Select Download Documents to save the labels. (Tip 3)

These should be saved using the PO as the title for the file.

Review the labels to confirm if they reflect the correct parcel count and weight for the part. (Tip 4) 

Attach to your ticket for the Supplier's reference. (Visual 4.2)

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Tip 3:

Orders shipping from the UK on the Freight Network will generate two separate shipping labels - one for the first mile carrier and one for the final mile carrier. 

Orders shipping from the EU on the Freight Network will only generate one box label. The Supplier will also need to generate a pallet label. This Help Center article can be shared with them to explain the process.

The FN_Register_Pallet template can be used in cases where you are providing the Supplier with a pallet label. 

More information on the Freight Network can be found here.  


Tip 4:

A tech issue may cause the labels to be generated for the original PO, rather than the replacement part. 

For example if the original shipped in 6 boxes, and 6 labels are generated, even though you selected to only print one. 

If this occurs, move to the Request Onshore Support tab. 


Visual 4.1:

Visual 4.2:



Manifest Request


What is a manifest?

When signed, a shipping manifest confirms that a collection occurred on the respective date. This does not confirm shipment of each individual carton included in the manifest, which is confirmed by carrier scans on each respective carton.

The supplier should have the driver sign a shipping manifest for every collection they have and retain it for their records. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Downland the Manifest

If the manifest is not generating from Partner Home, you will need to provide the Supplier with a generic copy to complete. 

Open the generic Manifest Template in Google Docs. 

Select File > Download > Microsoft Word (.docx). (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Attach the File

Attach the downloaded file to the ticket. (Visual 2)

Visual 2:

3

Send the Template

Use the PO_Manifest_Cannot_Be_Generated template to update the supplier of the next steps. 


4

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to Step 2 of the Resolve Ticket tab. 




Commercial Invoice Query


A commercial invoice is a document used in international shipping. It gives information about the product being sent, including:

  • what the item is

  • the seller and buyer

  • the date and terms of the sale

  • the quantity and weight

  • the value of each individual item

  • the total value of the package

  • any insurance and shipping costs

Information on commercial invoices for Wayfair shipments can be found in this Help Centre article. 

You may receive a query from the Supplier requesting a commercial invoice is provided. 

Depending on the carrier the request is for, follow the steps outlined below: 



DPD


DPD do not require a physical copy of the commercial invoice. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Update the Ticket

Use the PO_DPD_Commercial_Invoice_Not_Required template to update the supplier of the next steps.

If the Supplier pushes back to advise the carrier is refusing to collect without a physical invoice, this is generally a driver education issue. 

Provide the Supplier with the DECLARATION for the driver to sign advising he has refused to collect. 

Advise the Supplier to enter a Carrier Pickup Delay ticket with the signed declaration and the Ship to Deliver team will resolve with the carrier. 

This Help Centre article advises the Supplier how to enter a Carrier Pickup Delay ticket. 



2

Move to Resolve Ticket 

Move to Step 2 of the Resolve Ticket tab. 




UPS


UPS do not require a physical copy of the commercial invoice. This should be automatically transmitted with the label. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm Correct Transmission

To confirm that this was transmitted correctly, download the label from the Transportation Event History tool.


2

Search the PO 
  1. Search using the PO number and click Submit. (Visual 2)
  2. Click on the hyperlink in the Label Print section. (Visual 2)

This will open a new tab where you can view the label. 

Visual 2:

3

Review the Label

Ensure the label states EDI - this confirms the commercial invoice was transmitted. (Visual 3.1)

If this is missing or something else is stated, follow the steps in the Print Label tab to refresh the PO and reprint the label with the correct information.

Visual 3.1:







Request Onshore Support


If you require support from the In House team, you can request onshore support via the ticket you are working.

The In House team will take over ownership of your ticket and remove this from your queue. 

Always update your current ticket with an internal comment to confirm the steps you have taken to try to resolve the issue before requesting support. 

You may also request specific language support from the In House team if the Supplier reopens a ticket with an update in German, Polish or Mandarin with questions requiring an in-depth response. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Add Manual Trigger

Navigate to the Templates drop down menu.

Select the Manual Trigger (POS AOPS) category. (Visual 1.1)

Choose the Onshore Support Required template. (Visual 1.2)

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Add Escalation Template

The template will automatically populate in the comment section. (Visual 2.1)

Template

Dropship Order Fulfillment Support Needed  

SOP Followed: [Provide details]  

Steps Taken: [Provide details]  

Any Other Comments: [Provide details]


Do not make any edits to the first sentence: Dropship Order Fulfillment Support Needed.

This is required to trigger the automatic escalation process. 

Remove [Provide details] from each line.


Visual 2.1:

3

Complete Template

Complete the template by filling in:

  • A link to the SOP followed.
  • Steps you took, if any.
  • Any additional comments (Tip 1)

Select Post Internally to ensure that the comment is not visible to suppliers. (Visual 3.1)

Once the comment is added, the automation will be triggered to escalate the ticket. This will move the ticket out of your queue and assign it to an In House agent. 

An external comment will be automatically added to the ticket, informing the Supplier of the next steps. 

Template

Hi Team, 

Thank you for reaching out regarding issues on {{Customer Request type raised on initial ticket}} topics. 

We are reviewing this and will provide an update as soon as possible.

This ticket will remain open to provide the next steps.

We appreciate your patience and understanding.  

Kind Regards,

You do not need to take any further action. (Tip 2)

Tip 1:

Provide as much context here as possible to support the In House team in reaching a quick resolution for the Supplier. 


Tip 2:

You must not mark your ticket as Done.

This ensures the ticket stays open for the In House team to action.  


Visual 3.1:



Resolve Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Update the supplier

Ensure the label is attached to the ticket. (Visual 1.1)

Use the relevant template to update the supplier of the action taken. 

Templates for each scenario can be found here. (Tip 1)

If you need to make any edits to your comment after this is added, click the pencil icon. (Visual 1.2) 

Tip 1:

Ensure you make any necessary edits to the templates so these are tailored to the Supplier's specific query. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Complete ticket

Select Change Status > Done. (Visual 2.1)

This will open a pop up window.

In the Ticket Outcome dropdown: (Tip 2)

  • If the ticket was manually entered by the Supplier, select Manually entered
  • If the ticket was generated by the system, select Automatically generated 

Click Save and Close. (Visual 2.2)

Tip 2:

If you are unsure how the ticket was entered, return to the Review Ticket tab for guidelines on how to identify this. 


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

1256187304.body:
Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) 

Last Updated:




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

Add text here.



Overview

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Change Ship Method on PO - Not Shipped. 

These tickets are entered by the Customer Service team and they should provide all of the information required for you to action the ticket using the templates outlined below: 

ScenarioTemplate

Request to change from Less than Truckload (LTL) to Small Parcel (SP)

Have you confirmed the girth (using the formula above): Y/N

Did the supplier confirm they can accommodate the change: Y/N

Name of contact at the supplier who confirmed this for you: 

Request to change from SP to LTL

The reason we are changing this to LTL: 

Have you confirmed the pickup hours?

Did the supplier confirm they can accommodate the change: Y/N

Name of contact at the supplier who confirmed this for you: 

These tickets may also be entered by the LOM (Late Order Monitoring) team in cases where the Supplier incorrectly shipped a Small Parcel item to a Pool Point. The LOM team will replace the PO and enter this ticket to request an upgrade to LTL. You will see the below update on these requests:

trueLOM Template

Hi Team,

Please assist with changing the ship via method to a XXX shipment for replacement PO XXX and, if appropriate, update the ShipVia at the item level. A replacement was issued due to the original PO XXX being registered to ship large parcel through the WDN, but the supplier shipped it via a small parcel carrier to a large parcel freight crossdock.

Thank you




Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool (Linked)

Use Case











Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

Ship Method

The Ship Method defines whether an item will ship as a Small Parcel (SP) or as LTL (Less than Truckload). 

Ship Speed

Ship Speed is how fast an item will be delivered. This is only available for Small Parcel items. Examples of ship speeds are Express, Overnight, 2nd Day Air.

SP

Small Parcel

LTL

Less than Truckload





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant for your investigation. 

You can see the current Customer Request Type and the PO Number. The template completed by the Customer Service team will appear in the Description. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Review the Description

Review the Description to determine the next steps:

  • If the change was approved by the Supplier, move to the Change Ship Confirmed With Supplier tab. 
  • If the request was entered by the LOM team (as identified by the template in the Before You Start tab), move to the Change Ship Confirmed With Supplier tab. 
  • If the template was not completed correctly with the information required to change the ship method, move to the Change Ship Information Needed tab.
  • If the request is related to Replacement Parts, move to the Change Ship for Replacement Parts tab. 
  • If the request is related to changing the ship speed, delivery address or other change that is not ship method, move to the Incorrectly Entered Ticket tab. 
How to Identify a Replacement Part

A replacement part is indicated by a PO number with '-R-' and a number following it. 

For example:

  • CS123456789-R-12345
  • CA123456789-R-12345

If the PO noted in the ticket is a replacement part, move to the Change Ship for Replacement Parts tab


How to Identify if a Query Relates to Ship Speed (not Ship Method)

If the Service agent incorrectly enters the ticket to request a change to Ship Speed, the ticket may include the following language: 

  • Cannot fulfil at speed
  • Overnight not supported
  • Cannot ship air

If the query relates to Ship Speed, move to the Incorrectly Entered Ticket tab





Change Ship Confirmed With Supplier


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check Current Order Status

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page.  (Visual 1.1)

Navigate to Customer Orders > Dropship Orders. (Visual 1.2)

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO to identify the Fulfilment Status. (Visual 1.3)

The following statuses are eligible for a Ship Method Change: (Tip 1) 

  • New
  • Processing
  • Staged
  • Awaiting Pickup 

To ensure there are no discrepancies between the PO status in Partner Home, and the status on Order View, cross check this on the order. 

From the Order tab, select the relevant PO to navigate to the Product tab.  

In the Destination section, review the Delivery Status. (Visual 1.4)

The terminology between the statuses won't match up exactly between the Product tab and Partner Home. Please see a comparison of these below: 

Partner HomeProduct Tab
New Order is at warehouse

How to Reindex a PO

Check product dimensions

From the Order tab, select the relevant PO to navigate to the Product tab, and view the Carton Dimensions. (Visual 2)

Calculate the girth of the parcel using the formula: 

Girth = 2x Height + 2x Width + Depth (also known as Length)

Requirements for changing from SP to LTL
  • Girth is between 135” and 165”
  • If any one dimension of the package is over 108”
  • Package weight is over 150 lbs
  • There are multiple cartons

We can also upgrade the ship method even if it is within SP dimensions in cases where the upgrade is requested due to fragility (eg. mirrors, ceramics, etc.).



Requirements for changing from LTL to SP
  • Girth is under 165”
  • Package weight is less than 150 lbs.

If the PO meets the requirements:

  • To change SP to LTL, move to .
  • To change LTL to SP, move to .
