1256177851.title: Change Ship Method or Carrier on PO Due to Item Count / Size (Supplier Entered) SOP (DSC2S) - VCN
1256177851.author: Former user (Deleted)
1256177851.body:
Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN 

Last Updated:  




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective



EU

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Change Ship Method or Carrier on PO due to Item Count/Size. 

You should only change the shipping method if the item is too big or too small for its current shipping method. 

If the item(s) fit within Small Parcel (SP) dimensions and an SP carrier is assigned, the shipping method should not be changed to Large Parcel (LP), even if requested.

Likewise, if the item(s) are assigned to ship with an LP carrier and fit within LP parameters, you should not change the shipping method to SP.

Although it's possible to change the Ship Speed for NA orders, this is not currently available to Suppliers in the EU. 




Overview

Add text here.




Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

SKU Quantity Threshold

A feature exists in Manage Product which allows a SKU-level value to be set at which the order will automatically change ship method from SP to LP.





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



NA



Supplier Requests Ship Method Change


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Carton Count(s) and Dimensions
  1. Click on the Order Tab on right hand side of ticket in ServiceHub 
  2. Click on the item hyperlink that the supplier is requesting the change for (this will redirect you automatically to the Product Tab)
  3. View the Carton Count(s) and Carton Dimensions section for the applicable carton counts and dims to calculate the girth. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Calculate Girth

Quick Calculation for Girth: (smallest dimension x2) + (next smallest dimension x2) + (largest dimension) = Total Girth (in inches) Large Parcel Ship Method is required if any of the following apply:

  • Total Girth exceeds 165'
  • Any single dimension exceeds 108'
  • Any single carton weight exceeds 150 lbs.
  • Any multi-carton shipment weight exceeds 250 lbs.
  • Any order volume exceeds 12 cartons in a single shipment


Changing the ship method from LTL or Large Parcel (LP) to Small Parcel (SP) does not impact the lead time of the product. Lead times will remain the same on the product, even if the ship method is changed to Small Parcel.





Small Parcel to Large Parcel


mediuminfofalse
  • When we are addressing customers for change ship methods being upgraded to LTL from SP, we should be addressing the billing email address and contact name, as opposed to the shipping contact information. This is to align with Order to Delivery communication standards when outreaching customers.
  • We are able to accommodate ship via changes from SP to LTL at the Suppliers request, even if the girth falls below 165 and the SU does not explicitly mention fragility. If a SU is requesting an update from SP to LTL, we should not deny these requests.
  • Outbound emails to customers should only be sent for full unit changes, such as upgrades or downgrades on full POs. They are not required for replacement parts, but are necessary if the shipping method for a full unit is changed.

  • When customers order multiple units of a product that normally ships SP, the combined weight or dimensions may exceed FedEx limits after carton consolidation or make LTL more cost-effective. Therefore we educate the SU on if this occurs again how to request SKU Qty thresholds are added to those specific SKUs. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open Order
  1. Open order in Order Wizard and Verify Brand on Order (i.e. Wayfair, Perigold, AllModern, etc.)
  2. Click to view in shipping view
  3. Locate the correct PO (Use Ctrl + F)

2

Update Shipping Info
  1. Click 'Update Shipment Info'
  2. Set 'Delivery Type' to Truck Freight
  3. Set 'Carrier' and 'Third Party Carrier' to Estes
  4. Click Update

4

Pull up PO in Partner Home
  1. Pull up PO in Partner Home
  2. Refresh PO in Dropship Orders

5Send Template

Once change is made, update the ticket with the SP to LP template to let the supplier know we’ve updated the order to ship LTL, reminding them to then go to the Partner Home Portal and register the order for shipment.


6Email the Customer

Once ticket is updated, contact the customer to inform them their order will be taking longer than initially estimated.

  1. Click 'Email Customer' in the Available Actions section of the ticket
    1. If in ServiceHub, go to Outbound Contact, and switch the Drop down to Email - it will redirect you to SupportHub to send the email
  2. Retitle the email: Information on your (Store Brand) (Order#)
  3. Email Appropriately Branded Template to Customer
    1. Review and scroll down to the Customer Email Branded Templates section on the Change Ship Method (Supplier Entered) tab. 

7IF: Supplier Reopens the Ticket

If SU reopens a ticket requesting more info or support on management of SKU quantity thresholds then refer SU Back to SRM (Supplier Relationship Manager) by providing the email address for them in the response. 

Locating SRM Log in to SU account on Partner Home. 

Navigate to Contact details can be found in PartnerHome >> Help & Support >> Contact Us (Visual 7.1)

Visual 7.1:



Large Parcel to Small Parcel



Standard Order


mediuminfofalse
  • Outbound emails to customers should only be sent for full unit changes, such as upgrades or downgrades on full POs. They are not required for replacement parts, but are necessary if the shipping method for a full unit is changed.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open Order
  1. Open order in Order Wizard
  2. Click to view in shipping view
  3. Locate the correct PO (Use Ctrl + F)

2

Search SUID in ShipVia Tool Dashboard

Search the SUID and SKU or SU Part ID in this ShipVia Tool Dashboard (Visual 2)

  • If the Product Flags “Overmax Flip - Supplier Credit Eligible” or “Overmax Flip - Hard Deny” in the Ship Via Update Column of the search results, this means that the item has been flagged for Overmax Charges with FedEx before.
    • Provide the supplier with the OMAX Terms Agreement - LP to SP template, which introduces conditions and requires the supplier to accept or deny the change.
    • If the supplier accepts, you should update the PO to ship small parcel.
    • If the supplier does not accept, the change should be denied and we should confirm that the PO will remain large parcel (LTL).

Using the ShipVia Tool Dashboard:
1. Navigate to the Supplier ShipVia Requests Tab
2. Enter the Parent (Top Level) SUID for the supplier
3. Enter the Wayfair SKU or Supplier Part Number
3a. Note: you can search multiple products at once as long as you separate them by commas
4. Refer to the Ship Class Decision Section, and view the Approval column


Visual 2:

4

Verify Ship Method

Confirm ground is listed in the SKU level as the ship method.

  • If the PO set to Inside Delivery/White Glove, Open the Finance View in ServiceHub, and locate the line item for the relevant PO/item and review the Shipping section under the Receipt Summary

  • If the Shipping section under the Receipt Summary reflects any charges, this means the customer has PAID to upgrade their delivery service, and the supplier is expected to fulfill under the assigned method.

    • Deny the ship method change using the Shipping White Glove template.

    • Otherwise, move on to Step 5. 


  • If a PO is set to White Glove but there are no shipping charges, this indicates that upgrade was offered to the customer for free and we are able to downgrade the delivery service.
  • If there are shipping charges, that means the items delivery method was not set to White Glove by default, and the customer paid to upgrade their services.



5Update Shipping Info
  1. Click 'Update Shipment Info'
  2. Set 'Delivery Type' to Ground
  3. Set 'Carrier' and 'Third Party Carrier’ to FedEx
  4. Click Update

6Pull up PO in Partner Home
  1. Pull up PO in Partner Home
  2. Refresh PO in Dropship Orders

7Send Template

Once change is made, update the ticket to let the supplier know we’ve updated the order to ship SP, reminding them to then go to the Partner Home Portal and register the order for shipment


8Email the Customer

Once ticket is updated, contact the customer to inform them their order will be shipped via FedEx and tracking will be shared once shipped. 

  1. Click 'Email Customer' in the Available Actions section of the ticket

    1. If in ServiceHub, go to Outbound Contact, and switch the Drop down to Email - it will redirect you to SupportHub to send the email.

  2. Retitle the email: Information on your (Store Brand) (Order#)

  3. Email Appropriately Branded Template to Customer

    1. Scroll down to the Customer Email Branded Templates section on the Change Ship Method tab




Consolidated Delivery


mediuminfofalse
  • Flipping a LP item to SP will change routing, shipping direct to customer ,and thus breaking the promise of consolidated delivery. Consolidated delivery orders can be identified via reviewing the banner on the order wizard page.
  • NOTE: These banners are not identifiable by color alone, please ensure you are searching for (CTRL+F) and verifying the wording Consolidated Delivery is on the banner.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the  Consolidated Delivery LP to SP Denied template. 

ServiceHub View:

Order Wizard View:





Carrier Pickup Delay LTL


Supplier has entered a ticket reporting an LTL carrier did not pick up.

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Update the ticket with the applicable template. 


2

Initiate a Ticket Move

Initiate a ticket move

  1. The move option is under the more dropdown menu

  1. New project = FMOP
  2. New Issue Type= Supplier Request 
  3. (If asked) New Status = Waiting for support

Click Move

Click Move when ready. The ticket becomes a FMOP-##### ticket, and you are done

Carrier Change SP


Supplier has entered a ticket requesting a different SP carrier.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open Manage Supplier
  1. Open Manage Supplier
  2. Search SUID

  3. Navigate to the Logistics Header

    1. Some suppliers logistics are maintained at the child level and you may need to navigate to the child warehouse being used for this PO
  4. Confirm the requested carrier is listed on the suppliers logistics under SP

2Open the Order
  1. Go to Shipping View
  2. CTRL F to find PO 

3Update Shipping Info
  1. Select Update Shipping Info
  2. Under the Delivery Type Column select “Ground”
  3. Change the Carrier to “FedEx” 
  4. Change the Third Party Carrier to “FedEx”
  5. Leave Delivery Agent Blank
  6. Select Update

4View PO in PartnerHome
  1. Pull up PO in Partner Home
  2. Refresh PO in Dropship Orders

5Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the XX template.




Changing A Ship Speed


Supplier has entered a ticket advising they cannot ship Express/Overnight/2nd Day Air.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open Order

Open the Order


2

Navigate to Shipping or Service Upgrades Wizard
  1. CTRL F to find PO 
  2. Select Box > Continue
  3. Change the ship speed to the next lowest shipping option
  4. Express > Expedited
  5. Expedited > Ground
  6. If the wizard attempts to charge the customer, select discount 100%
  7. Send confirmation to customer's current email address
  8. Confirm
  9. If it does not auto approve the change, navigate back to Shipping or Service Upgrades Wizard
  10. CTRL F to find PO 
  11. Select Confirm Ship Speed Change
  12. Select PO
  13. Select  We were able to confirm the ship speed change on this item.
  14. Select Confirmation Email
  15. Submit


If the Shipping or Service Upgrades Wizard fails, proceed to Step 3. 



3

IF: Shipping or Service Upgrades Wizard Fails

In the event that the Shipping or Service Upgrades Wizard fails, try the Change Delivery Method V2 workflow.

Please note if the PO or order in general is a financed order it will not allow you to run a ship via wizard; these examples must be added to the help channel for support or sent onshore. 

  1. Select the  Change Delivery Method V2 workflow wizard.
  2. Select the PO required (Visual 3.1)
  3. Click Continue.
  4. Select Ground (Visual 3.2)
  5. Shipping fees if owed back to the customer should be sent to the original payment method. (Visual 3.3)
  6. Click Continue.
  7. Send confirmation to customer's current email address. (Visual 3.4)
  8. Click Wrap - Do not Wrap contact.
  9. Log into suppliers account via Partner Home. 
  10. On the landing page, you will see todays tasks “Ship Speed Change”. (Visual 3.5)
  11. Select the notification & Order page opens
  12. Alert for “ship speed change” appears select the dropdown. (Visual 3.6)
  13. Ship speed change page opens - click Save update (ensure Yes is highlighted in purple on right). (Visual 3.7)
  14. Success will appear on screen. (Visual 3.8)
  15. Refresh order view and ground will appear.
  16. Update and close ticket. 


If the Change Delivery Method V2 fails, proceed to Step 4. 


Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

Visual 3.3:

Visual 3.4:

Visual 3.5:

Visual 3.6:

Visual 3.7:

Visual 3.8:

4

IF: Both Wizards Failed to Run
  1. Check the banner on the order in blue could be showing the financed order; Klarna, Bread, MyWay, etc. (Visual 4.1). This means that the ship via speed wizards will fail to run through to completion. 
  2. Update the PO ship class by clicking on the Product tab and select Edit Shipping/Destination Info (Visual 4.2).
  3. In the pop-up window,  set the delivery type to “Ground” also ensure that “Carrier” and “Third Party Carrier” are also set to Fedex once the delivery type is updated. Click Update. (Visual 4.3)
  4. Confirm that it is saved via ServiceHub. (Visual 4.4)
  5. Refresh the order in Partner Home as well as Order View. Once it is showing Ground, the action is completed for the SU. 
  6. Given that we have manually adjusted the shipvia we need to ensure the customer is refunded the amount of difference. 
  7. Please be aware with MyWay subscriptions we cannot in any event refund the customer any costs as MyWay is a subscription simply change the ship via and move to Step 5 updating the SU and complete the ticket. 
  8. On order tab select “Get Help with this Order” (Visual 4.5). A new tab will open. 
  9. Select “Problem with Refund”  (Visual 4.6)
  10. Fill in the Problem with a Refund form:
    1. Marketplace: Express to Ground will always be US 
    2. What is the reason for this request?: Problem with a refund
    3. What kind of refund issue is this?: Refund not received
    4. Is the refund showing as issued in our system?: No - refund not showing as issued
    5. Did the refund trigger but get stuck?: No - it didn’t trigger
    6. Was the wizard definitely run on the item in question?: Yes - I’ve confirmed it
    7. Where did the refund come from?: Other…
    8. Based on the validation steps you completed above, is this request still needed?: Yes
    9. Customer Type: - B2C
    10. What refund amount has customer received?: 0 (Note 1)
    11. What refund amount is the customer expecting?
      1. Locate the item in the orders list that is being downgraded its name specifically
      2. To identify the amount to be refunded to the customer, navigate to the Finance tab in ServiceHub.
      3. In the Shipping column, you will see the dollar amount to be refunded. This amount reflects the difference between Express shipping (which the customer originally paid for) and Standard shipping. (Visual 4.7) 
      4. Only the shipping fee for the specific item being downgraded should be refunded.
    12. Item Number: Item number from order (Visual 4.8)
    13. Store Credit Code (if applicable): Leave this field blank 
    14. Is your refund related to one of the following methods: Rewards, WFCC/WFMC:  No
    15. What payment method is the refund issue with?: Other...
    16. If follow-up is needed, how would the customer prefer to be contacted? Email 
    17. Is there anything extra we need to know?: Insert the following: “The supplier is unable to accommodate expedited shipping, so the item has been downgraded to ground. The wizard did not allow alternate shipping speeds, so I have had to process the downgrade manually using shipping view. Please assist the customer with the refund for the expedited shipping speed. Related to AOPS-XXXXX."
    18. Once the PAYFIN- ticket is raised, link it to your open AOPS ticket as per link ticket instructions. 
  11. Proceed to step 5


Note 1

This is due to us being unable to run the wizard we couldn't run it through where the refund is autosent back to the CT therefore we are raising the ticket. 


Visual 4.1:

Visual 4.2:

Visual 4.3:

Visual 4.4:

Visual 4.5:

Visual 4.6:

Visual 4.7:

Visual 4.8:


5Send Template

If this is a FedEx request to change from Express to Ground, respond to the supplier utilizing the Changing FedEx Ship Speed template. For all other requests, respond to the supplier utilizing the Changing a Ship Speed template.


IF: SU reopens ticket with list of warehouses

The supplier reopens the ticket with a list of warehouses, please escalate the ticket to Tier 1 in order to add the restrictions





Shipping White Glove


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Viewing White Glove

When in Shipping View, it will say White Glove under Delivery Type. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:





EU


INLINE



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant for your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type, PO Number, and to which ship class the Supplier is requesting a change. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:



Confirm if Change is Needed


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Select Relevant PO

From the Order tab, select the relevant PO to navigate to the Product tab, and view the Carton Dimensions. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Identify the Carrier

Identify which carrier is assigned to the PO in the Destination section. (Visual 2)

Visual 2:

3

Compare the Carton Dimensions

Compare the Carton Dimensions to the max weight and dimensions of the carrier assigned usingtruesmallMax Weights & Dims for EU Carriershttps://wayfaircorp.atlassian.net/wiki/x/KYLeSg

You will also be able to confirm if the assigned carrier is Small Parcel or Large Parcel from the article above. 



4

IF: Item is Outside Carrier's Max Weight or Dimensions

If the item is outside the carrier's max weight or dimensions, move to the relevant tab to complete the change. 

You should not upgrade the item if it is within the carrier's dimensions:

  • If the Supplier has not advised of any issues on the carrier's end, use the Unable_SP_to_LP or Unable_LP_to_SP templates to advise the Supplier that we will be unable to upgrade and move to the Resolve Ticket tab.
  • If the Supplier has advised that the upgrade is needed as there is an issue with the carrier collecting, update the ticket asking the Supplier to confirm the following:
    • The reason the carrier is refusing to collect the item
    • If the carton weight and dimensions match the information on site

5

IF: Dimensions are Correct, Carrier Refusing to Collect

If the dimensions are correct and the carrier is still refusing to collect, transfer the ticket to the Supplier Collections team to assist.

How to Transfer the Ticket

Use the Incorrect_CPUD template to advise the Supplier of the next steps. 

Add an internal comment to the ticket advising the Supplier Collection team of the issue and what assistance is needed. 

Select Transfer (Visual 5.1)

This will open a new window. 

Update the Current Project to EU Collection Performance (SC), and click Next. 

Select Collection Failure Ticket as the Customer Request Type, and click Next. (Visual 5.2)

You will not need to take any further action on the ticket once it has been transferred.


Visual 5.1:

Visual 5.2:



Check Logistics Matrix


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

View Transportation Logistics

From the Supplier tab, click on Transportation Logistics. (Visual 1.1)

Visual 1.1:

2

View Delivery Section

Based on the order's destination, view the section for delivery to the relevant country. 

The preferred SP carrier for this lane will be shown here. (Visual 2.1)

The preferred LP carrier for this lane will be shown here. (Visual 2.2)

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

IF: SU Requests to Change Ship Method for RP

If the Supplier wants to change the ship method for Replacement Parts, move to the Replacement Parts tab. 

How to Identify a Replacement Part

A replacement part is indicated by a PO number with '-R-' and a number following it. 

For example UK123456789-R-12345.



4

IF: SU Requests Change from SP to LP

If the Supplier wants to change from a Small Parcel Carrier to a Large Parcel Carrier, move to the Change from SP to LP tab. 


5

IF: SU Requests Change from LP to SP

If the Supplier wants to change from a Large Parcel Carrier to a Small Parcel Carrier, move to the Change from LP to SP tab.

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

This article explains how VCN agents working AOPS and POS tickets can escalate a ticket that requires support from the In-House team.



Before You Start


Post to Slack channel #global-c2s-vcn-tickethelp when:

  • Reporting suspected tech issues (include PO #, ticket #, and details).
  • Raising process uncertainties or questions not tied to an open ticket.
  • Providing SOP feedback or suggestions for improvement.
  • Requesting training or clarification.
  • Sharing knowledge or recurring patterns that can improve workflows.

Do NOT post to Slack channel:

  • Routine ticket resolution (e.g., PO deletions, cancellations, downgrades, address changes, damages, lead time escalations, reopening non-actionable tickets).
  • Any issue that can and should be sent directly to onshore T1 for resolution.

Note: If you cannot resolve a ticket at first touch, send it directly to onshore T1, but you can still share insights or patterns about the issue in the Slack channel to support knowledge sharing and process improvement.

Before moving to escalate the issue, ensure you have followed all the steps outlined in the relevant SOP:

purpletrueinfoAOPS SOPs (DSC2S) - VCNhttps://infohub.corp.wayfair.com/x/mIPmTg

If you are unable to resolve the Supplier's issue with the information provided in the SOP, move to the Escalate Ticket tab. 




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Escalate Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Determine If Escalation Is Required

Escalation is required when:

  1. T0 does not have the knowledge, permissions, tools, or scope to resolve the issue.
  2. The issue requires onshore intervention or internal review.
  3. Policy within dictates T1 handling.

If the ticket can be actioned by T0 → Resolve as normal.

If the ticket cannot be actioned → Proceed to Step 2.


2

Provide Supplier facing Update 

Add a Supplier facing comment to the ticket (not internal comment as historically used) using the Escalation to T1 & SU Update template. (Visual 2.1, Visual 2.3)

  • The only permitted modifications to the template above are:
    • Adding the name of the agent providing the update, and
    • Addressing the supplier in alignment with the Response Structure outlined in the . (Visual 2.2)


The following section must remain unchanged:

“We have received your request and are currently reviewing this with the relevant internal team.

We will provide an update within one business day at the latest, or sooner if we have additional information or a resolution.”




Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

3

Ticket Movement 

  1. After posting comment externally Supplier facing, the automation will be triggered to escalate the ticket. This will move the ticket out of your queue and assign it to an In House agent. 
  2. The Component “Agent-Onshore-Escalation” will be added to the ticket. 
  3. The Supplier will have received your update stating that they will shortly receive support. 
  4. You do not need to take any further action. (Tip 1)


Tip 1

You must not mark your ticket as Done.

This ensures the ticket stays open for the In House team to action. 


Post to Slack channel #global-c2s-vcn-tickethelp when:

  • Reporting suspected tech issues (include PO #, ticket #, and details).
  • Raising process uncertainties or questions not tied to an open ticket.
  • Providing SOP feedback or suggestions for improvement.
  • Requesting training or clarification.
  • Sharing knowledge or recurring patterns that can improve workflows.



4

Ticket is “De escalated”

If a ticket has been:

  • Escalated to T1/T2
  • Then de-escalated back to T0

Before escalating again, T1/T2 must remove the component “Agent-Onshore-Escalation” (Visual 4). Failure to remove this component will prevent the automation from triggering correctly.

After removal, T0 must proceed with the standard SU template process (Step 2).

Any issues with reescalation should be flagged in the help Channel should they arise. 

Visual 4:




Review Automation Process

The first sentence, Dropship Order Fulfillment Support Needed, triggers an automation that changes the Customer Request Type to Dropship Order Fulfillment Support.

Components are added to the ticket to identify which Customer Request Type which was raised by the Supplier, before the move for In House Support. 

These Customer Request Types are only routed to onshore agents, allowing a clear escalation path from T0 (Offshore - Initial handling) to T1 (Onshore Support).

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

This article outlines how and when you might transfer a ticket in SupportHub to another team to resolve the Supplier's issue. 



Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool

Use Case

Ticket template responses for the Account Ops (AOPS) project.



Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

RMTResolutions Management Team
B2BBusiness to Business
P+Platinum+
Tried & True

Tried & True is our strategic review capture program in which suppliers provide free products to customers in exchange for high-quality reviews (at least 200 characters and 1 image). The supplier is responsible for the cost of the item, shipping, and any replacements related to damages. If the supplier enters a ticket with questions relating to ‘Review for Free Samples’ program.

Customers are allowed 30 days to submit a review or be removed from the program






#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow


mediumfalse

Unless designated with greenlocationNA Specific or magentalocationEU Specific, process should be presumed as being globalized.



What if I Received an Incorrect Transfer?


Ticket transfers are set up by individuals from departments that may not know our processes. If you receive a transfer that you cannot handle, please follow this guide.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Consult Leadership

Reach out to leadership for approval.

  1. Note: This is important as we also want to give feedback to the teams sending tickets to us.

2

Add an Internal Comment

Leave an internal comment with notes on what steps need to be taken and what/why AOPs can and cannot assist with. AO's should utilize the following template, and fill it out in it's entirety for every transfer to another department: 

Reason for Transfer:

Actions Taken: (N/A if not applicable)

Action Needed:



3

Transfer the TicketTransfer the ticket following the correct process above.



What if I Received a Transfer Ticket or Believe a Ticket Should be Transferred?


As we are finding multiple transfers to be a particular pain point for suppliers, we want to limit touch points. Please follow the provided instructions to help contribute towards bettering the supplier experience.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the TicketExpand & read the entire order ticket and review relevant details.

2

Address all QuestionsAddress all questions that you are able to answer.

3

Close the TicketIf this addresses all questions, then you are okay to close the ticket. If not, please ensure that you are confirming that the department that you want to transfer the ticket to is the correct department. Please reach out to any relevant slack channels or the AOPS leadership team to verify the validity of this transfer. 



What if I received a ticket re: a Credit Hold?


mediuminfo

A credit hold occurs when a supplier refuses to ship orders because they have not received payment or refuse to ship replacements because they are replacements. 

Wayfair's account suspended for MAP violations means Wayfair has priced something below the minimum advertised price is not a credit hold and should not be transferred these should be escalated to Tier II 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Add Template and TransferApply template & transfer ticket.

2

Flag to Leadership

Flag to leadership and request that they escalate the issue to Tier 2.

It is critical that we proceed with this action to ensure that this type of situation is gets to our tier 2 team as quickly as possible.





Supplier Service Desk Transfers



DESK Transfers


Ticket transfers for tail suppliers to SSD are a new process that is being implemented. The steps outlined are strictly for transfers to SSD as each team has their own project on ServiceHub and each project has its own requirements for what information is included in the ticket.

Ticket transfers 100% enabled

Please connect with leadership to review before transferring to any of the teams not detailed below.


Greenwhite2GreenIssues that SHOULD be Transferred to DESK
  • Carrier logistics change (for permanent account changes)
  • Pricing issue/change (for SKU and for individual POs entered by tail suppliers under the Problem with an Order ticket)
    • Note on Pricing: Please make sure you are still labeling these tickets with the PricingChanged or NoPricingChange labels!
  • FedEx carrier escalations only when the supplier has contacted their local FedEx rep, the volume not picked up is all for Wayfair. Suppliers first step in all requests for pick up assistance from FedEx should be to contact their local FedEx rep
  • Review for Free Samples (Tried & True for Customers) See Key Definitions for more information regarding this program. 


maroonwhite2maroonIssues that SHOULD NOT be Transferred to DESK
  • Ship Speed Exclusions


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Add Template

Update the ticket with

    1. If there are multiple issues on the ticket, please handle what you can first, modify the ticket template to reflect what you were able to handle, and then transfer the ticket to the desk.


Please do not transfer tickets where the supplier is managed by the Asian Supplier Service Desk to the Supplier Service Desk.



2

Initiate Ticket Move

Initiate a ticket move

    1. The move option is under the more dropdown menu


Choosing Move will load a new page



3Input the Settings

Enter the following settings

    1. Note: other fields besides those above do not need to be edited. Leave as-is
    1. New Project= DESK 
    2. New Issue Type= Supplier Request 
    3. (if asked) New Status = Waiting for support
    4. Customer Request Type= Supplier Email Request 



4Click Move

Click Move when ready. The ticket becomes a DESK-##### ticket, and you are done




ASIADESK Ticket Transfers


Ticket transfers for tail suppliers to ASIADESK are a new process that is being implemented. The steps outlined are strictly for transfers to ASIADESK as each team has their own project on ServiceHub and each project has its own requirements for what information is included in the ticket.


Greenwhite2GreenIssues that SHOULD be Transferred to ASIADESK
  • Carrier logistics change (for permanent account changes)
  • Pricing issue/change (for SKU and for individual POs entered by tail suppliers under the Problem with an Order ticket)
    • Note on Pricing: Please make sure you are still labeling these tickets with the PricingChanged or NoPricingChange labels!
  • Restrict item from shipping to selected zip codes due to local regulations


maroonwhite2maroonIssues that SHOULD NOT be Transferred to ASIADESK



#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Supplier Tab

Check the Supplier Tab on the ticket to ensure that the supplier is ASIADESK owned.


2

Add Template

Update the ticket with ASIADESK Transfer template

  1. If there are multiple issues on the ticket, please handle what you can first, modify the ticket template to reflect what you were able to handle, and then transfer the ticket to the desk.

3

Initiate Ticket Move

Initiate a ticket Move

  1. The move option is under the more dropdown menu
  2.  Choosing Move will load a new page

4

Input the Settings

Enter the following settings:

    1. Note: other fields besides those above do not need to be edited. Leave as-is
    1. New Project= ASIADESK 
    2. New Issue Type= Supplier Request 
    3. (if asked) New Status = Waiting for support
    4. Customer Request Type= Supplier Email Request 

5Click Move

Click Move when ready. The ticket becomes a ASIADESK-##### ticket, and you are done






Interdepartment Transfers



Collections Support (SC)


INLINE


This section outlines the process for actioning tickets where the Supplier has flagged a Carrier Pickup Delay (CPUD), outside the correct Collection Failure pathway. 

Suppliers may report this issue via a different ticket type or email, or another team may transfer the issue to C2S. Regardless of how it's reported, you must action the request to ensure the Supplier's issue is resolved. 

This article explains how to:

  • Identify if there has been a genuine CPUD
  • Check if this has already been correctly reported 
  • Gather the relevant information necessary for the Collections team to investigate
  • Transfer the ticket to SC to assist the Supplier 



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type, as highlighted below. This type of request may be entered as several different Customer Request Types.

It is important to take note of the Customer Request Type as it will determine how you raise a ticket to the Collections Support team in later steps. 



2

Review the Description

The Description will also contain important information, including the carrier and impacted POs. (Visual 2)

Visual 2:



Confirm if CPUD is Valid


This section explains how to determine if the Supplier is reporting a genuine Collection Failure. It is vital to determine this before passing the case to the Collections team. 

To confirm the validity of the request, you must check the following: 

  • The PO is unshipped and labels have been generated
  • An existing SC ticket is not already open for the issue
  • A collection has been scheduled



Check Current Order Status


This section outlines how to confirm the current status of the order to identify if what action is required to help resolve the Supplier's issue. 

This section also explains how to identify if the label has been printed. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Log into Partner Home

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Locate relevant PO

Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. 

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. (Visual 2)



Visual 2:

3

Confirm order status

If the Status is New or Processing, move to . (Visual 3)

If the status shows Awaiting Pickup, move to . 

If the status shows In Transit, move to . 

If the status shows Cancellation Requested/Cancelled/Delivered, move to . 






Visual 3:

4

New / Processing

A New status indicates that the order has not been acknowledged by the Supplier.

Once the order has been acknowledged by the Supplier, the status will change to Processing. 

Processing indicates that the Supplier has seen the order, all details are correct and they can process this be the Original Ship By Date. 

For both of these statuses, no label has been generated at this point. 

Review the Description to confirm if the Supplier is reporting the following and refer to the relevant SOP: 

  • Label Print Issues
  • Request to Change Ship Method

If this is not the case, advise the Supplier to print the label and schedule a collection following the specific process for their assigned carrier. Help Centre articles outlining each process can be found here. 

You should also educate the Supplier on the correct process for reporting CPUD in future. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.


5

Awaiting Pickup

This indicates the label has been printed and no collection has taken place.

Move to the Check for Existing Ticket tab. 





6

In Transit

This indicates the collection may have since taken place. (See Tip 1)

To confirm the order was shipped correctly, check the tracking for scans.

Click onto the PO result to open the PO details. (See Visual 6.2)

Confirm which carrier was used and the tracking number. (See Visual 6.3) 

Based on this, follow the Validating Tracking for Orders guide to confirm if there are scans on the tracking. 

Tracking has Valid Scans

Advise the Supplier that the collection appears to have taken place and to reach out if further assistance needed. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 


Tracking has No Scans

Update the ticket to confirm with the Supplier if the order has truly shipped. 

If the Supplier confirms they have shipped the order, follow the steps outlined in Tracking has Valid Scans. 

If the Supplier states this is still at the warehouse, and the Carrier is not UPS, move to the Check for Existing Ticket tab. 

If the Carrier is UPS and the labels were printed <48 hours ago, move to the Check for Existing Ticket tab. (See Tip 2)

If the Carrier is UPS and the labels were printed >48 hours ago, follow the steps outlined in the UPS Exception tab. 

UPS Exception

Unmark the order as shipped:

From the Product tab, select Edit Shipping/Destination Info. (See Visual 6.4)

This will open a new tab with the shipping details. 

Change the Status to Pending Ship Confirmation. This will remove the ship date. 

Click remove beside the tracking number. 

Select Update. (See Visual 6.5)

Follow the steps outlined in the Print Label tab to generate new labels. 

Advise the Supplier that order has been marked as processing and new labels have been provided. Advise them to avoid prematurely sending ASNs to prevent the order being shipped in error. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 



Tip 1:

The order may have been marked shipped in error if the Supplier provided the Advanced Shipping Notification (ASN) too soon. 

ASNs are visible from the Order Trail. (See Visual 6.1)


Tip 2:

UPS labels expire after 72 hours. If the collection has not taken place within 48 hours, you should generate new labels to ensure these are valid when the Carrier arrives. 


Visual 6.1:

Visual 6.2:

Visual 6.3:

Visual 6.4:

Visual 6.5:

7

Cancellation Requested / Cancelled / Delivered

This indicates the collection is no longer needed. 

Advise the Supplier that no collection is needed as the PO has been cancelled/delivered. 

Ensure you also educate the Supplier on the correct process for reporting CPUD in future. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 





Check for Existing Ticket


This section outlines how to check in Waystation if the Supplier's issue has already been reported to the Collections Support team on a different ticket.  


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open Waystation

Navigate to Waystation. 
Filter to the relevant Supplier using the search bar on the top right. (See Visual 1.1)

Visual 1.1:

2

Search for tickets

Select the Tickets tab. 

Use the JIRA Projects filter to select EU Collection Performance. 

Select the Ticket # hyperlink to open any recent/open tickets in SupportHub. (See Visual 2.1)

Review the ticket to confirm if it relates to the same carrier and is still being worked on by the team. 

If the issue is being investigated, use the Ongoing_CPUD template to advise the Supplier of the next steps and move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

If the issue has not been reported, move to the Confirm Collection Scheduled tab.

Confirm Collection Scheduled


This section outlines how to identify if a collection has been scheduled with the carrier. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm collection booking method

Navigate to the EU Logistics Ops: Supplier Collections Set-Ups sheet. 

In the Booking Collections tab, find the relevant carrier in Column A. (Visual 1.1)

Review the details in Column B to identify how collections are scheduled for that carrier. (See Tip 1)

If the collection is booked by label print, move to . 

If the collection is booked by Supplier request, move to . 

If the PO is shipping with DHL Freight Network, move to . 


Tip 1:

Column H also provides more detail on how the collection is scheduled for each carrier. 


Visual 1.1:

2

Collection is booked by label print

Review the Order Trail to confirm when the label was printed. (See Visual 2.1)

Convert the time to GMT using Google/WorldTimeBuddy.

Check Column I and J in the EU Logistics Ops: Supplier Collections Set-Ups sheet to confirm if the label was printed before the cut off time and when the collection would have been scheduled for. 

If the label was printed before the cut-off time and the collection did not take place on the same/next day as expected, move to the Gather Information tab. 

If the label was printed after the cut-off time, move to the relevant carrier tab in the EU Logistics Ops: Supplier Collections Set-Ups sheet.

Use CTRL + F to search for the SUID. (See Visual 2.2)

Check the Suppliers Collection Frequency in Column I:

  • If the Supplier is on daily collections, move to the Gather Information tab.
  • If the Supplier is on Ad-hoc collections, or is not showing on the sheet, use the CPUD_After_Cut-Off template to advise them of the next steps. Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 



Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Collection is booked by request

Move to the relevant carrier tab in the EU Logistics Ops: Supplier Collections Set-Ups sheet.

Use CTRL + F to search for the SUID. (See Visual 3.1)

Check the Suppliers Collection Frequency in Column I:

  • If the Supplier is on daily collections, move to the Gather Information tab.
  • If the Supplier is on Ad-hoc collections, or is not showing on the sheet, use the CPUD_Booking_Email template to request a copy of the booking email. This is required before transferring the issue to the SC project. 

Once the email screenshot is received, move to the Gather Information tab.

If the Supplier does not provide the screenshot within 12 hours, close the ticket advising them of the correct ticket entry path, advising to attach the email screenshot to it when entering. 

Visual 3.1:

4

DHL Freight Network is the carrier

DHL Freight Network have a 48 hour service level to collect orders. 

If 48 hours have not passed since the date of the reported CPUD, pushback on the Supplier to advise the collection has been scheduled for today and advise of the correct CPUD process if the collection fails. Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

If 48 hours have passed, navigate to the Supplier tab. 

Confirm the warehouse postcode matches any warehouse postcode included in the ticket description. (See Visual 4.1)

Navigate to Power BI.

Log in using the email address euoutreachc2e@wayfair.com.

Reach out in the #legacy-tool-requests Slack channel to request the login code from the Onshore mailbox. 

Once logged in, ensure Wayfair_InDepth_Dashboard is written on the top left of the dashboard. (See Visual 4.2)

Hover the mouse over the ‘Wayfair’ logo and 3 dots will appear. 

Click on the 3 dots and select Go to report. (See Visual 4.3)

Select Extract 2 to view all collections that have taken place and upcoming collections for the following day. (See Visual 4.4)

Select anywhere on the report to bring up the filter pane on the right.

Scroll to the Supplier Name filter and type the filter as per the Supplier's name in the Supplier tab. (See Visual 4.5)

Check the Requested Pickup Date column to confirm if the date the Supplier claims the collection failed appears there. 

  • If the Supplier is not showing for that date, the pallet was likely registered incorrectly.

Ensure that the PO's Ready for Pickup date matches the pallet registration date. (See Tip 2)

Check if the pallet was registered on Order Management:

  • From Dropship Orders, select the Pallet Shipments tab.
  • Any pallets that have already been registered will appear here. 
  • Filter using the PO number, collection date or destination. (See Visual 4.7)

If the pallet was registered and the collection did not take place, move to the Gather Information tab.

If there was no pallet registration, use the FN_Register_Pallet template to guide the Supplier on how to do this and move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Tip 2:

The Ready for Pickup date can be confirmed in Dropship Orders by clicking onto the PO. (See Visual 4.6)

Gather Information


This section outlines how to gather the necessary information for the Collections Support team to investigate the collection failure with the carrier. 

The following template must be filled out before the ticket is passed over: 

  • SUID (of child warehouse affected):
  • SU Name: 
  • Affected Warehouse Address:
  • Issue with Carrier: (missed or partial collection)
  • Was a collection arranged? (based on carrier)
  • Date of collection failure 
  • Is the WH closed for lunch?
  • Number of Parcels affected: 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Begin completing template

Gather the information you can before contacting the Supplier: 

  • SUID (of child warehouse affected): 
    • If the Supplier only has one location, confirm this in Manage Supplier under the Warehouse section.
    • If there are multiple locations, confirm the affected warehouse with the Supplier. (Visual 1.1)
  • SU Name: Stated in the Supplier section of Service Hub. (Visual 1.2)
  • Affected Warehouse Address: 
    • If the Supplier only has one location, confirm this in Manage Supplier under the Warehouse section.
    • If there are multiple locations, confirm the affected warehouse with the Supplier. 
  • Was a collection arranged? If you identified in the Confirm Collection Scheduled tab that the collection was booked via label print or a booking email, confirm this was arranged. (Tip 1) 
  • Date of collection failure:  Confirm based on label cut-off times when the collection should have occurred, or if stated in the booking email. 

Tip 1:

Be mindful of cut-off times for label prints to ensure this is a genuine collection failure.

Finance Ops (FINSAP)


Ticket transfers for finance issues are being implemented. The steps outlined are strictly for transfers to FINSAP as each team has their own project on ServiceHub and each project has its own requirements for what information is included in the ticket.


Greenwhite2GreenIssues that SHOULD be Transferred to FINSAP
  • Supplier has questions regarding payment 
    • Update Bank information
    • ACH Funds not received
    • Credit Card declines
    • General payment-related request
    • Carrier cost/Charge Reimbursement
  • Holding orders due to invoicing (not pricing issues)
    • Invoices not paid
    • Unpaid CG Orders
    • Credit Disputes
  • Supplier reports that they are unable to enter a finance ticket
  • Supplier reaches out regarding a Return status email
  • Disputing Returns
  • Received Wrong Return (see Received a Return for Another Supplier section as this requires action/direction for AOPs to resolve)


maroonwhite2maroonIssues that SHOULD NOT be Transferred to FINSAP

  • Tickets reporting mis-shipments where the end customer has NOT contacted Wayfair

  • Tickets where the supplier is requesting we refund the customer
    • FINSAP is unable to see anything regarding customer finance


Please also note that Finance Ops will also be transferring tickets to us: 

  • Reroute PO to ship from another warehouse
  • Add tracking to an order
  • Update an alert
  • Item is Out of Stock or Discontinued
  • Requesting return tracking
  • Questions regarding partial cancellations
  • Air/Express shipping issues on a PO basis
  • Not receiving email notifications
  • Inform Wayfair of warehouse closures
  • NOMs system issues
  • General system issues
  • Split a PO

Supplier Claims (previously Credit Recovery)


maroonwhite2maroonCommon Dispute Themes
  • Holding replacements pending a deduction
  • Supplier requests to “not seek credit”
  • Disputing an aged out credit
  • Disputing a self-approved (supplier) credit
  • Disputing a deduction that has already been deemed valid by Supplier Claims on a different dispute ticket


Even in the case of there being an Open Credit Incident, all deduction disputes MUST be submitted through Partner Home. This requires a redirect to the Deductions and Remittance Tools. As this is the case, transfers to CR often result in a negative experience and incorrect expectations for suppliers. All direct ticket transfers to CR project should be reviewed by Tier 1 and Tier 2 in conjunction, as this should largely be avoided.

GSCI


Please identify the issue & connection type prior to transferring the ticket to ensure it is routed correctly. 


Issues that SHOULD be Transferred to...

GSCI

GSCIOPS (Operations)

GSCIEXE (Execute)

GSCIADO (Adoption)

  • Suppliers reporting problems with their inventory feed
  • Orders placed against -1 or 0 inventory
    • Excluding BATC overrides
  • Set up Requests
  • Supplier reporting issues with their EDI feed
  • Castlegate EDI document failures on shipped orders 
  • Warehouse additions & updates 
  • Questions on integration set up 
  • MTO Catalog/Product set up
  • Out of compliance (OOC) alerts 
  • Item deactivation or reactivation 
  • Global stock status on site (as long as supplier is not on EST (escalated status-visible in Manage Supplier)
    • If supplier is on EST, we will not transfer, we will connect the supplier with the Supplier Service Desk instead 
  • EDI connection outages 
  • EDI document failures 
  • API errors & connection failures 
  • Adding/Updating MTO Settings on a SKU or entire Product Catalog
    • This includes suppliers seeking their products to have some/all of the following attributes:
      • Non-cancellable
      • Non-returnable
      • Always in Stock
      • Custom or Personalization
      • Merchandised as Made to Order
  • Warehouse updates 
  • Warehouse additions 
  • Supplier wants to set up automation with Wayfair

Complete Pre-Transfer Investigation

Before transferring a warehouse-related ticket (Warehouse addition, Warehouse update,Warehouse inactivation/deactivation) to GSCIEXE, complete the following checks:

    • Gray: Supplier does not have access to the Warehouse DIY tool (Visual 1.4)

If Supplier HAS DIY Access (Purple):

  • Send the supplier the  template.
  • Which advises them to complete the warehouse update/addition independently
  • Close the ticket no transfer required
  • Should the SU reopen the ticket stating they are encountering issues then proceed to transfer the ticket to GSCIEXE after confirming issues SU is encountering before transfer. 

If Supplier DOES NOT HAVE DIY Access (Gray):

  • Proceed with the existing workflow update ticket with the template. 
  • Transfer the ticket to GSCIEXE (Execute) so that the following (Warehouse addition, Warehouse update,Warehouse inactivation/deactivation) are actioned by the GSCIEXE team.

Actions

Visuals

Review Ticket

Review the ticket in full to determine what support the Supplier requires. 

The CGIM project handles inventory-related requests.

The CGIB project handles requests relating to Inbound Castlegate orders (SPOs).

The CGOB project handles queries relating to customer orders.

If you can resolve the issue for the Supplier, ensure you are still educating them on the correct ticket to enter in the future: 

"If you have a similar issue in the future, please enter a Castlegate Fulfillment ticket. This will notify our Castlegate team directly. Please see this article: https://partners.wayfair.com/help/2/article/997."

Ticket Transfers for Non-Supplier Facing Teams


mediuminfo

Supplier-facing teams in GPS receive a wide array of training featuring empathy, answer first communication, and 1:1 coaching whenever Execute/Embed identify a training opportunity as a result of a deep dive into a low SSAT score. 

Many of our carrier facing teams do not receive this training, as there is no pathway for suppliers to enter tickets. However, these teams occasionally get these tickets as a result of transfers. 

To improve the supplier experience, the tier 2 team will own the supplier interaction and work with the appropriate carrier facing team via slack/internal comments. 



Do Not Transfer List


mediuminfo

Non Supplier Facing Teams:

  • 4 Walls Support
  • Carrier Claims (LP Only - SP go to FINSAP)
  • Final Mile
  • Late Order Monitoring
  • Middle Mile
  • Order Processing
  • Order Support
  • Supplier Transfer Program (STOps)



Ticket Transfer Process


#

Steps

Actions

1

Review the Do Not Transfer list

Before transferring a ticket, GPS associates review the Do Not Transfer list, either via the previous tab or within .


2

Determine if team can work the ticket

Update The AOPS ticket with the Non SU Facing Team Request Escalated template.


Visual 2:

3

Review for erroneous transfer

If a ticket is erroneously transferred, the manager of the carrier facing team will own the ticket and provide feedback to the GPS associate who incorrectly transferred the ticket.

2

IF: Order is Bannered for RMT


IF: Open Ticket

If there is an open ticket, update it with the relevant information.


IF: No Open Ticket

If there is no open ticket, enter a ticket through the banner.



3

Review the order

Review the order to confirm if PO has been marked as shipped  

  • Select the PO Number to navigate to the Product Tab of ServiceHub and verify the status

4

Confirm that a cancellation was requested

Confirm that a cancellation was requested by the customer in the Order Trail

  • If the alert was denied, process the cancellation using the Bulk Cancellation tool
  • If the PO is marked shipped, run WIMS as an incomplete shipment and refund the customer.  We will notify the supplier that the PO cannot be cancelled, but does not require fulfillment
  • If customer has requested a reversal of the cancellation (as noted in the Order Trail), redrop the order to create new PO

5

Send Template

Send the appropriate to the supplier and close the ticket out under a Done status. The template utilized will vary based on action taken.

  • $50,000 or higher in spending for a rolling six months qualifies for a dedicated Platinum+ Account manager (P+ Account Executive)

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Overview

General Lead Time Expectations

  • NA Wayfair’s lead time expectation is that suppliers ship small parcels in 1 business day (24h) and ship large parcels in 2 business days (48h) for all non-Made to Order (MTO) products. 
  • EU Wayfair’s lead time expectation is that suppliers ship small parcels in 1 business day (24h) and ship large parcels in 1 business day (24h) for all non-Made to Order (MTO) products.


Definitions for different lead time words

bluelinkLead Time Requests - Additional Infohttps://docs.google.com/document/d/1sz6J4x7fcdGRBemNNzUj9oa97G6JDS8h9Pc3M7c8PdI/edit?usp=sharing


SLAs and Escalations

The average SLA for Global FS&R Tickets is 5 business days. To shorten resolution time, please make sure all relevant information is provided each time you submit.

If the ticket remains unactioned after 5 business days, please notify in Slack help channel, and it will be raised at the Thursday FSR meeting.



Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool (Linked)

Use Case

The templates applicable to this workflow can be found within the Lead Times tab.
Global FS&R Toolkit
Global FS&R SupportHub Ticket Portal Ticket entry path for FS&R



Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

SUSupplier
LTSSLead Time Self Service; The lead time self service (LTSS) tool gives suppliers the ability to update their own lead times through Partner Home
🛑 icon (can be found within the SOP)

STOP, this is the end of necessary steps

HSD

Head Supplier Desk

FS&R

Fulfillment, Speed and Reliability team

APS/WGS HSD

Asia Head Supplier Desk

SSOA

Suppliers Shipping on their Own Account

Lead Time

The amount of time in 24 hour increments in which a supplier can ship an order. They are applied at bulk levels or product levels

Lead Time Pads (LT Pads)

Additional lead time applied to a day of week or specific date to accommodate variability in fulfillment capabilities.

Cut-Off Time

The time of day a supplier will process orders until.

Order Caps

Maximum Wayfair order volume that a supplier can handle on a given day.

Bulk Level Lead Time

Wayfair managed lead times which are set at a catalog level for SP and LP items. Bulk lead times are recommended for any supplier who does not have MTO items in their catalog.

Product Level Lead Time

Product-level lead time method is mainly used for Made-to-Order (MTO) suppliers and suppliers with a mix of stocked and Made-to-Order (MTO) and Made-to-Stock (MTS) products.

Made To Order (MTO)

Made to order items are items offered that provide the customer with a level of customization

Speed Badging

Speed Badging allows dropship suppliers with strong delivery performance to have their 1-day, 2-day. 3-day, and "Fast Delivery" orders badged with delivery guarantees on the storefront. 

Cushions

An additional customer facing lead time applied by Network Management to compensate for variability in order to delivery performance.





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Change Lead Time


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead Time Settings

Check Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead Time Settings to determine if supplier has Bulk Lead Time settings or Product Lead Time settings

  1. Open Global FS&R Tool Kit > Fulfillment Metrics

    1. Enter the correct Region/GEO and SUID (Parent Supplier) (Visual 1.1)

  2. Step 2: Navigate to the “LT Method” Tab to determine if the supplier has Bulk LT settings or Product Level settings (MTO) and the ship class by parcel type 

    1. Ship Class refers to settings for Small Parcel (SP) vs. Large Parcel (LP)

    2. This report will be split based on Supplier Warehouse

    3. Bulk LT: LT Method will state “Bulk LT” - Self Service Tab will state “No” (Visual 1.2)

    4. Product Level: Method will state “Product LT” - Self Service Tab will state “Yes” (Visual 1.3)

  3. Step 3: Navigate to the “Cutoff Time” tab to determine the suppliers warehouse cutoff times and the “LT Pads” tab to determine if the supplier currently has lead time pads (Visual 1.4, Tip 2)


Tip 1

Global FS&R Toolkit - Supplier Lead Time Settings Guide

1. Open the Global FS&R Toolkit

2. Select 'Store' dropdown

3. Change 'Store' to 'NA' + 'Only' or 'EU' + 'Only' as applicable

4. Select 'Parent Supplier'


5. Enter Supplier Name or SUID

6. Select 'Only'

7. Data will populate below


Toolkit Key

  • Self Service → Yes = Supplier has access to LTSS tool
  • Self Service → No = Supplier does not have access to LTSS tool
  • Feed Level → Parent = Parent level inventory feed
    • Supplier has 1 inventory feed, regardless of warehouses
  • Feed Level → Child = Child level inventory feed
    • Supplier has multiple warehouses and submits inventory separately for each
  • Ship Class
    • SP = Small Parcel
    • LP = LTL
  • LT Method = Supplier's set lead time method
    • Product LT = Supplier sets SKU based lead times
    • Bulk LT = Supplier sets Bulk lead times
  • Bulk LT = Lead time assigned to Ship Class (SP or LT)


Tip 2

LT Pad means lead time pad and is also called a carrier pad, these mean the same thing and are used interchangeably by some vendors and internal employees. This means that we add this time on top of the lead time to allow for the carrier to come in.


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

Visual 1.3:

Visual 1.4:

2

Determine if the supplier is currently tracked as a MTO supplier
  1. To check if a SU has MTO products, go to  Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead time settings> Filter to the supplier and the filter MTO to 1
    1. If the MTO filter only shows 0 and not the option to select 1, then the SU is not MTO →  Step 3
    2. If the MTO filter shows 0 and 1 then the SU is MTO → If a supplier is MTO they can have access to the self service LTSS → Skip to Step 2 of Wants Access to Lead Time Self Service Tool. 

3

Check to see if the supplier is currently meeting our lead time expectations
  • If the supplier has lead times of NA 24 hour SP, 48 hour LP; EU 24 hour SP, 24 hour LP and they want to extend their lead times send  template
  • If the supplier currently is not aligned to our lead time expectations skip to step 3.2 “Supplier pushes back”. 
  1. Supplier agrees to  template→ Follow Done > Done Procedure🛑
  2. Supplier pushes back → Ask for the following information and proceed to Step 2 of the Wants Access to Lead Time Self Service Tool tab. 40 incomplete Can you provide insight and the business reasoning into why you are requesting this change? 41 incomplete Have you confirmed that the new lead time is compatible with your carrier's collections? 42 incomplete What is the time frame for this change? Is it temporary or permanent?
    1. Note: if the request is temporary and for less than 3 weeks, we recommend not to change your lead times as it will take our system this long to process the request

4

If the supplier wants to continue with the change of lead times

If the supplier wants to continue with the change of lead times, follow the steps below based on their current settings: either Product or Bulk Level, make sure to include all the information you found in steps 1-3.


IF: SU currently has Product Level

Suppliers with MTO catalogs can have access to this self service tool. Determine if supplier has access to Lead Time Self Service tool.

  1. Navigate to Lead Time Performance
    1. HAS Access (Visual 4.1): → Send template🛑
    2. NO Access (Visual 4.2) → To check if a SU has MTO products, go to  Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead time settings> Filter to the supplier and the filter MTO to 1
      1. If the MTO filter only shows 0 and not the option to select 1, then the SU is not MTO→ Send Access to LTSS - Need to Confirm MTO template to confirm if the supplier is MTO. If supplier IS NOT MTO → Send  template🛑
        • Suppler pushes back → Send template🛑
      2. If the MTO filter only shows 0 and 1 then the SU is MTO→ Follow steps below


CHECK IF: APS Supplier

How to Check if APS Supplier

  1. Go to tab “supplier” on ServiceHub (Visual 4.3)
  2. Scroll down to “Wayfair & Supplier Contacts” 
  3. Scroll to page 2 and view “SOPS Account Ops (EU: Order Handling SOPS)” 
    1. If showing Asia Supplier Service Desk then check on page 1 of contacts for a dedicated SRM (Supplier Relationship Manager) (Visual 4.4)

IF: No

    1. Submit Global FS&R Ticket

  6. Submit ticket and link to the ticket

Steps

Actions

How to check if SU has MTO products

To check if a SU has MTO products, go to Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead time settings > Filter to the supplier and the filter MTO to 1

    • If the supplier is not listed in the Toolkit as a MTO supplier, but they confirm they do have a large amount of MTO products, submit a ticket here to add them to the Toolkit. You can continue on the steps below and do not need to wait for this ticket to be complete, however make sure to include that the ticket to add the SU to the MTO toolkit has been completed and include the ticket number [ticket will start with POPSAI]. → STEP 4
    • If the supplier is not listed in the Toolkit as a MTO and confirms they do not have MTO products → send  template
      • Suppler pushes back → Send SKU Based Lead Time Pushback template🛑


If the SU is not appearing on MTO filter, review this master sheet:

greentruelargelinkMTO Listhttps://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jQC7PGlP7ON-SzmaGh3wcUevk6teUN1ZENjhvJPVVts/edit?usp=sharing

Check if SU already has access to LTSS

      1. If the supplier is not listed in the Toolkit as a MTO supplier, but they confirm they do have a large amount of MTO products, submit a ticket here to add them to the Toolkit. You can continue on the steps below and do not need to wait for this ticket to be complete.

Request Lead Time Method Change (Bulk to SKU Based OR SKU to Bulk Based)


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead Time Settings

Check Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead Time Settings to determine if supplier has Bulk Lead Time settings or Product Lead Time settings

  1. Open Global FS&R Tool Kit > Fulfillment Metrics

    1. Enter the correct Region/GEO and SUID (Parent Supplier) (Visual 1.1)

  2. Step 2: Navigate to the “LT Method” Tab to determine if the supplier has Bulk LT settings or Product Level settings (MTO) and the ship class by parcel type 

    1. Ship Class refers to settings for Small Parcel (SP) vs. Large Parcel (LP)

    2. This report will be split based on Supplier Warehouse

    3. Bulk LT: LT Method will state “Bulk LT” - Self Service Tab will state “No” (Visual 1.2)

    4. Product Level: Method will state “Product LT” - Self Service Tab will state “Yes” (Visual 1.3)

  3. Step 3: Navigate to the “Cutoff Time” tab to determine the suppliers warehouse cutoff times and the “LT Pads” tab to determine if the supplier currently has lead time pads (Visual 1.4)


Global FS&R Toolkit - Supplier Lead Time Settings Guide

1. Open the Global FS&R Toolkit

2. Select 'Store' dropdown

3. Change 'Store' to 'NA' + 'Only' or 'EU' + 'Only' as applicable

4. Select 'Parent Supplier'


5. Enter Supplier Name or SUID

6. Select 'Only'

7. Data will populate below


Toolkit Key

  • Self Service → Yes = Supplier has access to LTSS tool
  • Self Service → No = Supplier does not have access to LTSS tool
  • Feed Level → Parent = Parent level inventory feed
    • Supplier has 1 inventory feed, regardless of warehouses
  • Feed Level → Child = Child level inventory feed
    • Supplier has multiple warehouses and submits inventory separately for each
  • Ship Class
    • SP = Small Parcel
    • LP = LTL
  • LT Method = Supplier's set lead time method
    • Product LT = Supplier sets SKU based lead times
    • Bulk LT = Supplier sets Bulk lead times
  • Bulk LT = Lead time assigned to Ship Class (SP or LT)

Send Template

Send  template


3

Submit a Global FS&R Ticket

Once confirmed, submit a Global FS&R Ticket to change Bulk LT or request LTSS access (product level/MTO products)

  1. Select subtopic: ‘Request a LT method change’
  2. Add Supplier Name + SUID
  3. Select current LT settings and what they want them changed to
  4. Submit Global FS&R Ticket  and link to the ticket
    1. **Before submitting a ticket, make sure you have all needed information. FSR tickets have a longer response time so filling out the ticket completely will help to cut down further delays.
  5. Inform supplier ticket has been submitted and SLA is 5 days
  6. Proactively check the ticket and update the supplier on the progress. Do not let it close automatically without any comments from our side.
  7. Once lead time method change is confirmed → send  template and submit Global FS&R Ticket
    1. Bulk LT Change
      1. Select ‘NA: Supplier Lead Time Settings’ or ‘EU: Supplier Lead Time Settings’ based on Geo
      2. Select subtopic: ‘Request a Bulk LT Change’ or
      3. Add requested lead time in x, confirm with the supplier what lead times should be set for SP and/or LP
      4. Use the information you received from the supplier in from  template to fill out the ticket
        1. **Before submitting a ticket, make sure you have all needed information from the supplier. FSR tickets have a longer response time so filling out the ticket completely will help to cut down further delays.
      5. Submit ticket and link to the ticket
      6. Send  template → Follow Done > Done Procedure🛑
    2. Product Level → Suppliers with MTO catalogs can have access to the self service tool. → Follow steps below to give them access to LTSS
      1. Submit Global FS&R Ticket  
        1. **Before submitting a ticket, make sure you have all needed information. FSR tickets have a longer response time so filling out the ticket completely will help to cut down further delays.
      2. Select subtopic: “Request a LT Method Change”
      3. Add Supplier Name + SUID and request access to the Lead Time Self Service tool → “Requesting access to LTSS as the the supplier’s products have been confirmed MTO”
      4. Submit ticket and link to the ticket
        1. Pause the ticket until a response is given on the FS&R ticket
        2. If the FS&R team requests more information, follow up on the ticket to the supplier and relay any further information to FS&R
      5. Once confirmed that access to LTSS has been given → Send  template🛑


If SU is reaching out with requests to change lead times for all items and have additional requests such as; changing their cut off times, updating order caps, leadtime pads, advising they manufacture in another country, then you can raise the LT bulk ticket and add additional requests as a comment on this ticket raised. Ie. you do not need to raise multiple requests for the same SU once you have one main Global FS&R Ticket open.



4

IF: Submitting Global FS&R Ticket

Send the  template.

Steps

Actions

Review Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead Time Settings

  1. Open Global FS&R Tool Kit > Fulfillment Metrics

    1. Enter the correct Region/GEO and SUID (Parent Supplier) (Visual 1.1)

  2. Step 2: Navigate to the “LT Method” Tab to determine if the supplier has Bulk LT settings or Product Level settings (MTO) and the ship class by parcel type 

    1. Ship Class refers to settings for Small Parcel (SP) vs. Large Parcel (LP)

    2. This report will be split based on Supplier Warehouse

    3. Bulk LT: LT Method will state “Bulk LT” - Self Service Tab will state “No” (Visual 1.2)

    4. Product Level: Method will state “Product LT” - Self Service Tab will state “Yes” (Visual 1.3)

  3. Step 3: Navigate to the “Cutoff Time” tab to determine the suppliers warehouse cutoff times and the “LT Pads” tab to determine if the supplier currently has lead time pads (Visual 1.4)


Global FS&R Toolkit - Supplier Lead Time Settings Guide

1. Open the Global FS&R Toolkit

Toolkit Key

  • Self Service → Yes = Supplier has access to LTSS tool
  • Self Service → No = Supplier does not have access to LTSS tool
  • Feed Level → Parent = Parent level inventory feed
    • Supplier has 1 inventory feed, regardless of warehouses
  • Feed Level → Child = Child level inventory feed
    • Supplier has multiple warehouses and submits inventory separately for each
  • Ship Class
    • SP = Small Parcel
    • LP = LTL
  • LT Method = Supplier's set lead time method
    • Product LT = Supplier sets SKU based lead times
    • Bulk LT = Supplier sets Bulk lead times
  • Bulk LT = Lead time assigned to Ship Class (SP or LT)

Confirm new cutoff time with SU

If not already provided, confirm with supplier what they are requesting the new Cutoff time to be:

3

Submit a Global FS&R Ticket 

Once confirmed, submit a Global FS&R Ticket **Before submitting a ticket, make sure you have all needed information. FSR tickets have a longer response time so filling out the ticket completely will help to cut down further delays.

  1. Select subtopic: “Request a Cutoff Time Change”
  2. Input Parent SUID
    1. Add Child SUID(s) as applicable
  3. Input requested SP / LP Cutoff Times in Warehouse local time.
  4. Submit ticket and link to the ticket
  5. Inform supplier ticket has been submitted and SLA is 5 days→ Pause the ticket until a response is given on the FS&R ticket

  6. Once confirmation is given by FS&R Team, let supplier know → Follow Done > Done Procedure🛑


If SU is reaching out with requests to change lead times for all items and have additional requests such as; changing their cut off times, updating order caps, leadtime pads, advising they manufacture in another country, then you can raise the LT bulk ticket and add additional requests as a comment on this ticket raised. Update Replacement Parts


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template to Supplier

Replacement part lead times are centrally managed, and we have defined shipping expectations for acknowledging and shipping replacement parts within established service levels. As such, we are unable to change Replacement Part lead times for Suppliers.

Utilize the  template to respond to the Supplier.




Speed Badging


mediumnote

Note: applicable for US, CA, UK, except for Wayfair Ireland.


mediuminfo

Speed ship badging at Wayfair is a feature on the storefront that highlights the reliability and speed of a product's delivery promise. This program allows dropship suppliers with strong delivery performance to have their products marked with a badge indicating guaranteed delivery times. The badges specify that items will be delivered within a certain timeframe, such as 1-day, 2-day, 3-day, or "Fast Delivery" (arriving in 3-5 days for small parcels and 4-7 days for large parcels). Please note that this service is only for Wayfair US, CA, UK, we don't have this set up for Wayfair Ireland at this time.

To qualify for speed badging, products must meet specific criteria, including:

  • Delivery within 5 days or less for small parcels and 7 days or less for large parcels.
  • Positive inventory quantities (items with unlimited inventory quantities do not qualify).
  • Consistent on-time shipping performance.
  • Eligibility for speed badging is updated weekly based on supplier performance metrics, ensuring that only suppliers who consistently meet the delivery promises are awarded the badge.

If further investigation is needed to see why ship badge is missing, #wayfair-verified-help Slack channel can help.

The screenshot below is an example of what speed badging looks like:


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Respond to SUS using the Dropship Speed Badging Overview template and close the ticket. 


2

Move Onshore

If SUS replies & reopens with additional questions or follow-up queries, move the case onshore for further handling.




Issues with Delivery Promise on Site


mediuminfo

This will require some investigation! 🤓Please make sure you complete the following steps before reaching out in a cross functional Slack channel - if you need help please reach out in team lead


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Acquire Examples

Make sure you have SKU and/or PO examples

  1. Determine if its impact all SKUs and POs or if it is just some

Confirm the suppliers current lead times using the Lead-Time Settings tool to determine the lead time the supplier’s products are adhering to


2

Determine Frequency of Pickups

Determine what is the frequency of pickups from carriers

  1. If suppliers have longer lead times and limited pickups this can cause longer delivery times. Please confirm with the supplier that their settings are accurate

3

IF: Settings Inaccurate - Escalate

If you find the settings are inaccurate, please escalate the ticket onshore for further support. 


4

IF: Submitting Global FS&R Ticket

Send the  template.


This step is ONLY applicable if you are submitting a Global FS&R Ticket.





Questions on an order / PO Must Ship by Date


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm the date the PO was placed

Confirm the date the PO was placed


2

Confirm the supplier's cutoff time

Confirm the supplier's cutoff time


3

Confirm the supplier's lead times

Confirm the supplier’s lead times

  1. If supplier is on Bulk-level lead times, check Global FS&R Toolkit > Lead Time Settings
  2. If supplier is on Product-Level lead times, check Global FS&R Toolkit > Product LT Optimization > Search Impacted Part #. This allows you to view the lead time for a specific item

4

Determine if there is a discrepancy

Determine if there is a discrepancy by checking Order Wizard for the time the order was sent to the Supplier

  1. If the order was placed before the cutoff time, and the supplier has a lead time of 1 day, it is possible that the must ship by date will be the same day 

5

Escalate Onshore

Once you have all the findings and if all settings are accurate, please first reply with education:  template.

  1. Fill in highlighted portions of response with lead time information from the Global FS&R Toolkit > Fulfillment Metrics
  2. If you are unsure about your findings, please add them to the ticket as an internal comment and escalate the ticket onshore. 

6

IF: Submitting Global FS&R Ticket

Send the  template.


This step is ONLY applicable if you are submitting a Global FS&R Ticket.





Less Common Inquiries


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Update Lead Time Pads
  1. Submit Global FS&R Ticket 
  2. Select subtopic: “Request to Add/Remove LT Pads’”
  3. Send  template ensuring to edit/tailor it to the SUs topic.


  • Additional lead time applied to a day of week or specific date to accommodate variability in fulfillment capabilities


If SU is reaching out with requests to change lead times for all items and have additional requests such as; changing their cut off times, updating order caps, leadtime pads, advising they manufacture in another country, then you can raise the LT bulk ticket and add additional requests as a comment on this ticket raised. Ie. you do not need to raise multiple requests for the same SU once you have one main Global FS&R Ticket open.



2

Add or Remove Order Caps
  1. Submit Global FS&R Ticket 
  2. Select subtopic: “Request to Add/Remove Order Caps’”
  3. Send  template ensuring to edit/tailor it to the SUs topic.


  • Order caps is the maximum number of order supplier can process in a day


If SU is reaching out with requests to change lead times for all items and have additional requests such as; changing their cut off times, updating order caps, leadtime pads, advising they manufacture in another country, then you can raise the LT bulk ticket and add additional requests as a comment on this ticket raised. Ie. you do not need to raise multiple requests for the same SU once you have one main Global FS&R Ticket open.



3

EU Supplier manufactures in a different country
  1. Submit Global FS&R Ticket 
  2. Select subtopic: “Request a LT method change” or “Supplier Part Lead Times” depending on the request
  3. Send  template ensuring to edit/tailor it to the SUs topic.


  • Some EU Suppliers have one Warehouse with Wayfair (in DE or UK) but they manufacture in another country and ship the SKUs to their main Warehouse, hence Lead Times are different → they can have access to SKU Based Lead Times and LTSS


If SU is reaching out with requests to change lead times for all items and have additional requests such as; changing their cut off times, updating order caps, leadtime pads, advising they manufacture in another country, then you can raise the LT bulk ticket and add additional requests as a comment on this ticket raised. Ie. you do not need to raise multiple requests for the same SU once you have one main Global FS&R Ticket open.



4

IF: Submitting Global FS&R Ticket

Send the  template.

This step is ONLY applicable if you are submitting a Global FS&R Ticket.
