#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

Address supplier inquiries regarding NMFC and Freight Class Codes


Overview

Content addresses supplier inquires to NMFC and Freight Class Codes



Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

NMFC Codes

NMFC codes (National Motor Freight Classification) are standardized codes in North America to classify goods for LTL shipping.

Freight Classes

Shipping class codes based on density, handling, stowablity and liability.

NMFTA

National Motor Freight Traffic Association



General Concepts


General Concepts

Guidance

Suppliers who don’t frequently ship freight enters a ticket asking for the NMFC code or Freight Class

These are highly specific based on the exact nature of the goods vendors are responsible to obtain this information directly from NMFTA guidelines





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Review the Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Ticket
  1. Determine if the only questions are related to NMFC or Freight Codes
  2. Answer any questions unrelated to NMFTA guidelines
  3. Direct to reference the NMFTA guidelines to classify their items.

2

Send Template
Respond to the supplier utilizing the NMFC and Freight Code Requests template.

Overview

  • Suppliers enter EDI PO resend tickets when they require a PO to be resent to their system. POs can be resent via both EDI and email depending on the SU's level of integration.
  • EDI POs must be in a status of Pending Ship Confirmation for it to be successfully resent




Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool

Use Case











Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition











Before You Start

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Need PO to be Resent. 

Suppliers enter these tickets when they require a PO to be resent to their system.

We can resend POs via email and EDI, depending on the Supplier's level of integration.

As best practice, you should confirm that the EDI or Email event occurred as expected with no identifiable tech issues, resend the PO, and advise the Supplier to verify with either the email recipient or EDI provider to ensure no further receiving issues.

We are unable to resend orders via API, but we can support the Supplier in advising how they can resolve their issue.

Update Packing Slip


mediuminfo

#sc-palletization to submit request to remove or add ok to stack from packing slips

  • Example: https://wayfair.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C01BQPASF63


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm reason supplier is making request

Confirm reason supplier is making request (ie remove okay to stack because items are fragile)


2

Confirm SKUs Impacted and get Sprids

Confirm skus impacted and get sprids


3

Submit Request in Channel

Script to Pull Sprids

SELECT

spr.Sprid

,spr.SprPrSKU SKU

,pd.SupplierID

,pd.shipviaid Sku_Ship_Via_ID,

   CASE pd.shipviaid

    WHEN 1 THEN 'Ground'

    WHEN 2 THEN 'LTL'

    WHEN 3 THEN 'White Glove'

    WHEN 4 THEN 'White Glove'

    WHEN 5 THEN 'White Glove'

    WHEN 6 Then 'White Glove'

    When 7 Then 'LTL'

    WHEN 10 THEN 'White Glove'

    WHEN 11 THEN 'LTL'

    WHEN 16 THEN 'White Glove'

    WHEN 19 THEN 'LTL'

    WHEN 204 THEN 'LTL'

    WHEN 205 THEN 'LTL'

    WHEN 206 THEN 'White Glove'

    WHEN 209 THEN 'LTL'

    WHEN 278 Then 'LTL'

    WHEN 207 THEN 'WG'

    WHEN 265 THEN 'LTL'

    WHEN 309 Then 'Ground'

    END Sku_Ship_Via




FROM `wf-gcp-us-ae-sql-data-prod.csn_wms.tbl_stock_product` spr

LEFT OUTER JOIN `wf-gcp-us-ae-merch-engg-prod.ds_catalog_identifier.vw_product_supplier_manufacturer_part` pd on spr.sprprsku = pd.prsku


WHERE pd.SupplierID = 130456 --< Use Primary SUID

-- AND spr.sprprsku in ('LATA1007','LATA1604','LATA1621') --< remove -- to search specific skus


Submit request in #sc-palletization Slack channel with the following information:

  • Supplier Name
  • SUID
  • Request to add/remove ‘Okay to Stack’ from packing slips
  • Reason for request
  • Include Sprid/SKU list


If supplier wants all items by freight class filter appropriately, if supplier provides specific items include skus in script




4

Send Template

Respond to the supplier with the Update Packing Slip Request Submitted template.

Cancellation Blockers SOP


mediuminfofalse

When we are unable to successfully cancel a PO due to a replacement, refund, or return set up against an original unshipped item, we will need to inform the supplier the PO status will remain unchanged, and log the impact of the blocker. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals


1

Review PO

Review the PO in the order view to determine what is blocking the cancellation. This can be for one of several reasons:

  • A replacement was issued on an unshipped PO (Hard Block)
  • A return/refund was issued on an unshipped PO
  • An order is paid for through a Financer (ACIMA, AfterPay, Klarna, etc.) 


Original Unshipped PO Replaced


Hard Block: Move directly to Step 2



Rerouted Return on an Unshipped PO


Potential Soft Block: Please ping to see if the return line item can be deleted and to cancel the PO



The Payment Method on the Order is a Financer


Soft Block: Please ping to cancel the PO





2

Send Template

If the PO cannot be cancelled, please send the supplier the Unable to Cancel PO Template 



3

Report the Impacted PO

Open the Cancellation Blocker Examples Sheet. Record the following information in the sheet to completion: 

  • Ticket ID
  • Impacted PO
  • Blocking Issue
  • Resolution
  • Related Tech Ticket (if applicable)

Confirm Reason

Confirm the reason supplier is making the request i.e. duplicate requests, excessive samples, out of stock

Review for Cancellation
  • Fabric, wallpaper or other no cost are okay to cancel as they are offered as a courtesy
  • Tile samples customers are allowed to order 10 samples within 2 weeks with a max of five samples per transactions anything over this should be cancelled if, one sample per item and color combination, customers wanting more than this need to purchase a box. (service policy US SFS: Samples of Flooring)

3

Process Cancellation

Process the cancellation


4

Send Template

Update the ticket with the Needs PO Cancelled template




Cannot Print SOP



Process


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Attempt to reprint the label

Before attempting to reprint: 

  1. Reindex the PO via this link
  2. Refresh the PO in Partner Home >> Dropship Orders following the steps in the Check Current Label Print Status tab.


  • If you are successful, attach the label and update the ticket for the Supplier using the relevant template (AOPS General Label Print Error Resolved or POS Issue resolved, label printed (Label_Resolved) . 
  • If you are unsuccessful, move to step 2


When reindexing the PO, you should not be logged in as the Supplier. It is helpful to open this in an incognito tab


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2: 

2

Identify root cause

If you are unfamiliar with the reported error or are unable to determine from the error message what the root cause is and it is not listed in either the BPO AOPS or POS Common Label Print Errors then review the Service owner and Service failure reported in the ticket.

Visual 2:

3

Research if error has been reported

Use the EU Bugs Report, NA Bugs Report and Sandra’s Daily Tech Ticket updates to identify whether a ticket exists already for this error. If the issue has already been reported, add your examples. Be sure to provide as much detail as possible and ensure the example you’re adding matches the description of the ticket. Details include: Geo, SuID, PO examples, screenshots, the current outcome and the expected outcome.

Ctrl + F in slack and search the tech ticket number to find the most up to date thread if this is being discussed in multiple channels



4

Enter Tech Ticket

 If a ticket has not yet been entered, use the C2S: Tech Ticket Entry Triage Guide for assistance on identifying the correct path based on the Service name and Service owner in the ticket.

For best practices on entering the ticket, please refer to the Global Partner Support Tech Issue Handbook



5

Inform team of error

Inform your team via your team slack channel that a new issue has been identified. Be sure to briefly describe the issue and share the ticket link.


6

Escalate tech issue

When should I escalate a tech ticket?: If a ticket has not been updated within the owners team’s SLA and you cannot see anybody working on this in slack or the relevant slack channels or tickets. The teams SLA can be found at the top of the NA Bugs Report Infohub here.

How do I escalate tickets? Contact your manager. Your manager will be responsible for escalating this issue to Tier II. Tier II will take ownership of the issue in an effort to drive a quick resolution.

If the issue is leading to consistent poor supplier experiences (think SSAT), if Suppliers are becoming increasingly frustrated, if we’re seeing the issue grow in size, if the issue has grown to being a complete blocker/criticality.





Incorrect Label Count


If the supplier needs 1 label, but gets multiple labels, then set it to 1 label per order. If the supplier needs multiple labels, then set it to 1 label per carton. 


DO NOT SEND TO BPO

Actions

Log into Partner Home as the Supplier


Note: If the supplier has multiple warehouses, make sure to double-check that you are updating the setting for the correct warehouse.

Visual 2:

Click on Account

Click on ‘Account’

Click on Supplier Account

Click on ‘Supplier Account’

Check for the Supplier’s selection for ‘Default number of labels per purchase order’ under the ‘Documents, Terms and Configurations’ section 

  1. If changing the account settings to either “1 label per carton” or “1 label per order”: Attach the updated documents, update the ticket with the Incorrect Label Count template, and mark as complete

Unable to Retrieve Order Details - Missing MPID


This SOP should be enacted when the ‘Print Documents’ page in Partner Home fails to load with the following error: 

1 order has been removed from your selection because of issues retrieving order details


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Replicate the Issue

Replicate the issue on incognito browser. 


2

Open GBQ
  1. If the issue persists, open GBQ using the following link: https://console.cloud.google.com/bigquery?project=wf-gcp-eu-dbaas-prod&ws=!1m0
  2. Create a new window in GBQ by selecting the “+” button. (Visual 2)

Visual 2:


3

Copy and Run the Query
  1. Copy the following query into the query editor, and enter the PO number in the last line. Make sure to remove the PO prefix (“CS”, “UK”, etc), then select “Run”. (Visual 3.1)
  2. If the OpComponentManufacturerPartID returned is “null,” submit a ServiceNow ticket using this intake form. If the OpComponentManufacturerPartID column populates number(s), submit a GPS Tech Help Desk ticket here.


SQL Query


ServiceNow Intake Template
  • Who is this for?:  Your name
  • Location: [Leave as auto-populated]
  • Select a service or product: Order Processing 
  • Please select a symptom: Tools Issue
  • Provide a brief summary of the issue: [Add PO] - Cannot Generate Shipping Documents Due to Missing MPID
  • Order number: [Add order id] (Ex. For CS646462191, order id = 4248847066)
  • Item number: [Add item number(s)] (Ex. For CS646462191, item # = 11306022216, 11306022246, and 11306022266)

Additional information: leave empty


To identify the order number and item number(s), search for the PO in the legacy order wizard. Visual 3.2 shows where you can identify these fields.



Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

4

Wait 45 Minutes

Once the engineer on the ServiceNow ticket reports that the MPID has been backfilled, wait 45 minutes before validating the backfill. After 45 minutes, validate the backfill by either re-running the query in Step 3 or attempting to reprint labels in Partner Home.

  • If the backfill was completed successfully, the query in Step 4 will return a number(s), and labels will print successfully in Partner Home. 
  • If label printing fails again, inform the assignee by commenting on the ServiceNow ticket.








EU



Before You Start

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Dropship Order Fulfillment Support. 

These tickets were initially entered by the Supplier under a different Request Type and assigned to a BPO agent. When BPO cannot resolve the Supplier's issue from the information provided in the SOP, they will escalate for In-House Support.

The original Request Type will be visible to you in the ticket Components and in an automated comment added to the ticket. You should always check this to ensure the Supplier followed the correct ticket entry pathway for their issue and provide education where this was not the case. 

The exact same ticket categories exist in the POS project as in the AOPs project. However, some differ very slightly in the naming, for example AOPS = Cannot Print Shipping Documents vs POS = Cannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label.

FYI The actionable queue statuses also work the same in the POS project as the AOPs project. 


Request Types Handled by BPO
  • Cancellation Inquiry
  • Cannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label
  • Change Ship Method or Carrier on PO due to Item Count/Size
  • Need PO to be Resent
  • PO has a Wrong Price
  • Problem with an Order
  • Product Out of Stock
  • Reroute PO to a different warehouse
  • Shipping/Carrier Questions
  • Something Else
  • Tier 1 Adhoc Request
  • Update Tracking Number/ Order Status

It is always helpful to first validate that the BPO agent has followed the correct steps in the relevant SOP. 

 This is particularly important for label failures which make up 65% of our onshore escalations as we often see the label print upon second attempt (can also be due to some time passed and system glitch has resolved).



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant for your investigation. 

You can see the current Customer Request Type and the PO Number. You can also see the original Customer Request Type from the Components:

Visual 1:

2

Review Internal Comment from BPO

The BPO agent will also have added an internal comment advising on the support required. 

Visual 2:

3

Open the relevant SOP

Based on the original Customer Request Type, open the relevant SOP.

Use the information provided by the BPO agent and the SOP to identify the specific issue: 

  1. Additional steps required by Onshore to resolve the Supplier's issue
  2. BPO agent requires a training pointer as they did not follow the SOP
  3. Relevant information is missing from the SOP
  4. Specific language support is required 
  5. Issue may require Tier 2 Escalation

Based on this, move to the relevant tab.

Manual Pallet Registration


A manual pallet label may be required when you cannot create pallets in Partner Home due to:

3: Shipment Status

Scheduled: 

  1. Status of a shipment immediately after you have created it. 
  2. This status will remain until the cutoff time (1:30pm CET one business day before the collection date) has passed after which the status will change to “Transmitted”. 
  3. Whilst a shipment has status “Scheduled”, you can edit, cancel it and print pallet labels. 

Transmitted: 

  1. Status of a shipment once Wayfair has communicated the shipment data to the collection carrier after the cutoff time. 
  2. Once a shipment has this status, you can no longer cancel it. Pallet label print is still possible at this stage.

Cancelled: 

  1. Status of a shipment once you have cancelled it. 
  2. You will not be able to edit or print pallet labels for these shipments.

Legacy Tool Requests to Reset Carrier Assignment


The Legacy Tool is a tool built by engineering that helps us reset the original carrier assignment.

This is used for label failure error messages such as:

  • Received an empty response from Carrier Booking API
  • This value should not be blank. at Account name
  • shipment.serviceAgentId , serviceAgentId is invalid
  • Shipment record was not found in the database.
  • Account number must be provided at Account number

BPO and other teams can request our help to run the legacy tool in this slack channel #legacy-tool-requests. The POCs in this channel are Sylwia Mikolajczyk and Anett Grego.

Please run the legacy tool if you have access to it. 

This guide provides a detailed outline of how to use the Legacy Tool. 



Tech Issues


Similar to NA, the EU also experiences some system hiccups which lead to tech issues for our Suppliers and us.

The best place to check for EU tech issues is the EU Partner Support - Bugs Report, in addition to the NA Bugs Report.

EU typically experiences the same issues as NA such as label failures, change ship method not working etc.

The reporting steps are the same for both geos in that if you can identify the correct team/project/slack channel responsible, you should go ahead and submit the ticket to them to report the issue.

Review the truesmallGPS Tech Issue Handbookhttps://infohub.corp.wayfair.com/x/1APlNg for guidance on entering a tech ticket.

This specifically relates to cases where a ticket transfer pathway is not outlined in the Ticket Transfer Matrix.

This is still a common request we get from Suppliers who aren’t familiar with their DDA agreements and they request us to confirm the charge prior to sending replacement parts.

Parts are not covered in the DDA but we do not accept the charges and these should be sent at the Supplier's expense as per this Help Center article.

Evri Postcode Issue


This is often due to one of 3 root causes: 

  • The weights/dimensions exceed Evri’s capacity and it needs to be upgraded to LP
  • The shipping postcode is not available as a 24 Evri service and so the delivery type needs to be changed to 'UK - Small Parcel Courier' as per the SOP
  • The shipping postcode is incorrect based on Evri’s system. This could be due to the area being a new build or newly established postcode:
    • Take the post code and add it to https://www.royalmail.com/find-a-postcode 
    • To verify that the postcode is the correct one, copy the postcode and navigate to Google maps enter the address or postcode and zoom in. Zoom in to the area and you will get the postcode on the left side of the screen, you can try if this is working. It takes some trial and error and experience to get the correct post code.

1256145383.body:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S)

Last Updated:  

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP IntroductionPurpose


Objective

This SOP outlines the required process for actioning tickets submitted under the Request Type: PO Reroutes.

Effective December 15, 2025, a compliance policy requires all suppliers to ship orders from the warehouse assigned by the Wayfair system at the time the order is placed.

Orders are routed based on:

  • Supplier-reported inventory
  • Warehouse availability
  • Customer delivery location


Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool

Use Case

Ticket template responses for the Account Ops (AOPS) project.


Before You Begin


Important Guidelines

  • Suppliers cannot reroute POs to CastleGate themselves — follow the department transfer matrix instructions.
  • If a PO is already routed to a CastleGate warehouse, it cannot be rerouted again.
  • Suppliers cannot ship multi-item orders from different warehouses unless Wayfair reroutes the individual items.
  • Suppliers cannot change a PO from a Canada warehouse to a U.S. warehouse (or vice versa), as these are treated as separate suppliers.
  • Suppliers may change the ship-from warehouse for a complete PO without contacting Wayfair.
  • Parts cannot be rerouted. 
Scenarios Where Reroute Requests Will Be Rejected

Suppliers may submit reroute requests for several reasons, including:

  • No stock in the originally assigned warehouse, but inventory is available in another warehouse
  • A new PO is needed because the original PO was cancelled in error in the supplier’s system
  • The PO number was not received
  • Inventory is available in CastleGate and the supplier cannot complete the reroute themselves
  • As part of the resolution for other request types
  • API-registered suppliers needing reroutes for billing or registration purposes
  • Split PO requests raised from Supplier (separate to onshore internal request)

Policy Enforcement Statement 

Agents must deny all Dropship-to-Dropship reroute requests unless explicitly permitted under the Dropship to CastleGate exception outlined in this SOP.

Wayfair is no longer able to reroute Dropship POs to another Dropship warehouse.

Suppliers must ship from the warehouse assigned in the Wayfair system or change the Ship From warehouse via Partner Home.

Shipping from a non-assigned warehouse may result in automated credit deductions. Click to Ship cannot override or waive these deductions.


#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflowmediumfalse

Unless designated with greenlocationNA Specific or magentalocationEU Specific, process should be presumed as being globalized.

Review Ticket

#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

  1. Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

  2. You can see the Customer Request Type, PO Number, and the warehouse to which they wish to reroute. (Visual 1.1)

  3. Confirm the following; 

    1. Confirm PO is Dropship (not CastleGate)

    2. Confirm PO has not already shipped

    3. Confirm PO is not cancelled

  5. Move to PO Reroutes tab.

Visual 1.1:

PO Reroutes

IMPORTANT INFO

Effective December 15, 2025, VCN agents must deny all dropship-to-dropship reroute requests unless explicitly permitted under Section 6 (CastleGate exception), or for the following suppliers: Emery Jensen (Parent SUID 23967).

If Emery Jensen request a reroute, please immediately escalate the ticket to Tier 1 for next steps.

  • Orders are routed based on inventory availability at the time the order is placed.

  • To align with this policy, we are no longer able to reroute dropship POs to another dropship warehouse.

  • All suppliers must ship from the warehouse assigned in the Wayfair system or self-serve by changing the Ship from Warehouse at label print.

#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Supplier Reporting Wayfair Inventory Tech Issue 

If the supplier states there is a Wayfair inventory system issue, respond using: the Supplier Reports Wayfair Inventory Tech Issue template.


2

Review Common Denial Scenarios 

  1. Supplier Requests Reroute to a Different Warehouse (Dropship POs)

 If the supplier requests a reroute for a Dropship PO for any of the following reasons, deny the request:

  • No stock in the assigned warehouse (system showing incorrect stock) 

  • Inventory is held at another warehouse

  • Request to ship from a warehouse other than the one assigned

  • Request to split the PO raised from Supplier (separate to onshore requests)

  • API registration suppliers requiring POs rerouted for billing or registration purposes

  • PO number was not received

Respond utilizing the Assigned Warehouse Policy – No Dropship Reroute template.


3

Supplier Reopens Ticket (Pushback Scenario)

  1. If the supplier reopens the ticket and continues requesting the reroute, respond with the No Dropship Reroute: SU Pushback template.

    1. This template is specifically used when the supplier re-requests a reroute after denial.

  2. If the supplier reopens the ticket claiming there is a Wayfair inventory tech issue, respond with the Supplier Reports Wayfair Inventory Tech Issue template.

    1. Use this template only if it has not already been applied within the current ticket thread. If it has already been used, respond with the standard No Dropship Reroute: SU Pushback template.


4

Alternative Response

If the supplier continues to push back after the standard No Dropship Reroute: SU Pushback template has been used, utilize the  Alternative Escalation Response template.

This template should be used when:

  • Policy has already been clearly communicated

  • No exception applies

  • The supplier continues to challenge the decision

  • The offshore agent has exhausted standard resolution steps.

After sending the Alternative Escalation Response template, If the supplier persists or further review is required, route the ticket to onshore for review in accordance with team guidelines by utilizing Escalating for Onshore Support (Tier 0 to Tier 1) SOP (DSC2S) - VCN.


5

Supplier Reopens Ticket Requesting PO Cancellation

If the supplier reopens the ticket to request cancellation of a PO, utilize the Cancellation Inquiry SOP (DSC2S) - VCN and follow the cancellation steps on the How to Cancel an Order tab; proceed with cancelling the PO on the supplier’s behalf, provided the below critera is all met: 

Before Cancelling, Confirm:

  • The PO has not shipped

  • No tracking has been uploaded

  • No ASN has been created

  • The cancellation reason is clearly documented in the ticket

Volume Threshold:

  • If the request includes more than 10 POs, escalate the ticket onshore for T1 review before taking any cancellation action.

  • If the request includes fewer than 10 POs, proceed with the cancellations and respond to the supplier with the No Dropship Reroute: SU Requests Cancellation template.


6

Dropship → CastleGate 

If the supplier requests a reroute from Dropship to CastleGate, follow the steps below to ensure correct handling and routing.

  1. Before proceeding, confirm:

    1. Supplier has explicitly requested Dropship → CastleGate reroute

  2. If the reroute is eligible, do not manually process the transfer. Instead, reference the Global Transfer Matrix SOP (DSC2S) - VCN.

  3. Navigate to the “CastleGate” section.

  4. Follow the outlined transfer instructions.

  5. Transfer the ticket to the CGOB team as specified in the matrix.

  6. If the reroute is eligible, follow the outlined transfer instructions in the  Global Transfer Matrix SOP (DSC2S) - VCN under the the “CastleGate” section for CGOB.


7

Replacement Parts Reroute Request 

Replacement parts are automatically assigned to ship from the same warehouse as the original order.

We are unable to manually reroute replacement parts to a different warehouse. However, suppliers can select their ship-from warehouse during shipment registration in Partner Home.

If the supplier requests a replacement part reroute, respond using the Part Reroute Supplier Self Service template.


8

Create New PO to Replace Cancelled Supplier Order

If the supplier contacts us stating they have “canceled this in their system due to no stock” “PO canceled from our side” etc and requests that we cancel the PO, follow the steps below:

  1. Do not cancel the PO immediately

    1. We do not cancel POs due to no stock unless they are marked as Fill or Kill.

    2. Review Product Out of Stock SOP (DSC2S) - VCN, specifically the Cancellation Request due to Stock tab. 

    3. Identify Fill/Kill Status

    4. If the SU is marked as Kill, review Product Out of Stock SOP (DSC2S) - VCN, specifically the Cancellation Request due to Stock: Fill/Kill SU tab.

  2. If the SU is not marked as Fill or Kill (i.e., backorders are allowed), proceed to the next step.

  3. Reroute the PO

  4. Reroute the PO to the same warehouse it is currently assigned to.

  5. Wait for New PO Generation

  6. Allow the system to generate the new PO number.

  7. Notify the Supplier:

    1. Once the new PO number is generated, provide it to the supplier using the SU Reported No Stock - PO Rerouted & Reinstated template.

    2. Edit the template to include the newly generated PO number before sending.


Resolve Ticket

#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Use the relevant template to update the supplier of the action taken.

Ensure you make any necessary edits to the templates so these are tailored to the Supplier's specific query. 


2

Edit Comment (Optional)

If you need to make any edits to your comment after this is added, click the pencil icon. (Visual 1.2)

Visual 1.2:

3

Update Status

Select Change Status > Done. (Visual 1.3)

Visual 1.3:

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: PO has a wrong price.  

This process is partially automated, and you should take action based on the Supplier's reply or unresponsiveness. 

Regardless of the outcome, you must adhere to the and reply to every comment from the supplier before marking the ticket as complete.

Note: We are unable to resend PO's via EDI and amend the price when requested.


Overview

Add text here.




Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool (Linked)

Use Case

Templates to utilize when working PO has a Wrong Price tickets.



Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition













#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow


mediumfalse

Unless designated with greenlocationNA Specific or magentalocationEU Specific, process should be presumed as being globalized.



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type, the PO Number impacted, and an attached Excel file with the correct pricing information. (Visual 1.1)

Visual 1.1:

2

Review the Description
  1. When the ticket is entered by the Supplier, a first response will be automatically generated. (Visual 2.1)
    1. If the ticket was auto-updated, move to the Check for Supplier Response tab.
  2. If the supplier has submitted a request to cancel a PO due to incorrect pricing using a different customer request type, the automated update will not appear on the ticket. In this situation, navigate to the Incorrect CRT tab for the next steps. 

Visual 2.1:



Review Incorrect CRT


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Request
  1. Rather than change the customer request type which we no longer do, We will work the ticket under the current customer request type.
  2. Add the "Pricing" component to the ticket.  (Visual 1.1)
  3. Provide first update to the supplier utilizing the Incorrect Pricing: First Update template. (Visual 1.2, Tip 1)


Tip 1

The Incorrect Pricing: First Update template is slightly different to the automated message that suppliers receive when they raise the correct ticket “PO has a wrong price”. Incorrect Pricing: First Update has steps included to educate the supplier for future pricing issues requesting they raise the correct Customer Request type ticket. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Allow 2 Business Days to Pass
  1. Allow 2 business days to pass. 
  2. if no reply from the SU, navigate to the Check for Supplier Response tab and action following the normal process. 




Check for Supplier Response


This section explains what steps to take based on whether or not the Supplier responds. 

If the Supplier responds, the ticket will be automatically assigned to an agent via round robin/DRE.

If there is no response from the Supplier within 3 days, the below comment will automatically populate, and the ticket will be assigned to an agent via round robin/DRE:

Unresponsive Supplier Comment

This supplier has not responded in 3 days. Please verify that the order has not shipped, cancel the order, and close out this ticket.




Supplier Unresponsive


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Log into Partner Home

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (See Visual 1.1)

Visual 1.1:

2

Locate relevant PO

Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. (See Visual 2.1)

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. (See Visual 2.2)

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Confirm order status

If the Fulfilment Status is New or Processing, the order has not shipped. (See Visual 3.1)

If the status shows Awaiting Pickup or In Transit, the order has either shipped or the label has been printed and the order is awaiting shipping. 


Visual 3.1:

4

Cross Check Status on Order

From the Order tab, select the relevant PO to navigate to the Product tab.  

In the Destination section, review the Delivery Status. (See Visual 4.1; See Tip 1)

If the Delivery Status does not match the Fulfilment Status in Partner Home, move to Step 5. 

If the Delivery Status matches the Fulfilment Status in Partner Home, skip Step 5. 


Tip 1:

The terminology between the statuses won't match up exactly between the Product tab and Partner Home. Please see a comparison of these below: 

Partner HomeProduct Tab
New Order is at warehouse
ProcessingItem is on backorder
Awaiting PickupReady for pickup
In Transit (Awaiting Carrier Scan)Ready for pickup
In Transit Shipped



Visual 4.1:

5

Reindex the PO

If you are logged in as a Supplier in Partner Home, type Switch Back to Admin View in the red banner. (See Visual 5.1; See Tip 2)

Navigate to the Re-Indexing Tool. 

Paste the PO number into the text box in the Request Purchase Order Data Re-Indexing section. (See Visual 5.2; See Tip 3)

Select Submit. 

The Delivery Status on the Order should now match the Fulfilment Status in Partner Home.

Tip 2:

This tool can only be accessed when in Admin View and will not worked when logged in as a Supplier to Partner home. 


Tip 3:

If you need to reindex multiple POs, these can be entered together separated by a comma. 

This tool can be used to easily add commas. 


Visual 5.1:

Visual 5.2:

6

Review Order Status

  1. If the order status shows Awaiting Pickup or In Transit, update the Supplier using the Incorrect Pricing: Order Fulfillment template, and move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 
  2. If the order has not shipped, move to the Cancelling the Order tab.




Supplier Responds


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Response
  1. If the Supplier responds to advise the order should be cancelled, move to the Cancelling the Order tab. (Visual 1.1)
  2. If the Supplier responds to advise the order has shipped, or will be shipped, update the Supplier using the Incorrect Pricing: Order Fulfillment template, and move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Visual 1.1:





Cancelling the Order


This section explains how to cancel the order for the Supplier in cases where they have requested this or are unresponsive on the ticket. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Cancel the order

  1. Open the Bulk Cancellation Tool.
  2. Navigate to Initiate Cancellation.
  3. Select Purchase order numbers. (Visual 1.1)
  4. Paste in the list of relevant POs, with each separated by a comma. (Tip 1)
  5. Select Pricing Error for the Reason for cancellation. (Visual 1.2)
  6. Select Yes for the question, "For Have you already confirmed these items can be cancelled with the supplier?"
  7. Tick the checkboxes for Send Customer Email and Send Supplier Email.
  8. Click Initiate Cancellations. (Visual 1.3)


Tip 1:

This tool can be used to easily add commas. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

Visual 1.3:

2

Update Supplier

Update the Supplier using the Incorrect Pricing: Order Cancelled template, and move to the Resolve Ticket tab. (Tip 2)

Tip 2:

Ensure you make any necessary edits to the template so this is tailored to the Supplier's specific query. 





Resolve Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Use the relevant template to update the Reporter of the action taken. 

Templates for each scenario can be found . 

Ensure you make any necessary edits to the templates so these are tailored to the specific query. 



2

Edit Comment (Optional)
If you need to make any edits to your comment after this is added, click the pencil icon. 

 Visual 2.1:

3

Update StatusSelect Change Status > Done. (Visual 3.1)

 Visual 3.1:

1256152285.title: Problem with an Order (PWAO) SOP (DSC2S)
1256152285.author: Jenn Gill
1256152285.body:
Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) 

Last Updated:  




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

Wholesale Cost

Wholesale cost is the price a business (like a retailer) pays to buy products in large quantities directly from a manufacturer or distributor, significantly lower than the retail price to allow for bulk discounts and profit when reselling to the public. It covers the base cost of goods (materials, labor, overhead) plus a profit margin, enabling the business to mark it up and sell for a higher price to consumers.

Payment Voucher Status

This status shows where a suppliers payment request is in the payment disbursement process. 





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow


mediumfalse

Unless designated with greenlocationNA Specific or magentalocationEU Specific, process should be presumed as being globalized.



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type and PO Number. The ticket Description should be reviewed to idenitfy what the supplier needs support with. (Visual 1.1)


Visual 1.1:



Specialty Supplier Exceptions



Ashley Furniture Reports an Item is Returned

greenlocationNA Specific

mediuminfofalse

Ashley Furniture has faster insight into small parcel tracking and will report that an item has been returned to their warehouse, so a replacement is needed.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check tracking for PO/Item number  indicated by supplier

Utilize FedEx website to verify tracking information shows no movement or returned to sender



2

Issue Replacement via WIMS Wizard

Run the WIMS wizard against an item on the original PO utilizing the following Wizard Path:

  1. Select Eligible Item
  2. Indicate if CT has received item: No
  3. Select Reason of Customer Contact: Notified of a delay/issue
  4. Select Issue Diagnosis: Returned to Sender
  5. Continue
  6. Select Resolution: Replace the item with a brand-new one
  7. Confirm Shipping Address:  Use same address as original shipment
  8. Continue
  9. Submit Resolution

3

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Ashley Reports an Item is Returned template and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 




Ashley Furniture Reports an Item is Returned (Item is Delivered)

greenlocationNA Specific

mediuminfofalse

Ashley Furniture has faster insight into small parcel tracking and will report that an item has been returned to their warehouse but tracking shows it has been delivered to the customer.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check tracking for PO/Item number  indicated by supplier

Utilize FedEx website to check tracking information.  

If it shows as delivered, no replacement is necessary



2

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Ashley Reports Item Returned - Item Delivered template and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 




Emery Jensen Reports Item OOS

greenlocationNA Specific

mediuminfofalse

Emery Jensen’s internal dropship order software is unable to accommodate delaying POs in conjunction with Partner Home. If they report an item is OOS, we will assume the Fill/Kill designation and process for their team. Please note this setting *is not* noted in their Manage Supplier settings, but we will effectively treat them as if it is. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review order in ServiceHub

Open the order details to see when the order was placed.


2

Cancel Impacted PO(s)

Cancel the impacted PO(s):


Full PO Cancellation
  1. Enter the PO or OPID# into the Bulk Order Cancellation tool to cancel the replacement PO. 
  2. Select Reason for Cancellation: OUT_OF_STOCK_NO_RESTOCK_DATE
  3. Be sure to select email notifications for the supplier and customer so they are notified.


For Part Cancellation
  1. Run the Cancellation Wizard in ServiceHub and cancel the part(s) impacted. 
  2. Again, be sure to send a notification email to our customer. The supplier will be notified automatically via email.



3

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Supplier is Fill/Kill *Or Ashley Furniture template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution.




Serta/Simmons reports shipped PO undeliverable/returned to sender


mediuminfofalse

When Simmons/Serta is unable to deliver a PO due to the inability to contact/successfully schedule delivery for an order, they will assume refusal and ask for us to cancel the impacted POs. In other cases, customers cancel delivery ahead of the DOD, as well as refusal upon delivery attempts.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review ticket in ServiceHub

Open the supplier request, and take note of the high level reason for the cancellation. Common themes will include: 

  1. Delivery Scheduling Contact Failures
  2. Refusal of Delivery at DOD 
  3. Refusal/cancellation of delivery prior to DOD

2

Cancel Impacted PO(s)

Cancel Impacted PO(s) utilizing the following WIMS Wizard Path

  1. Select Eligible Item
    1. If delivered status is indicated: Select No to “Does the customer have the item?”
  2. Select Customer Contact Reason: Notified of Delay/Issue
    1. Select Issue Diagnosis Reason as appropriate: Failed/no delivery attempt or Returned to Sender
  3. Click Continue
  4. Select Resolution: Cancel Item(s)
  5. Select Cancellation Reason: Other
    1. REQUIRED: Please leave a note on the order clarifying the reason for cancellation (Visual 2 shows how this translates in the order notes).
  6. Click Toggle on Send Confirmation Email (do not send)
  7. Click Submit
  8. When the wizard wrap pops, click Do Not Wrap and Submit

Visual 2:

3

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Serta/Simmons Reports PO Undeliverable/RTS template, and close the ticket out under the Done status.




Stock Market Split Ship - Current Issue


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Issue Replacement

Issue the customer a replacement


2

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Supplier Reports Split Shipment template.




TBD - LG/GE/Electrolux

Tab to be filled in at a later date - waiting on info! 




General Order Inquiries



Commercial Use Products


Before You Start


mediuminfofalse

B2B (Business to Business) refers to any business orders that do not fall into the Platinum+ (or P+) category. 

  • B2B tickets are worked in the same way that all other tickets are within the project, with the exception of cancelling a PO due to data error
  • Platinum+ is a premium program with two tiers
    • $20,000-49,000 in spending for a rolling six months is flagged as P+ (P+ General)
    • $50,000 or higher in spending for a rolling six months qualifies for a dedicated Platinum+ Account manager (P+ Account Executive)

B2B or Plat+ Team members may reach out to agents directly with requests. If B2B is outreaching on an order, we would advise that we’ll respond to the suppliers ticket as soon as we can. 

If Platinum+ agents are reaching out asking for an update or exception on a ticket: 

  • Exceptions can be made with Manager approval. Create a group slack or have google meet with the P+ agent and your manager. 
  • When exceptions cannot be made troubleshoot with the P+ agent and your manager to find a resolution



mediuminfofalse

Supplier enters a ticket questioning the quantity on an order and if the product will be used commercially.  They may also advise that the product is not approved for commercial use and request that the customer sign a waiver.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check if the product is listed for commercial use

This can be found on the Wayfair website under Specifications Warranty section

If item is listed as ‘Commercial Use’ and should not be, the product listing will need updated via a Merch ticket/transfer and we will cancel the order


2

If product is not listed for ‘Commercial Use’ we need to verify if the customer is aware

Reach out to sales agent via Slack to confirm if discussion was had with customer.  If not, reach out to B2B agent listed for the account in the bannered order


3

Flag ticket to Platinum+ with internal note

If the platinum customer is not aware and a discussion needs to happen, please place the ticket in ‘Waiting for Supplier’ status until P+ team confirms that the customer accepts or wants to cancel


4

If customer confirms awareness, we ask supplier to proceed with shipping

If supplier advises that a waiver is necessary, we can transfer request to CSO 



5

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the appropriate template based on previous steps, and close the ticket under a Done resolution.

Template will vary





Mis-Shipped Item Retrieval


Supplier is outreaching reporting an item was shipped, but is in a cancelled state, incorrect item, sent to the wrong address, or was duplicated. Wayfair does not assist with mis shipped item retrieval.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review SU Request

Review SU Request


2

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Mis-Shipped Item Retrieval template.




Update Customer Review


Supplier reports that a customer left a review that they believe is misleading or incorrect and would like to have the review removed or corrected.  We do not update customer reviews but can advise the supplier to reach out to the Supplier Service Desk (SSD) if they believe that there is something egregiously wrong with the review.  If the supplier pushes back on the original response, please transfer to SSD.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review SU Request

Review SU Request


2

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Update Customer Review template.




Wrong Carrier Used


Supplier has shipped PO with a carrier/method different than requested


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Order

Review order


2

Confirm Shipping Information

Confirm shipping information supplier provided is valid


3

Update Shipping Info

Update shipping info


4

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Wrong Carrier Used template.




Combine POs


Supplier's enter this ticket when they have shipped 2 or more POs under the shipping information for only 1 PO and they are unable to update the second PO.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review SU Request

Review SU Request


2

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Supplier Requests to Combine POs template.




Incorrect Pricing


The wholesale price on the PO doesn’t match SU records; often due to missed promotions, inaccurate price updates, or upload errors.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Ticket

  1. The SU has requested cancellation of the PO due to incorrect pricing, as the listed price is lower than the correct wholesale cost they should receive.
  2.  Navigate to for next steps in reviewing and resolving SU queries.



PO Reroutes


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Ticket 

If the SU has requested a PO Reroute due to varied reasons, navigate to for next steps in reviewing and resolving SU queries. 






PO Data Issues



Part Number/Listing/Quantity is Incorrect


mediuminfo

Supplier is notifying that the Part Number, Listing, Quantity, or Product Description is Incorrect, and they need a new PO transmitted with the correct information

Once a PO is transmitted, we are unable to change any of it’s previously sent information


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Cancel the PO via Bulk Order Cancellation 

Enter the PO or OPID# into the Bulk Order Cancellation tool to cancel the PO. 

Note: Be sure to select email notification for the supplier and customer so they are notified. 


2

Refresh Cancellation Details

Refresh the cancellation results to confirm the PO has been cancelled successfully. 


3

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Part#/Listing/Qty Incorrect template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 




PO Data Error - B2B Order


mediuminfo

Supplier is notifying that the Part Number, Listing, Quantity, or Product Description is Incorrect with relation to a B2B order, and they need a new PO transmitted with the correct information

Once a PO is transmitted, we are unable to change any of it’s previously sent information


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Message B2B Agent to Notify of PO Error 

Outreach the assigned B2B agent/BAM on the order in Slack to notify them that the PO cannot be fulfilled as is, and requires cancellation.


2

Confirm B2B Acknowledgement

Please wait for B2B response and acknowledgement of cancellation

Note: If no response from B2B within 24 natural hours (except for outreach sent on Friday) please proceed to step 3.


3

Cancel Original PO

Enter the PO or OPID# into the Bulk Order Cancellation tool to cancel the replacement PO. 

Note: Be sure to select email notifications for the supplier and customer so they are notified.


4

Refresh Cancellation Details

Refresh the cancellation results to confirm the PO has been cancelled successfully. 



5Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Original B2B Order template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 




Replacement PO Error


mediuminfo

Supplier is notifying us that the data/information on a Replacement PO is incorrect - most often, this applies to quantities for replacement units orders against an original PO that was in a set (i.e. Set of 2: Dining Chairs) and therefor breaks the SU’s MOQ.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Request
  • If the supplier can fill as parts, follow the process for MOQ - Can Fill As Parts.
  • If the supplier cannot fill as parts, steps 2-4 will apply.

2

Cancel the PO via Bulk Order Cancellation

Enter the PO or OPID# into the Bulk Order Cancellation tool to cancel the PO. 

Note: Be sure to select email notification for the supplier and customer so they are notified.


3

Refresh Cancellation Details

Refresh the cancellation results to confirm the PO has been cancelled successfully. 


4

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Replacement PO template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 





MOQ - Can Fill as Parts


mediuminfo

Supplier has notified that a replacement unit does not meet their minimum order quantity, but that they can fulfill the request if it is submitted as replacement parts


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Cancel the Replacement PO

Enter the PO or OPID# into the Bulk Order Cancellation tool to cancel the replacement PO. 

Note: Be sure to select email notification for the supplier and customer so they are notified.


2

Refresh Cancellation Details

Refresh the cancellation results to confirm the PO has been cancelled successfully. 



3

Run WIMS Wizard to submit parts request on the original PO

Run the WIMS Wizard against an item on the original PO utilizing the following Wizard Path:

  1. Select Eligible Item
  2. Indicate if CT has received item: Select Yes or No as applicable
  3. Select Reason of Customer Contact: Notified of a delay/issue
  4. Select Issue Diagnosis: Damaged In Transit
  5. Continue
  6. Select Resolution: Get replacement parts for free!
  7. Continue
  8. Enter Item Parts

4

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the MOQ - Can Fill As Parts template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 






Returns



Before You Start



Identifying Wholesale Cost (WSC)


mediuminfo

When suppliers report receiving the wrong item returned, we’ll need to identify its wholesale cost in order to determine what to advise the supplier to do with the product, before we connect them with Finance Partners to address the return credits deducted.


CG returns follow dropship return settings resulting in CG C2S cannot support return questions as CG does not take returns. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Identify the Item

Utilize any information possible your supplier provides to locate the order. This can include: 

  • Tracking IDs
  • PO numbers
  • Item Descriptions
  • Customer names/PII

2

Click on the PO ServiceHub

When you click on the PO it will open a new window with the PO information, including the wholesale unit cost (Visual 2.1)

Visual 2.1:



Identifying Payment Voucher Status


mediuminfo

When suppliers report receiving the wrong item returned, we’ll need to identify whether the Return is pre-voucher or post-voucher to determine which Finance Partners to connect the supplier with to address their request. 

  • Paid invoices indicate the item is post-voucher
  • Unpaid or pending invoices indicate the item is pre-voucher


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Finance View in the Legacy Order View

Search the order ID in the legacy order search tool. When the order is opened, click on the Finance View tab in the upper middle of the order view 


2

Review the item under Wayfair Costs

Scroll down to the Wayfair Costs Section, and look for the PO and OPID of the item returned (you can use control F to reverse search this information as well). In the Description Column, the item should reflect “Return” 


3

Identify Payment (PMT) Status

When reviewing items in the finance view, review the column titled “PMT Status” - this section will indicate one of the following statuses: 

  • Paid: (Date) 
  • N/A
  • Pending Voucher

Visual 3.1:

4

Identify Finance Partner Team Support based on Voucher Status

Paid invoices indicate the item is post voucher, i.e. anything that reflects “Paid:(Date)” status, will be transferred to the Supplier Claims team (CR project in SupportHub) 

Unpaid/Pending invoices indicate the item is pre-voucher, i.e. anything that reflects “N/A” or “Pending Voucher” status, will be transferred to the Finance Operations team (FINSAP project in SupportHub)

Please note: If you cannot find a Return line item related to the PO, this also goes to FINSAP






Small Parcel


#7B189Fwhite1#7B189F




Item is LESS than $50 WSC

The item the SU has received is less than $50 WSC, so we will advise them to donate/dispose of the product, and initiate a transfer to Finance to process a return dispute since the SU didn’t receive their product back. 




Item is MORE than $50 WSC

The item the supplier has received is more than $50 WSC, so we will advise them ship the product to our warehouse in Kentucky to retrieve the product for Wayfair, and initiate a transfer to Finance to process a return dispute since the SU didn’t receive their product back. 





#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Identify the Return Voucher Status

Identify the Return Voucher status utilizing the Finance View in the Legacy Order View


2

Send Template


IF: Item is LESS than $50 WSC

Respond to the supplier utilizing the SP Return WSC <$/€/£50  template, then leave the ticket in Waiting for Support status.

  • Make sure you update the template with the correct team name based on the voucher status. 


IF: Item is MORE than $50 WSC

Respond to the supplier utilizing the SP Return WSC >$/€/£50 template, then leave the ticket in Waiting for Support status.

  • Make sure you update the template with the correct team name based on the voucher status. 



3

Transfer to the Appropriate Finance Group


IF: Pre-Voucher

If the return is pre-voucher, transfer the ticket to Finance Operations (FINSAP Project in SupportHub) 


IF: Post Voucher

If the return is post-voucher, transfer the ticket to Supplier Claims (CR Project in SupportHub)






Large Parcel


#7B189Fwhite1#7B189F




Item is LESS than $500 WSC

The item the SU has received is less than $500 WSC, so we will advise them to donate/dispose of the product, and initiate a transfer to Finance to process a return dispute since the SU didn’t receive their product back. 




Item is MORE than $500 WSC

The item the SU has received is more than $500 WSC, we will advise them ship the product to our warehouse in Kentucky to retrieve the product for Wayfair, and initiate a transfer to Finance to process a return dispute since the SU didn’t receive their product back.





#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Identify the Return Voucher Status

Identify the Return Voucher status utilizing the Finance View in the Legacy Order View


2

Send Template


IF: Item is LESS than $500 WSC

Respond to the supplier utilizing the LP Return WSC <$/€/£500 template, then leave the ticket in Waiting for Support status.

  • Make sure you update the template with the correct team name based on the voucher status. 


IF: Item is MORE than $500 WSC

Respond to the supplier utilizing the LP Return WSC >$/€/£500 template, then leave the ticket in Waiting for Support status.

  • Make sure you update the template with the correct team name based on the voucher status. 



3

Transfer to the Appropriate Finance Group


IF: Pre-Voucher

If the return is pre-voucher, transfer the ticket to Finance Operations (FINSAP Project in SupportHub) 


IF: Post Voucher

If the return is post-voucher, transfer the ticket to Supplier Claims (CR Project in SupportHub)






Large Parcel Return Status


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Provide LP Return Update

When providing a large parcel return update, agents should let the supplier know that they will be contacted when the item is available to return. If they have not been contacted to arrange for shipment back after 45 days, only then should they submit a request to escalate.




Item is not a Wayfair Item


mediuminfo

A supplier has received the wrong item under the return tracking we provided, but the item is not sold by Wayfair. Similarly to other return questions, this typically happens when a customer is returning multiple items and uses the wrong labels.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Identify the Return Voucher Status

Identify the Return Voucher status utilizing the Finance View in the Legacy Order View


2

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Return is not a Wayfair Item template, then leave the ticket in Waiting for Support status.

  • Make sure you update the template with the correct team name based on the voucher status. 


3

Transfer to the Appropriate Finance Group


IF: Pre-Voucher

If the return is pre-voucher, transfer the ticket to Finance Operations (FINSAP Project in SupportHub) 


IF: Post Voucher

If the return is post-voucher, transfer the ticket to Supplier Claims (CR Project in SupportHub)





Supplier Reports Item was Refused/Returned by Carrier


mediuminfo

A supplier is reporting that an item has been returned to them by a carrier directly to their warehouse; this can indicate that the item was either undeliverable, or refused/returned by the customer. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Order Trail

Check if the customer contacted service to state that they did not need/want the item


IF: Customer Elected to Return Item


mediumtipfalse
  • Send Template: Carrier Returned - No Replacement, Normal Return



IF: Customer Did not Elect Return


mediumtipfalse
  • Issue Replacement PO
  • Send Template: Item Returned by Carrier - Replacement Needed




2

Close Ticket under Done Status

Close the ticket out under a Done status




Return Reason Inquiry


mediuminfo

A supplier has received a notice that an item is being returned, but would like to know specifically why. 

We do not investigate the reason for returns, and we will educate the supplier on what to expect from the return process.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Respond utilizing the Querying Reason for Return (Return_Reason) template, and close out the ticket under a Done status. 




Return Email Query


When the Customer creates a return, an automated email will be sent to notify the Supplier. This will appear to them as below. (Visual 1.1) The Supplier does not need to take any action in response to this email.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

If the Supplier responds to this looking for next steps, use the Return_Email template to update them. 

Visual 1.1:

2

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




Disputed Returns


mediuminfo

A supplier has received an item that is damaged or in an unsellable condition, and disputing a return credit that was deducted. We will connect our SU with Finance partners in order to resolve their concerns


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Dispute Return template, then leave the ticket in Waiting for Support status. 


2

Transfer Ticket to Finance Operations (FINSAP)
  • In ServiceHub, select Transfer
  • Select New Project: FINSAP
  • Select New Issue Type:  Partner Home External Request
  • New Status (if asked):  Waiting for Support
  • Customer Request Type: Requests from Partner Home
  • Select Move
  • The ticket becomes a FINSAP-##### ticket, and you are done



Return Settings Clarification


mediuminfo

A supplier is reaching out inquiring about why an item was returned to Wayfair rather than the supplier directly. This is typically because they received an RMA alert. We will review their return settings in Partner Home to determine their set up and provide expectations


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Log In as the Supplier
  1. Open the Partner Home portal.
  2. Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.1)


Visual 1.1:

2

Confirm Supplier's Return Setup
  1. Select the person icon in the top right-hand corner to open the dropdown menu.
  2. Click Returns Management. (Visual 1.2)
  3. A new window will open showing the available options and which the Supplier has selected. (Visual 1.3; Tip 1)


Tip 1:

The Supplier also has the option here to change their selection or update their return address. (Visual 1.3)

In Returns Management, you will see 3 options: Supplier-Managed Returns, Wayfair-Managed Returns (All Parcels), and Wayfair-Managed Returns (Large Parcels Only)

  • Make note of the section with the purple box that notates Selected.
  • Next to the Selected box, it will also notate which use selected this setting, and the date the setting was activated


Visual 1.2:

Visual 1.3:

3

Send Template

Update the Supplier using the Supplier Managed Returns (SMR) or Wayfair Managed Returns (WMR) template. 


4

Update Ticket Status

Close the ticket out under a Done status.




Received Item Back in Warehouse in Error


This section outlines how to assist the Supplier when they are querying why they have received an item back in their warehouse. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm Supplier's Return Setup

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (See Visual 1.1)

Select the person icon in the top right-hand corner to open the dropdown menu. 

Click Returns Management. (See Visual 1.2)

A new window will open showing the available options and which the Supplier has selected. (See Visual 1.3; See Tip 1)

Tip 1:

Replacement PO/Parts Investigation



Before You Start


largeinfo

Enrollment in SCT programs only determine post order resolution paths and execution. As such, C2S teams should still provide support regarding fulfillment questions or practices on pre-shipment POs.



Adding "RP" Component to Tickets


When a ticket from the AOPS or POS project is identified as being related to replacement parts, a replacement part (RP) component needs to be added in ServiceHub.

We’re currently seeing several RP-related tickets appearing under various ticket categories due to:

 • Technical issues

 • Service Unit (SU) education opportunities

 • System behavior that still allows users to raise RP tickets via the “Submit a Ticket” section

A project is underway to restrict POs with “-R-” from being submitted into AOPS and POS. The ETA for this change is still unconfirmed.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Determine if Ticket is Related to RP Topic

Replacement parts are on order and SU is reaching out in query to these. We’d like to gauge how many RP tickets are coming through AOPS/POS, as not all tickets are clearly labeled with “-R-” when raised. 


If the ticket IS related to RP:

  1. Navigate to components section of ServiceHub 
  2. Click on the Pencil icon
  3. Insert “RP”
  4. Select "RP" when it appears. 
  5. Click on the ✓ symbol.  (Visual 2.1)

Visual 2.1:

250

2

Continue Working the Ticket

The task of adding the tracking component has been completed.  Next, support the supplier with their query, using additional SOPs to fully resolve the ticket. 




Confirmation of Charges

RPOps does not approve replacement part charges. Replacement parts are not covered in Damage & Defect allowances and should ship at the Supplier's expense.

The only exception is when replacement parts are ordered because part of a multi-box shipment is Lost in Transit. The Finance team has a process for handling these charges, so tickets should not be opened in these scenarios.  

Suppliers should not submit tickets to request charge approvals. If you receive a ticket for this, educate the Supplier of the correct process using the RP_Charges template. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.

1256143448.title: Product Out of Stock SOP (DSC2S) - VCN
1256143448.author: Former user (Deleted)
1256143448.body:
Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN 

Last Updated:  




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Product Out of Stock. 

These tickets are entered by the Supplier to report if:

  • a product is out of stock and a PO needs to be backordered
  • a product is discontinued and a PO needs to be cancelled

For each of the issues above, the Supplier is not required to enter a ticket. Instead, they should delay the order through Partner Home. It is important to educate the Supplier on the correct process for the future. Educational elements are included in the templates highlighted throughout this SOP.

The Supplier may also enter these tickets if they have tried to self-service but are unable to through Partner Home. This could be due to system issues or the order having an incorrect status. 


Overview

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Product Out of Stock. 

These tickets are entered by the Supplier to report if:

  • a product is out of stock and a PO needs to be backordered
  • a product is discontinued and a PO needs to be cancelled
  • part of a multi-item PO is out of stock and the Supplier wants to fulfil the order from an alternative warehouse

These types of requests may also be entered under the Problem with an Order Request Type on the AOPS or POS projects, or as Something Else on the POS project. 

The Supplier is not required to enter a ticket to backorder or discontinue a PO. Instead, they should delay the order through Partner Home.

It is important to educate the Supplier on the correct process for the future. Educational elements are included in the templates highlighted throughout this SOP.

Education is not required if the Supplier was unable to self-service the PO due to a system issue. 

The Supplier may also enter these tickets if they have tried to self-service but are unable to through Partner Home. This could be due to the order having an incorrect status, due to Supplier error or system issues. 

EU Ticket Example (POS Project)

You can see the Customer Request Type and the PO Number that is out of stock:


NA Ticket Example (AOPS Project)

You can see the Customer Request Type and the PO Number that is out of stock:




Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool

Use Case

Bulk Cancellation ToolThis tool is used for cancelling PO numbers outside of using the Cancellation wizard 
NA: PO History This tool is used for tracking Large Parcel shipments.
EU: Validating Tracking for Orders SOPThis SOP explains how to validating the tracking information for our carriers in the EU.
Dropship OrdersThis is a Supplier-facing tool used to view PO details. The Supplier can also confirm, delay or cancel orders here. 
Re-Indexing ToolThis tool is used to Re-Index an order when the Fulfillment status is not reflecting the correct information for a Supplier. It essentially refreshes the data so the PO is showing the most accurate information.

Key Word

Definition

If the item is discontinued, this should be noted here. 

Select the Expected Dispatch Date and click Delay Orders. (Tip 1)

Tip 2:

>Select Eligible Item

>Select Issue Diagnosis: Incomplete Shipment

>Continue

>Select Resolution: Get a full refund

>Select Refund Method: Original Payment Method

>Continue

>Submit Resolution


3

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the  template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 

Please note: If the POs tracking ID does reflect movement, please send the “Discontinued Item: Shipment Clarification” Template first to confirm with the SU if the item is still on dock.





Backorder Unavailable: PO Marked Shipped


A supplier is reporting that a PO has come through and needs to be backordered due to stock, however, they are unable to process the order delay because the PO is marked as shipped


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm order status

Open the order details to confirm the status of the PO

Please note: The PO is often marked shipped in these cases is due to an ASN transmission, or a supplier prematurely uploading Tracking Information into Order Alerts, or Dropship Orders in PartnerHome.



2

Confirm No Movement on Tracking


If SP:

Review the tracking ID on FedEx’s website.


If LTL:

Review PO History to confirm no movement through network



3

IF: No Movement on Tracking

Remove Shipped Status

magentalocationEU Specific

From the Product tab, select Edit Shipping/Destination Info. (Visual 3.1)

This will open a new tab with the shipping details. 

Change the Status to Pending Ship Confirmation. This will remove the ship date. 

Click remove beside the tracking number (if applicable). 

Select Update. (Visual 3.2)

Move to the Delay Order tab. 


We can only remove the shipped status if the status in Partner Home is In Transit (Awaiting Carrier Scan). If the status is just In Transit, follow NA process to run WIMS.


Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

4

Run WIMS Wizard

Run the WIMS wizard against an item on the original PO utilizing the following Wizard Path:

  1. Select Eligible Item
  2. Indicate is CT has received item (No)
  3. Select Reason of Customer Contact: Notified of a delay/issue
  4. Select Issue Diagnosis: Incomplete Shipment
  5. Continue
  6. Select Resolution: Replace the item with a brand-new one.
  7. Continue
  8. Submit Resolution
  9. Wait up to 15 minutes for the new PO to generate (it is recommended you work other tickets while this generates). 

5

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution.




PO Reroutes


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Ticket 

If the SU has requested a PO Reroute due to varied reasons, navigate to for next steps in reviewing and resolving SU queries. 




Resolve Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Use the relevant template to update the supplier of the action taken. 

If the Supplier was unable to backorder themselves and you took action for them use: 

  • if the order status was incorrect due to Supplier action
  • if the order status was incorrect due to a technical error

Templates for each scenario can be found

Ensure you make any necessary edits to the templates so these are tailored to the Supplier's specific query. 



2

Edit Comment (Optional)

If you need to make any edits to your comment after this is added, click the pencil icon. (Visual 2.1)

Visual 2.1:

3

Update Status

Select Change Status > Done. (Visual 3.1)

Visual 3.1:
