#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Shipping/Carrier Questions. 

Suppliers enter these tickets for a variety of reasons, including: 

  • Reporting a carrier pickup delay 
  • Request to change carrier 
  • Request to change order status
  • They can't ship to the customer's postcode
  • Querying how to register a pallet for a Freight Network (FN) order
  • Querying the final mile carrier for a FN order
  • Querying the collection process
  • Querying a Return to Sender (RTS)
  • Requesting Proof of Delivery (POD) (specific to Polish Suppliers)

Ensure you are educating the Supplier on the correct ticket process if they enter this request type for the following reasons: 

  • Reporting a carrier pickup delay (Correct process: Carrier did not Come on Time for Pickup / Missed Pickup)
  • Request to change carrier (Correct process: Change Delivery Method or Carrier on PO Due to Item Count/Size)
  • Request to change order status (Correct process: Problem with an Order / Other > Update Tracking Number/Order Status)

Please note you must also resolve the issue for the Supplier in these cases.


Overview

Add text here.




Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool (Linked)

Use Case











Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition













#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type and PO Number. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:


2

Review the Description

Review the Supplier's query in the ticket Description. Move to the relevant tab to assist them with the resolution. 




Carrier Pickup Delay

true


Change Carrier

true


Change Order Status

true


Can't Ship to Postcode


This section outlines how to action a ticket where the Supplier advises they are unable to deliver to the customer's postcode. 



Review Logistics Matrix


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Click Transportation Logistics

From the Supplier tab, click on Transportation Logistics. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Review Delivery Section

Based on the order's destination, view the section for delivery to the relevant country. 


3

Review Carriers Column

Identify if the Supplier ships on a Wayfair account, which will show a specific Carrier name under Carriers: (Visual 3.1)

Or if they ship on their own account, which will appear as shown below: (Visual 3.2)

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

4

Review Relevant Tab

Depending on how the supplier is shipping, review the relevant tab: Supplier Shipping on Wayfair Account or Supplier Shipping on Own Account.




Supplier Shipping on Wayfair Account


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Use the Postcode_Query template to advise the Supplier of the next steps. 

You can find links to Help Center articles explaining the collection process for each carrier here. 



2

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




Supplier Shipping on Own Account


This section outlines how to assist the Supplier when they ship on their own account. 


purplewhitepurpleTraining Resources

turquoisetrueblockinfoPallet Management Slideshttps://drive.google.com/file/d/1kYx_7sDLulTcAz5l2WKUVhsvl6ASNokY/view?usp=drive_link

orangetrueblocknewsTraining Recordinghttps://drive.google.com/file/d/1GFfwYucdHlmUXSN16UPoa1HWVWtkWWeM/view


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Access the Bulk Cancellation Tool
  1. Stay on the “Initiate Cancellation” tab and choose “Purchase order numbers”
  2. Enter the PO number(s)
  3. Choose reason from the drop down menu
  4. Mark “Yes” for the cancellation has been confirmed mark the checkboxes next to both “Send Customer Email” and “Send Supplier Email”
  5. Click the “Initiate Cancellations” button.
    1. This will run the cancellation and take you to the “Cancellation Results” tab where the request will show as “Queued”
  6.  After a few minutes, if you refresh the page the status will update to show the order as “Canceled”

2

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utiziling the Needs PO Cancelled template.






FN Pallet Registration Query


In order to register a pallet, the pick-up date and pallet dimensions are required. 

Based on this, it usually makes sense for the Supplier to register this themselves. 

However, we can support with printing pallet labels if this information has been provided. 



Reviewing and Registering Pallet


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm if Pallet is Registered
  1. From Dropship Orders, select the Pallet Shipments tab.
    1. Any pallets that have already been registered will appear here. 
    2. You can filter using the PO number, collection date or destination. (Visual 1.1)
  2. If the Supplier has managed to successfully register their pallet since entering the ticket, you can advise them that this has been scheduled.  
  3. If the Supplier is requesting that edits are made to the pallet, this can be done up until 1:30 pm CET one business day before collection.

Visual 1:

2

Editing a Pallet
  1. Select the Edit icon to modify, or the Delete icon to remove. (Visual 2)
  2. Modify pallet dimensions, weight or included POs as required. 
  3. Once the changes are completed, select Save. 

Visual 2:

3

Register a Pallet

The steps for registering the pallet is outlined in the Help Centre articles here: https://partners.wayfair.com/help/2/article/2680

This can be shared with the Supplier to support them in resolving their issue.

If you have the pallet dimensions and pick-up date, you can also follow the steps in these articles to print the pallet label for the Supplier. 

If you do not have the pallet dimensions, you can use the FN_Request_Dims template to request these from the Supplier. 

Ensure to attach the pallet label to the ticket in cases where this is being provided. 

Use the FN_Register_Pallet template to advise the Supplier of the registration process.

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 




Registration Errors


The Supplier may experience an error preventing them from generating their pallet labels. 
Move to the relevant tab to troubleshoot the error. 



After Cut-Off Time


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Request

If the Supplier tries to register their pallet after the cut-off time, they will see the below error message. (Visual 1)

The cut-off time for registering a pallet is 1:30pm CET, one business day before the collection date.


Visual 1:

2

Send Template

Use the FN_Register_Pallet template to advise the Supplier of the correct registration process.

Ensure to edit the template if the label is not being provided. 



3

Resolve the Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 




Registration Button Not Visible


This section explains how to support Supplier's in cases where the Pallet Shipments/Pallet Registration button is missing.

This is generally due to the incorrect settings in Manage Supplier. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check Logistics Matrix settings

From the Supplier tab, select Transportation Logistics. (See Visual 1.1)

This will open the Logistics tab of Manage Supplier. 

Confirm if Automated Pallet Transmission and Uses New Pallet UI are ticked. (See Visual 1.2)

If either of these boxes are unchecked, move to the next step. 

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Enter a Label Failure ticket

Navigate to the Label Failure ticket entry form. 

Follow the ticket entry form to provide all the necessary information and add a screenshot showing the unchecked box. 

Include that this is a known tech issue.

Link the tickets, by expanding the Linked Tickets dropdown and selecting Add. (See Visual 2.1)

Enter the Issue ID for the ticket and select Validate.

Change the Relationship to Relates to and select Link Ticket. (See Visual 2.1)


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Update Status

Select Change Status > Pending to await an update from the Shipping Solutions Team. (Visual 3)

Once they have confirmed the Logistics Matrix is updated, use the FN_Pallet_Error template to advise the Supplier. 

If so, move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Visual 3:



Error Message Printing Label


This section explains how to support Supplier's who experience the below error message when attempting to print the pallet label:


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check for Inactive Warehouses

Navigate to Manage Supplier.

Search in the Jump to Supplier ID field using the Supplier's SUID. 

Check in the Jump to Warehouse dropdown to confirm if any of the Supplier's child warehouses are set to Inactive. (Visual 1; Tip 1) 

Tip 1:

There is currently a known tech issue where an inactive child warehouse may prevent the Supplier from printing pallet labels. 


Visual 1:

2

Enter a Label Failure ticket

Navigate to the Label Failure ticket entry form. 

Follow the ticket entry form to provide all the necessary information and add a screenshot showing the inactive warehouse. (See Visual 2.1)

Include that this is a known tech issue.

Link the tickets, by expanding the Linked Tickets dropdown and selecting Add. (See Visual 2.2)

Enter the Issue ID for the ticket and select Validate.

Change the Relationship to Relates to and select Link Ticket. (See Visual 2.3)


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

3

Update Status

Select Change Status > Pending to await an update from the Shipping Solutions Team. 

Once they have confirmed the SUID is updated, check with the Supplier to see if the issue is resolved. 

If so, move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

If the issue persists, escalate for In House Support. 

Visual 3.1:

4

Enter Tech ticket


This step applies to In House agents only.

Navigate to the Label Printing / Manifesting Issues ticket entry form. (See Tip 2)

Complete the entry form as per the guidance in Visual 4.1.

Add the error screenshot to the ticket. 

Once entered, add the link to the tech ticket as an internal comment in your POS ticket. 

Use the Ongoing_Tech_Issue template to update the supplier of the next steps. (See Tip 3)

Select Change Status > Pending to await an update from the Tech team. 

Once they have confirmed the SUID is updated, check with the Supplier to see if the issue is resolved. 

If so, move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Tip 2:

This tech team deal with more complex label printing errors. 


Tip 3:

Ensure you make any necessary edits to the template so this is tailored to the Supplier's specific query. 


Visual 4.1:







FN Final Mile Query



Review Logistics Matrix


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Click Transportation Logistics

From the Supplier tab, click on Transportation Logistics. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Review Delivery Section

Based on the order's destination, view the section for delivery to the relevant country. 

Some countries will have their own specific setup, which you can view by selecting the Plus symbol at the end of the country tab. (Visual 2.2)

Other countries will follow the default settings.

From the Product tab, you can confirm the destination country from the Customer's Shipping address. (Visual 2.1)


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Review Carriers Column

The First Mile carrier will be shown under the Carrier heading, and you can see which cross dock facility the POs will be passing through in Overpack Settings: (Visual 3.1)

Some Polish Suppliers may also inject directly into a Hermes Einrichtungs Service hub for them to complete the first mile delivery. This will appear as below: (Visual 3.2)

Wayfair Lich in our cross dock facility in Germany.

Wayfair Mallow is our cross dock facility in the UK.


Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:




Check Final Mile Carrier


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm Final Mile Carrier

Review the table below to confirm who the Final Mile carrier will be based on the ship class, and the origin and destination countries:

Final Mile Carriers


Origin
Destination
Supplier WarehouseFirst Mile (Collection)Cross Dock FacilityUK: Final MileDE: Final MileIE: Final Mile

Supplier Injection

Hermes Hub (Mosina / Anton Roehr)LP: BJS / AIT Home Delivery / WDN / XDPLP: Hermes EinrichtungLP: WDN




2

IF: SU Queries Multiple Labels
  1. If the Supplier is querying why there is more than one label per carton, use the XDP/WDN_Labels template to advise on the process.
  2. Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

3

IF: PO has more than one option for carrier
  1. In cases where the PO has more than one option for which carrier it will be assigned to, check in the Transportation Event History tool to confirm if the Supplier has already printed the label.
  2. Search using the PO number and click Submit.
  3. If the label has been generated, this will show under the Label Print Information, and you will be able to identify the final mile carrier from the LabelService (ID). (Visual 3.1)
  4. If available download the label by selecting the hyperlink under the Label Path. Save with the PO Number as the file title. (Visual 3.2)
  5. Attach to the ticket for the Supplier's reference. (Visual 3.3) Move to the Resolve Ticket tab to update the Supplier.
  6. If the label is not available, move to the Print Label tab to generate this. 

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

Visual 3.3:



Print Label


This section explains how to print the label for the Supplier. 

If multiple carrier options were available in the table outlined in the previous tab, you will be able to confirm the correct Final Mile carrier by printing the label. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Refresh the PO

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (See Visual 1.1)

Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. 

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. 

Click More Actions > Refresh Orders. (See Visual 1.2)



Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Generate the Shipping Docs

Select the check box beside the PO. 

Click Print Documents. (See Visual 2.1)

This will open a new tab. 

Confirm the following: (See Visual 2.2) 

  • All documents selected to print
  • Warehouse (See Tip 1)
  • Pickup Date
  • Confirm Labels quantity (See Tip 2)

Select Generate Documents. (See Visual 2.2) 

Close the window. 

Tip 1:

This is only required for Suppliers who ship out of multiple warehouses. 


Tip 2:

If the Supplier advises a different amount of labels are required, select Edit Shipping Labels. (Visual 2.2)

Input the amount of labels required in Piece Count. 

Divide the Total Weight by the Piece Count to get the Unit Weight.

Select Done Editing. (Visual 2.3) 


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

3

Save the labels

The Fulfilment Status should now appear as Awaiting Pickup. (Visual 3.1)

A green pop-up will appear at the bottom of the screen. (Visual 3.2)

Select Download Documents to save the labels

These should be saved using the PO as the title for the file. 

Attach to your ticket for the Supplier's reference. (Visual 3.3)

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab to update the Supplier. 

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

Visual 3.3:





Collection Process Query


This section outlines how to resolve tickets where the Supplier is querying how their orders will be collected. 



Confirm Assigned Carrier


This section outlines how to confirm which carrier has been assigned to collect a PO. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Log into Partner Home

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1)

Visual 1:

2

Locate relevant PO

Navigate to Customer Orders > Dropship Orders. (See Visual 2.1)

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. 

The Carrier and Ship Class can be confirmed here. (See Visual 2.2)

If the carrier is Evri, GLS Spain or AIT Home Delivery (previously called Panther), move to the Confirm Carrier Setup tab. 

If the Supplier is querying a Freight Network collection, move to the FN Pallet Registration Query or FN Final Mile Query tab. 

For all other carriers, move to the Update Supplier tab. 

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:



Confirm Collection Setup


This section outlines how to identify the collection setup for the Supplier. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm collection setup

Navigate to the EU Logistics Ops: Supplier Collections Set-Ups sheet. 

Move to the relevant carrier tab.

Use CTRL + F to search for the SUID. (Visual 1)

Check the Suppliers Collection Frequency in Column I.

This will identify if the Supplier is set up for daily or ad-hoc collections. 

If the Supplier is not listed on the sheet, you can assume they are set up for ad-hoc collections. 

Move to the Update Supplier tab. 

 

Visual 1:



Update Supplier


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Advise the Supplier of their specific collection process using the DHLPaket/DPD_Collection, GLS/UPS_Collection, UK_SP_Collection or LP_Collection template. 

Ensure to only include the relevant carrier and collection setup information as identified in the previous tabs. These templates should not be shared with the Supplier without making any edits. 


SP/LP Carrier Breakdown


UK Small Parcel (SP)EU Small Parcel (SP)Large Parcel (LP)

DHL Parcel UK

DHL Paket

AIT Home Delivery (previously called Panther)

Evri

DPD

WDN

UPS

GLS

XDP


UPS

XPO



If the Supplier's query is in relation to a direct injection to the carrier (eg. DHL Home Delivery or Hermes Einrichtungs), you can find the relevant Help Centre article to support them here. 



2

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.






RTS Query


This section outlines how to assist the Supplier when an item has been Returned to Sender (RTS) while in transit. 

This may occur due to issues with customs documentation or if the customer refuses, or misses delivery.  


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check for Return Information

From the Order tab, review the notes in the Order Trail to see if there is any information regarding why the item is being returned. (See Visual 1.1)

Open the tracking information by clicking on the tracking hyperlink in the Product tab. (See Visual 1.2)

Review the tracking to see if any additional information is provided on why the item was RTS while in transit. (See Visual 1.3; See Tip 1)

Possible RTS Reasons
  • Inadequate or missing data (Usually indicates an issue with customs paperwork)
  • Damaged in Transit
  • Customer Refused Delivery

If the Order Trail or tracking information explain why the item was RTS, you may advise the Supplier of this if they ask. 

There may be no extra information on the tracking showing why the item was returned. In this case we do not do any further investigation to find out for the Supplier. 

Tip 1:

For additional guidance reviewing tracking information, please see this article: Validating Tracking for Orders.


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

Visual 1.3:

2

Confirm if Replacement/Refund Processed

If the item was RTS in transit, the end customer may not yet be aware of this. A replacement or refund may be required to resolve the issue for them. 

From the Product tab, review the Service History to confirm if either has been processed. (See Visual 2.1)

If a resolution has already been processed, move to .

Enter a Customer Information Needed ticket to Customer Service to process a resolution for the Customer. (See Visual 2.2; See Tip 2)

Link the tickets, by expanding the Linked Tickets dropdown and selecting Add. (See Visual 2.3)

Enter the Issue ID for the CSO ticket and select Validate.

Change the Relationship to Relates to and select Link Ticket (See Visual 2.4)

Tip 2: 

From the Order tab, check if the B2B banner is visible. (See Visual 2.3) 

If the banner is visible, enter a B2B Support > Service Inquiry ticket, instead of a Customer Information Needed ticket. (See Visual 2.2) 


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

Visual 2.4:

3

Update the Supplier

Update the Supplier using the RTS_Resolution_Needed/RTS_Resolution_Actioned template. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




POD Query (VAT Report Request)


This section outlines how to assist a Polish Supplier who is requesting a proof of delivery (POD) for a PO. 

This may occur due to specific tax requirements within Poland.  

The Supplier's query may include the following terms:

  • Proof of Delivery / POD
  • VAT report
  • 0% VAT

To resolve this, we can guide the Supplier to download their VAT Report from Partner Home to fulfil any tax requirements. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm Supplier Based in Poland

From the Supplier tab, review the Warehouse Address to confirm this is based in Poland. (Visual 1, See Tip 1)

Tip 1:

If the warehouse is not within Poland, the Supplier's request is likely unrelated to the VAT Report.

Instead check to see if the Supplier is reporting the incorrect fulfillment status on Partner Home or querying their tracking information as per the steps in this SOP.

If you are unsure how to resolve the Supplier's request, follow the escalation process


Visual 1:

2

Update the Supplier

Update the Supplier using the POD/VAT_Report_Request template. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




Resolve Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Send Template

Review your comment to the Supplier advising them of the action taken. 

Ensure you make any necessary edits to the templates so these are tailored to the Supplier's specific query. 



2

Edit Comment (Optional)

If you need to make any edits to your comment after this is added, click the pencil icon. (Visual 2)

Visual 2:

3

Update Status

Select Change Status > Done. (Visual 3)

Visual 3:

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

Add text here.



Overview

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Split PO. 

These tickets are entered by the Customer Service team and they should provide all of the information required for you to action the ticket using the template outlined below: 

  • Name of supplier contact who approved update: 
  • Item Number(s) to be split: 
  • Quantity of items to be split: 
  • If the above items need to be routed to a new warehouse, which warehouse? If blank, no rerouting is necessary. Warehouse: 
  • Description:

These tickets can also be submitted by internal partners that work directly with suppliers to request for POs to be resent.



Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool (Linked)

Use Case










Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition













#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Review Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant for your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type and the PO Number. The template completed by the Customer Service team will appear in the Description. (Visual 1.1)

Review the Description to determine the next steps:

  • If the change was approved by the Supplier, move to the Split PO Confirmed With Supplier tab. 
  • If the template was not completed correctly with the information required to split the PO, move to the Split PO Information Needed tab.
  • If the request is not related to splitting a PO, move to the Incorrectly Entered Ticket tab. 
  • If a resend request, move to the Split PO - PO Resend tab. 


Tip 1

These are internal requests only the SU cannot view these in Partner Home.


Visual 1.1:



Split PO Confirmed With Supplier


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm Inventory Levels

To check the stock, click on Inventory when viewing the PO in the Product tab. (Visual 1.1)

Review the amount showing in the Quantity Available column for the requested warehouse. (Visual 1.2)

If there is available inventory, move to Step 2

If there is no inventory available, use the  template to advise the Reporter and move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Open Reroute tool

From the Order tab, select the relevant PO to navigate to the Product tab.  

From the Product tab, click on Run a Wizard > More Wizards. (Visual 2.1)

This will open the Order page.

Click on Shipping View. (Visual 2.2)

Use Ctrl + F to locate the relevant PO.

Click on Launch Reroute/Backorder Wizard. (Visual 2.3)

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

3

Confirm Item and Quantity

Select Re-route Items option. 

Click Next. (Visual 3.1)

Select the correct PO number.

Click Next. (Visual 3.2)

Select the Item number and the relevant quantity as per request.

Click Next. (Visual 3.3)

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

Visual 3.3:


4

Confirm Supplier warehouse

Select the Supplier warehouse the order should be routed to based on the request. 

If a warehouse is not automatically suggested, tick the box next to Override to a non-available supplier.

Type in the Supplier name and select the warehouse to which you wish to reroute. (Visual 4.1)

Click Submit. 

Visual 4.1:

5

Complete the reroute

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective



NA

What is this ticket for?

  • Our internal Partners will use this intake to enter an internal ticket to the AOPS project for in scope requests.
    • Order Status Update
    • Cancellation Inquiry
    • Reroute PO
    • Bulk Requests (>25 POs) 
  • Most common asks in these tickets are: 
    • Individual or Bulk reroute requests or cancellations from SRMs and CMs
    • Reroute requests from B2B partners
    • Ship status updates from SRM’s/B2B partners


EU

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Tier 1 - Adhoc Request. 

These tickets are entered by various stakeholders, including the Finance, Collections Support and EU Carrier Support teams, when they require assistance contacting a Supplier. 

These tickets provide support to internal teams/stakeholders on four topics:

  1. Order Support Query
  2. Finance / Invoicing / Payment Support
  3. Logistics/Carrier Switch Support
  4. Performance/Supplier Account-Related Enquiry
Ticket Entry Breakdown (Routed to C2S)
  1. Order Support Query
    1. Incomplete Shipment/Split Consignment
  2. Finance / Invoicing / Payment Support
    1. Cancelled Order Shipped
    2. Manifest Required
    3. Order Status Issue (restricting SU from raising invoices)
  3. Logistics/Carrier Switch Support
    1. EU Logistics Product Question
    2. EU Logistics Adhoc Support
  4. Performance/Supplier Account-Related Enquiry
    1. Supplier Partnership Discontinued

This SOP provides guidance to our internal stakeholders on how to enter these tickets. 







Overview

How do these tickets differ from your typical ticket?

  • They are internal only, and not supplier facing
  • The internal partner who opened the ticket will be your primary contact
  • The SSAT prompt is not required. All other QC measurements are the same





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



NA



Order Status Update


An internal partner is requesting we update the fulfillment status of a PO. The most frequent request we receive in these cases is to mark POs as shipped to trigger invoice payments to the supplier


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Intake Form

Review the intake form and confirm that the reporter has included the following information in their update:

  • The SUID
  • Name of the SU contact who approved the request
  • The Impacted PO(s)
  • The Status the PO(s) should be updated to 
    • For Backorders - the reporter should include the date the PO(s) should be backordered to 
    • For Shipped Status updates - the reporter must include valid tracking IDs or signed BOL for the PO(s)

IF: The Reporter enters a request asking for us to do something other than update the order status, we will respond with the Ad Hoc -Incorrect Ticket Entered


2

Update PO Status

Update the PO status as appropriate using Shipping View and/or the Bulk Order Delay tool based on the request the reporter makes.


3

Send Template

Respond to the reporter utilizing the Adhoc Order Status Update template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 




PO Reroute Request


No tickets should come through via Adhoc for Reroute requests should you receive a rereoute request under another category of Adhoc support please flag this to your manager or onshore POC.

Under the updated Warehouse Compliance Policy (effective December 15, 2025), purchase orders must be fulfilled from the warehouse assigned at the time of order placement, based on reported inventory availability.

We will not be rerouting orders between dropship locations.



Cancellation Inquiry


An internal partner is requesting we cancel a PO on behalf of a supplier. Our cancellation policy is very restrictive, and we will not cancel POs without it falling under a pre-approved category. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Intake Form

Review the intake form and confirm that the reporter has included the following information in their update:

  • The SUID
  • Name of the SU contact who approved the request
  • The Impacted PO(s)
    • The Impacted OPID(s) if the cancellation is partial
  • Any additional pertinent details

2

Confirm Cancellation Reason

Confirm the cancellation reason. Cancellation will only be approved in the cases of: 

  • Item Discontinuation
  • PO Transmission Errors
  • SKU set up errors
  • Regulatory Compliance

If OTHER is listed, please review the details of the cancellation - these will generally be denied, though if you’re unsure, check with your manager or enter a ticket-help request


3

Cancel Items

Process Cancellation of the item by applicable PO(s) or OPID(s) using the Bulk Order Cancellation tool


4

Send Template

Respond to the reporter utilizing the Adhoc Cancellation Approved template, and close the ticket under a Done resolution.






EU

Actions

Visuals

Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type and PO Number from the ticket. (Visual 1.1)

Move to the relevant tab based on the query in the ticket description and any comments added by the Reporter to assist them with the resolution.

Cancelled Order Shipped


These requests may be entered by the Finance team to confirm if the Supplier processed the order before or after a cancellation was initiated. 

The information we provide helps the Finance team determine who is liable for the cost of the PO. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm Order Cancellation Info

Confirm who initiated the cancellation by searching for the PO in the Order Trail, from the Order tab. (Visual 1.1)

If the customer initiated the cancellation you will see the order note in Visual 1.2.

If Wayfair initiated the cancellation you will see the order note in Visual 1.3.

If the Supplier initiated the cancellation you will see the order note in Visual 1.4.

Take note of the time of cancellation. (Tip 1)

If the Supplier provided a manifest (attached on the POS or original linked ticket), move to .

If no manifest was provided, move to . 

Tip 1:

Time stamps are generally in Eastern time so you will need to translate this to the Supplier's time zone. 

This time converter can be used to compare different time zones. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

Visual 1.3:

Visual 1.4:

2

Review Manifest

Open the attachment on your ticket, or on the original linked ticket, to review the manifest. 

Check to confirm it includes the following:

  • A physical signature from the driver collecting
  • The same PO as referenced in the ticket
  • The tracking number for the relevant PO

An example of a valid Wayfair manifest is included in Visual 2.1.

An example of a valid Mosina manifest is included in Visual 2.2.

An example of an invalid manifest is included in Visual 2.3. This is considered invalid as no driver signature is present. 

If the driver signature is not present, but the tracking number is included on the manifest, check to see if this as scans as per the steps outlined in this SOP.

If there are scans present, we can accept this as proof of shipment and provide education to the Supplier to ensure the driver signs the manifest going forward. 

Move to . 

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:


3

Confirm PO Shipping Info

Review the Order Trail to locate any of the following events for the specific PO:

  • Label Printed (Visual 3.1)
  • PO Backordered (Visual 3.2)
  • PO Shipping (Visual 3.3, 3.4 &  3.5)
  • Carrier Pick Up Delay (Visual 3.6)

Take note of the time of these events.

Check to confirm if there are any scans on the tracking information as per the steps outlined in this SOP. Take note of the time of the first scan, indicating when the carrier received the item.

Compare these times to the time the PO was cancelled (as identified in Step 1) to determine if the Supplier received a notification of the cancellation before the order was shipped. (Tip 2)

Tip 2:

Ensure you are familiar with Wayfair's cancellation policy: 

Cancellation Policy
  1. If a customer cancels an order within 30 minutes of placement, we will honor it.
  2. Orders will automatically be cancelled if:
    1. The order has been back-ordered more than three days from the original estimated ship date.
    2. The Supplier does not reply to the request within 24 hours (excluding weekends and warehouse closure on the Partner Home calendar for up to 2 consecutive days).
  3. If the Supplier denies a cancellation request, they must provide:
      1. fulfillment confirmation (e.g., ASN) within three calendar days of the estimated ship date
      2. transportation scans from the carrier within five calendar days of the estimated ship date (including weekends).
  4. If an order is shipped after cancellation or fails to meet the denied-cancellation criteria above, the Supplier is liable for product costs, taxes and shipping costs.

Update Ticket

Update the ticket using the Cancellation_Info_Internal template.

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.

Marking Orders as Shipped


You may receive a ticket from one of our Logistics teams to request that an order is marked as shipped. 

This may happen when both the Supplier and Carrier confirm the order has shipped, but no scans are present. 


If we do not have confirmation from both the Supplier and Carrier, or another valid proof of shipment (eg. scans or signed manifest), please following the steps in the Check Current Order Status and Check for Scans tabs of the Update Tracking Number/ Order Status SOP to confirm we have enough information to mark the order as shipped.

Reroute PO to Correct Warehouse Address


No tickets should come through via Adhoc for Reroute requests should you receive a rereoute request under another category of Adhoc support please flag this to your manager or onshore POC.

XDP Remanifest Request


You may receive a ticket for a PO shipping with XDP where the carrier is requesting that the PO be remanifested. 

This occurs when there is a change in depot or warehouse address resulting in the PO having the incorrect information on XDP's end.

The PO needs to be manifested (assigned) to the correct XDP depot or collection warehouse in their system or they will be unable to collect.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Generate the Shipping Docs

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.1)

Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. 

Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. 

If the request is due to the Supplier changing their warehouse address, click onto the PO to open the Order Details page to confirm the correct warehouse address is now showing in Partner Home. (Visual 1.2)

If the correct warehouse information is not showing, reindex the PO until it does. 

How to Reindex a PO

Navigate to the Re-Indexing Tool. (Tip 1)

Paste the PO number into the text box in the Request Purchase Order Data Re-Indexing section. 

Select Submit. 

Select the check box beside the PO. 

Click Print Documents in the pop up menu. (Visual 1.3)

This will open a new tab. 

Confirm the following: (Visual 1.4) 

  • All documents selected to print
  • Warehouse (Tip 2)
  • Pickup Date
  • Confirm Labels quantity (Tip 3)

Select Generate Documents. (Visual 1.4; Tip 4) 

Close the window. 

Tip 1:

This tool can only be accessed when in Admin View and will not work when logged in as a Supplier to Partner Home. 


Tip 2:

This is only required for Suppliers who ship out of multiple warehouses. 


Tip 3:

If the Reporter advises a different amount of labels are required, select Edit Shipping Labels. (Visual 1.4)

Input the amount of labels required in Piece Count. 

Divide the Total Weight by the Piece Count to get the Unit Weight.

Select Done Editing. (Visual 1.5) 


Tip 4:

If there are any issues generating the shipping documents, see the turquoisetruesmallCannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label SOPhttps://infohub.corp.wayfair.com/x/GgdBQgto troubleshoot.

Save the labels

Review the Fulfilment Status – it should now appear as Awaiting Pickup. (Visual 2.1)

A green pop-up will appear at the bottom of the screen. (Visual 2.2)

Select Download Documents to save the labels.

These should be saved using the PO as the title for the file. 

Check the labels to ensure they are showing the correct collection address. If they are not, return to to reindex the PO and reprint the label.

How to Reindex a PO

Navigate to the Re-Indexing Tool. (Tip 1)

Paste the PO number into the text box in the Request Purchase Order Data Re-Indexing section. 

Select Submit. 

Select the check box beside the PO. 

Click Print Documents in the pop up menu at the bottom of the page. (Visual 1.3)

This will open a new tab. 

Confirm the following: (Visual 1.4) 

  • All documents selected to print
  • Warehouse (Tip 2)
  • Pickup Date
  • Confirm Labels quantity (Tip 3)

Select Generate Documents. (Visual 1.4; Tip 4) 

Close the window. 

Tip 1:

This tool can only be accessed when in Admin View and will not work when logged in as a Supplier to Partner Home. 


Tip 2:

This is only required for Suppliers who ship out of multiple warehouses. 


Tip 3:

If the Reporter advises a different amount of labels are required, select Edit Shipping Labels. (Visual 1.4)

Input the amount of labels required in Piece Count. 

Divide the Total Weight by the Piece Count to get the Unit Weight.

Select Done Editing. (Visual 1.5) 


Tip 4:

If there are any issues generating the shipping documents, see the turquoisetruesmallCannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label SOPhttps://infohub.corp.wayfair.com/x/GgdBQgto troubleshoot. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

Visual 1.3:

Visual 1.4:

Visual 1.5:

2

Save the labels

Review the Fulfilment Status – it should now appear as Awaiting Pickup. (Visual 2.1)

A green pop-up will appear at the bottom of the screen. (Visual 2.2)

Select Download Documents to save the labels.

These should be saved using the PO as the title for the file. 

Check the labels to ensure they are showing the correct collection address. If they are not, return to to reindex the PO and reprint the label. 

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Update Ticket

Attach the label to the ticket for the Supplier's reference. (Visual 3.1)

Select Outbound Contact > Email. (Visual 3.2)

In the From Address field, select eusuppliertier1@wayfair.com from the dropdown.

Remove any auto-populated emails from the Recipients (To) and Copy recipients (Cc) fields. 

Add the Order Processing email address. 

How to Identify Supplier Email Address

From the Supplier tab, scroll down and expand the Wayfair & Supplier Contacts section. 

Find the Order Processing contact within the Supplier Contacts section.

No edits are required to the Subject Line. 

Add the New Label Provided Email template.

Select the label attachment from the Attachments dropdown menu. (Visual 3.3)

Select Send. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

Visual 3.3:







Performance/Supplier Account-Related Enquiry



Supplier Partnership Discontinued


This request may be entered to request support with cancelling a Supplier's open orders when the partnership is being discontinued. 

Before taking any action on these requests, it is vital you validate that the request was entered through the proper channels.

These requests can only be entered by the Supplier's SRM (Supplier Relationship Manager), CM (Category Manager) or a member of the Desk (EU HSD & TSD & PAT) team.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Validate Request

Identify the Reporter from the Ticket panel. (Visual 1.1)

Navigate to the Supplier tab. 

Expand the Wayfair and Supplier Contacts section. 

Locate the Supplier Relationship Manager and Category Manager in the Wayfair Contacts section. (Visual 1.2)

If this name matches the Reporter, move to . 

If not, confirm if the Reporter is a member of the Desk team. 

Navigate to the OrgBrowser. 

Search using the Reporter name.

If the team is showing as EU HSD & TSD & PAT, move to . (Visual 1.3)

If the Reporter is from a different team, add an update to the ticket asking them to confirm if this request has been approved by the SRM before moving to the next steps.


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

Visual 1.3:

2

Export Open Orders & Parts

Open the Partner Home portal. 

Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 2.1)

Navigate to Customer Orders > Order Exports. ( Visual 2.2) 

Expand the Export Filters dropdown. Select All Orders for Fulfillment Type and Order Type. 

Select Generate Report. (Visual 2.3; Tip 1)

Navigate to the Download Center. (Visual 2.4)

Locate the Order Exports file and download it. (Visual 2.5)

Add the file as an attachment to your ticket for reference.

Tip 1:

You must be logged in as the Supplier for the report to generate.


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

Visual 2.4:

Visual 2.5:

3

Enter a CAT ticket

Navigate to the CAT ticket entry portal. 

If only one PO needs to be cancelled, enter a Customer Outreach Needed ticket. (Visual 3.1)

If multiple POs from the same supplier need to be cancelled, enter a Mass Error ticket. (Visual 3.2)

Create the ticket advising that all of the orders in the attached file need to be cancelled. 

Ensure to add the Order Exports file as an attachment. 

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

4

Link the tickets


Link the tickets, by expanding the Linked Tickets dropdown and selecting Add. (Visual 4.1)

Enter the Issue ID for the CAT ticket and select Validate.

Change the Relationship to Relates to and select Link Ticket (Visual 4.2)


Visual 4.1:

Visual 4.2:

5

Update Ticket

Update the Reporter using the Cancel_All_POs_Internal template. 

Move to the Resolve ticket tab. 






Resolve Ticket

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Add a Comment

Add a comment to advise the Reporter of any action taken and feedback from the Supplier.


2

Editing Comment (Optional)

Click the pencil icon if you need to make any edits to your comment. (Visual 2.1)

Update Status

Select Change Status > Done. (Visual 3.1)

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) - VCN

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

This article outlines the process for actioning tickets with the Request Type: Update Tracking Number/ Order Status. 

Suppliers enter these tickets when they require a change to their order status. 

It may also be necessary to mark an order as shipped or add tracking as part of the resolution for other request types. 


Overview




Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool (Linked)

Use Case

Re-Indexing Tool


Dropship Orders


Transportation Event History




Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

Small Parcel (SP)
Large Parcel (LP)
Less-than Truckload (LTL)
In Network LTL
Out of Network LTL
PRO Number
Manifest
Bill of Lading (BOL)





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow


mediumfalse

Unless designated with greenlocationNA Specific or magentalocationEU Specific, process should be presumed as being globalized.



Review the Ticket


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Ticket

Review the ticket to identify information relevant to your investigation. 

You can see the Customer Request Type and PO Number.

  • NA Ticket Example (AOPS Project) (Visual 1.1)
  • EU Ticket Example (POS Project) (Visual 1.2)


Currently, it is not possible for Supplier's to raise these Request Types on replacement part POs, so it is important you review the Description to identify if the Supplier is querying the full PO or any replacement parts related to it. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Review the Description

Review the ticket to confirm the Supplier's query. 

If they are experiencing an error printing their labels, move to the appropriate regional tab.

If they are requesting a different number of labels, need help with a replacement part, or have a query regarding manifests or commercial invoices, move to the Additional Queries tab (this is specific to the EU space at this time). 

How to Identify a Replacement Part

A replacement part is indicated by a PO number with '-R-' and a number following it. 

For example UK123456789-R-12345.





Before You Start


mediuminfo

This article outlines how to validate the tracking information on an order. 

This action is generally part of a larger process, this article may be used in combination with another SOP.

This will be useful in cases where you need to check the status of an order. It is usually the first step in any WIMS (Where Is My Stuff) investigation to confirm if the PO has valid carrier scans. 

As Wayfair works with several different carriers, the tracking portal you must check will depend on the assigned carrier. Please refer to the relevant tab below. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Verify Scans

If you are unsure of which carrier the Supplier has used, this generic tracking website may be useful to verify scans. (Visual 1)

Enter the tracking number provided and select Track Package. 

Depending on the carrier the supplier is assigned, follow the steps outlined in the relevant tab to confirm if there is valid scans on the tracking.

Visual 1:



Check Current Order Status


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Log into Partner Home


  1. Open the Partner Home portal.
  2. Log in as the Supplier using the search bar in the red banner at the top of the page. (Visual 1.1)

Visual 1.1:

2

Locate Relevant PO

  1. Navigate to Orders > Dropship Orders. (Visual 2.1)
  2. Using the search bar, filter to the relevant PO. (Visual 2.2; Tip 1)


Tip 1

If the Supplier's inquiry is related to a replacement parts order, ensure you select the Replacement Parts tab to locate the relevant PO.


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Identify Fulfillment Status

  1. Identify the Fulfilment Status and review the relevant tab. (Visual 3.1)
  2. Ensure there are no discrepancies between the PO status in Partner Home and the status on Order View by crosscheck the order.  
    1. From the Order tab, select the relevant PO to navigate to the Product tab. (Visual 3.2)
    2. In the Destination section, review the Delivery Status. (Visual 3.2)

The terminology between the statuses won't match up exactly between the Product tab and Partner Home. Please see a comparison of these below:

Partner HomeProduct Tab
New Order is at warehouse
ProcessingItem is on backorder
Awaiting PickupReady for pickup
Picked Up (Awaiting Carrier Scan)Ready for pickup
In Transit (Carrier Scan Received)Out for delivery
Cancellation RequestedItem is pending cancellation
CancelledItem has been cancelled
DeliveredDelivered
ReturnedDelivered (Return will show under Service History)

If the Delivery Status does not match the Fulfilment Status in Partner Home, resolve by following the steps in the Reindex tab. This will refresh the status in Partner Home so it appears correctly for the Supplier. 

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:



Identify Fulfillment Status



New


A New status indicates that the order has not been acknowledged by the Supplier.

If the New status is showing for a Replacement Part, it is not possible to print the label until the order has been acknowledged. (Tip 1)

Tip 1

If the Supplier has entered the ticket to request a replacement parts label, please follow the steps outlined in the Cannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label SOP, under the Additional Queries > Replacement Part Labels section.




Processing


Once the order has been acknowledged by the Supplier, the status will change to Processing. 

Processing indicates that the Supplier has seen the order, all details are correct and they can process this be the Original Ship By Date. 



Staged


This status applies only to Suppliers using the Wayfair Companion App. 

It is not very commonly seen. 

You can find more information on the app here. 




Awaiting Pickup


This indicates the label has already been printed.



Supplier Requesting the Label


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Download the Label

Download the label from the Transportation Event History tool. (Tip 2)

Search using the PO number and click Submit. 

Under the Label Print Information you will see: 

  • Tracking Number
  • Amount of Labels
  • Carrier (LabelService (ID))
  • The Label (Label Path)

Click on the Label Print hyperlink.

This will open a new tab where you can review the label to ensure it is correct. 

2

IF: Label Count / Must Ship By Date is Incorrect

  1. Select the check box beside the PO.

  2. Click More Actions > Refresh Orders.

  3. Refresh multiple times until the status reverts to processing.
  4. Follow the steps in the Print Label tab to generate the correct label. 

3

IF: Label is Correct
  1. Save with the PO Number as the file title. 
  2. Attach to the ticket for the Supplier's reference. 
  3. Use the SU_Already_Printed_Label template to advise the Supplier of the next steps. 

4

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




Supplier Advising Order has Shipped


Review the Check for Scans tab to confirm if there is movement on the tracking. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

IF: No Scans on Tracking

Use the SU_Claims_Shipped template to request a manifest.

Add #InfoReq as an internal comment.

Update the ticket status to Waiting for Supplier.  

If the Supplier does not reply, no more action is required and the ticket will be automatically closed. 

If the Supplier responded, the ticket will move back into your queue to be actioned accordingly.


2

IF: Scans on Tracking

Move to the Reindex PO tab to update the correct PO status in Partner Home.

Use the Incorrect_Status_Reindexed template to advise the Supplier of the next steps.






Picked Up (Awaiting Carrier Scan)


  • This indicates the Supplier has declared the order as shipped but it has yet to receive scans on the tracking. 
  • If the Supplier advises the order needs to be marked as shipped, review the Check for Scans tab to confirm if there is movement on the tracking.
  • It may take up to 24 hours for Evri to scan an item after pickup. In this case, ask the Supplier to allow time to for scans to appear.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

IF: No Scans on Tracking
  1. Use the SU_Claims_Shipped template to request a manifest.
  2. Add #InfoReq as an internal comment.
  3. Update the ticket status to Waiting for Supplier. 
    1. If the Supplier does not reply, no more action is required and the ticket will be automatically closed.
    2. If the Supplier responded, the ticket will move back into your queue to be actioned accordingly.
  4. If less than 24 hours since Supplier advises shipped:
    1. Use the Overdue Order Status Reply template
  5. If more than 24 hours since Supplier advises shipped:
    1. Use the SU_Claims_Shipped template to request a manifest.
    2. Update the ticket status to Waiting for Supplier.
    3. If the Supplier does not reply, no more action is required and the ticket will be automatically closed.
    4. If the Supplier responded, the ticket will move back into your queue to be actioned accordingly.

2

IF: Scans on Tracking

Move to the Update Shipping Info tab.




In Transit (Carrier Scan Received)

This indicates the Supplier has provided the Advanced Shipping Notification (ASN), or the PO has received scans on the tracking. 

The order may have been marked shipped in error if the Supplier provided the Advanced Shipping Notification (ASN) too soon. 

ASNs are visible from the Order Trail. 

Review the Check for Scans tab to confirm if there is movement on the tracking.


Supplier Advises Order has not Shipped


Follow the steps outlined in the Reindex PO tab to refresh the PO status. 

Follow the steps in the Print Label tab to generate the correct label. 

Advise the Supplier that order has been marked as processing and new labels have been provided. Advise them to avoid prematurely sending ASNs to prevent the order being shipped in error. 

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 



Full PO


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Select Edit Shipping/Destination Info
  1. From the Product tab, select Edit Shipping/Destination Info. (Visual 1.1)
    1. This will open a new tab with the shipping details. 
  2. Change the Status to Pending Ship Confirmation. This will remove the ship date. 
  3. Click remove beside the tracking number. 
  4. Select Update.

Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:



Replacement Part PO


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Select View Parts

The Replacement Part will show under Service history in the Product tab. 

  1. Select Show Details > Select View Parts. (Visual 1.1)
    1. This will open a new tab with the Replacement Parts Request Info.
  2. Change the status to Pending Ship Confirmation and remove the tracking number. (Visual 1.2)
  3. Click Submit. 


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:







Cancellation Requested


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Cancellation Requested

This indicates that a cancellation request is pending. 

No labels can be printed while the order is in this status. 

The Supplier needs to confirm or deny the cancellation request in the Order Alerts section of Partner Home. (Visual 1)


Visual 1:

2

Send Template

Use the Confirm_Pending_Cancellation or Deny_Pending_Cancellation template to advise the Supplier of the next steps. 


3

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab. 




Cancelled


This indicates that the order has been cancelled. 

It is not possible to reverse a cancellation. 



Supplier Advises Order Shipped After Cancelled


If the PO is registered or the label is printed prior to cancellation, we are charged by the carrier for this. 

If the Supplier confirms a cancellation through Partner Home and still ships the order, they are responsible for all associated costs. 

The Cancellation Policy is outlined for the Supplier in this Help Center article.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review the Check for Scans tab

Review the Check for Scans tab to confirm if the parcel is still in transit or if it has been delivered to the customer. 


2

Enter a Ticket

Enter a  ticket so Customer Service can notify the Customer and get the item RTS/returned. 

If the B2B banner is visible on the Order tab, enter a B2B Support > Service Inquiry ticket, instead of a Customer Information Needed ticket.


3

Link the Tickets
  1. Link the tickets, by expanding the Linked Tickets dropdown and selecting Add. (Visual 3.1)
  2. Enter the Issue ID for the CSO ticket and select Validate. (Visual 3.2)
  3. Change the Relationship to Relates to and select Link Ticket. (Visual 3.2)

Visual 3.1:

Visual 3.2:

4

Send Template

Use the Shipped_after_cancelled or Delivered_after_cancelled template to update the supplier of the next steps. 


5

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.

If the Supplier has a different query regarding cancellations, the Cancellation Inquiry SOP may be useful in resolving their issue. 







Delivered

Delivered indicates that the product has been received. This may also occur in cases where the parcel has been returned to sender (RTS), making it appear delivered in error. 

UPS may return the item if the labels are not generated with the information necessary for delivery. 

The Supplier may enter a ticket for support with labels in these cases. 

Review the Cannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label SOP to resolve this issue. 



Supplier Advises Order is RTS


If the PO is registered or the label is printed prior to cancellation, we are charged by the carrier for this. 

If the Supplier confirms a cancellation through Partner Home and still ships the order, they are responsible for all associated costs. 

The Cancellation Policy is outlined for the Supplier in this Help Center article.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Enter Customer Information Needed ticket

Enter a Customer Information Needed ticket.

If Customer Service have already replaced/refunded the PO, it is not required to enter a ticket for them as outlined below.


If the B2B banner is visible on the Order tab, enter a B2B Support > Service Inquiry ticket, instead of a Customer Information Needed ticket. (Visual 1)


Visual 1:

2

Send Template

Use the Item_RTS template to advise the Supplier of the next steps.


3

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.






Returned


This status indicates that the the item has been returned.

This could be due to a customer returning or refusing the order. It may also be due to the carrier returning the item to sender. 

This status indicated that the refund has been processed for the customer. 

We are unable to change the order status in this case. 



Supplier Advises Order is RTS


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1


  1. Enter a Customer Information Needed ticket.
  2. Enter a ticket to request Customer Service replace/refund the order. 



If the B2B banner is visible on the Order tab, enter a B2B Support > Service Inquiry ticket, instead of a Customer Information Needed ticket.


If Customer Service have already replaced/refunded the PO, it is not required to enter a ticket for them as outlined below.


Ticket Template

Hi team, 

  • PO / Item number: 
  • SU Ticket number:
  • Issue: 
  • Action required:

Thank you


2

Send Template

Use the Item_RTS template to advise the Supplier of the next steps.


3

Move to Resolve Ticket

Move to the Resolve Ticket tab.




Supplier has Payment Questions


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Review Help Center Resources

Review these Help Centre resources to see if you can find the answer for the Supplier: 

  • About the Finance Dashboard
  • Frequently Asked Questions

Advise them to enter an Accounting ticket for the Finance team if you are unable to answer their query. 


2

Send Template

Update the Supplier using the General_Invoice/Deduction or PO_Deduction template. 








Accessing PasswordState


This section outlines how to access and use PasswordState.

This is where all the carrier portals and login details are stored.

It's recommended to use the carrier's portal, compared to tracking via the link in 'Order View', as the portal provides more detail.

For example, to obtain a POD (Proof of Delivery) almost all carriers require you to be logged into the portal to view this. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Accessing PasswordState

Open PasswordState. (Tip 1)

Use the search bar or page navigation to locate the relevant carrier details. (Visual 1.1)

Tip 1:

PasswordState may also be referred to as the Vault, which was its legacy title.


Visual 1.1:

2

Access carrier portal

Click on the black circle under URL to navigate to the carrier portal. Alternatively you can select the arrow to copy the URL link. (Visual 2.1)

The portal login details are listed under User Name and Password. Select the arrow to copy each of these to paste into the carrier portal. (Visual 2.2; Tip 2 and 3)

Tip 2:

Ensure to Clear Clipboard between copying the User Name and Password to ensure an incorrect password is not used. (Visual 2.3)


Tip 3:

Not all carriers have login details. This will be explained in each specific carrier tab. 


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:



Small Parcel



NA SP Guidance


mediuminfofalse

A supplier reaches out and asks us to update the tracking number and ship status on an order. This is often likely due to an aged alert populating in the suppliers dashboard, but suppliers also make this request proactively


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Confirm Tracking ID

If the SU does not provide a tracking number please ask them to provide one, and put the ticket in a Pending status until the SU responds. (Tip 1)

Tip 1

When labels are generated via Partner Home, the shipment is presumed to be shipped on the Wayfair account as expected.



2

Open Shipping View

Locate the PO in Shipping View



3

Update Tracking Information

Select “Update Shipping Info” in the PO’s line item - this will pop up a new window to enter the shipping details into

  1. Update the PO Status to: Shipped
  2. Update the Carrier if needed (Should be defaulted to FedEx)
  3. Check the Shipping Confirmation Sent checkbox
  4. Enter Tracking Number 
  5. Click the green “Update” Button

4

Re-Index the PO

Enter the PO(s) into the Re-index tool after you have submitted the changes to ensure that the PO status also reflects correctly in PartnerHome. Instructions on using the Re-index tool can be found on the Reindex PO tab. 


5

Send Template

Respond to the supplier utilizing the Update Tracking/Order Status - SP template and close the ticket under a Done resolution. 




EU SP Carriers



DHL Paket


This section outlines how to validate tracking for DHL Paket. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Search for tracking

Navigate to the DHL Paket website. (Tip 1)

Enter the tracking number and click the magnifying glass to search. (Visual 1.1)

Tip 1:

No login details are required for this site. 


Visual 1.1:

2

Read the scans

Check the status of the consignment. (Visual 2.1 - 1)
The scans are explained below: 

  • Shipping labels printed (Visual 2.1 - 2)
  • Consignment collected (Visual 2.1 - 3)
  • Delivery date and timeslot send to customer - (Visual 2.1 - 4)
  • Consignment on delivery truck (Visual 2.1 - 5)
  • Consignment delivered (Visual 2.1 - 6)


 

 Visual 2.1:

3

Locate POD

For delivered orders, you will be able to confirm the name of person who accepted the delivery. (Visual 3.1 - 7)

DHL Parcel UK


This section outlines how to validate tracking for DHL Parcel UK. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Search for tracking

Navigate to the DHL Parcel UK portal. (Tip 1)

  • If tracking does not appear via the Wayfair DHL UK portal, try DHL UK - Track a Parcel instead.

Enter the tracking number and click Find Your Parcel. (Visual 1.1)

Tip 1:

No login details are required for this portal. 


Visual 1.1:

Read the scans

If the parcel was not collected, no scans will appear here. 


 Visual 2.1:

Locate POD

If any signature or GPS co-ordinates were captured on delivery, these will also be available here.

DPD


This section outlines how to validate tracking for DPD. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Search for tracking

Navigate to the DPD portal. (Tip 1)

Enter the tracking number and click the arrow to search. (Visual 1.1)

Enter the customer postcode when prompted to view the consignment details. (Tip 2)

Tip 1:

No login details are required to see the full scan overview/history.

You do need to be logged in to see the POD. 


Tip 2:

Visual 1.1:

2

Read the scans

To view scans, you may need to click on the 3 dots icon. (Visual 2.1)

Scans will appear as it Visual 2.2.


Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Locate POD

Currently, the POD cannot be accessed through the Business portal link on Passwordstate.

Paste the following link into your browser - https://tracking.dpd.de/status/de_DE/parcel/ - and add the specific tracking number at the end of the link.

To view the POD, click the 'Evidence' link under the 'Delivered' scan.

Log in using the details provided on Passwordstate. 

The POD will then be downloaded and can be opened as a separate image file.

Steps are outlined in Visual 3.1.

#

Steps

Actions

1

Search for tracking

Navigate to the Evri website. (Tip 1 and 2)

Scans are explained below:

  • Parcel received (Visual 2.2 - 1)

  • Parcel is out for delivery (Visual 2.2 - 3)

Tip 3:

The customer post code can be found in Order View.

Visual 2.2:

 

3

Locate POD

Click 'View proof of delivery' to see any images and GPS captured on delivery.  (Visual 3.2)

This section outlines how to validate tracking for GLS. 


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Search for tracking

Navigate to the GLS portal. 

Enter the tracking number and click the arrow to search. (Visual 1.1)

Enter the customer postcode when prompted to view the consignment details. (Tip 2)

Tip 1:

EU - Wayfair Delivery Network (WDN)


This section outlines information on the EU Wayfair Delivery Network's (EUWDN) carrier tracking, how to access the WDN Portal and track orders. 

There are two tools to track WDN Orders and see the status of the PO:

  1. The Global Visibility Tool which will tell you the dates labels were printed, shipped and delivered.

  2. 4sight which will provide further details such as date goods were scanned to the hub, placed in storage, moved onwards and delivered.



What is WDN?


WDN is short for Wayfair Delivery Network. WDN is Wayfair’s own Large Parcel courier, handling deliveries out of Castlegate as well as a rapidly growing base of dropship suppliers. All WDN shipped orders offer customers next day delivery.


#7f187f#F2F4F4White3#7f187fsolidWDN Delivery Schedule

All WDN Deliveries take place between Monday and Friday.

London Deliveries take place between 7am and 8pm.

Scottish Deliveries take place between 11am and 8pm.

All other deliveries take place between 8am and 8pm EXCEPT for a select few postcodes in the North of England where deliveries take place between 10am and 6pm.



WDN Tracking


For information on Customer WDN Tracking please see information here.


#7f187f#F2F4F4White3#7f187fsolidDropship WDN UK to UK

Supplier prints shipping labels, goods are handed over to the Carrier WDN on the Must ship on date.  NB; When discussing with our suppliers WDN Orders please ensure they are aware WDN will only collect the orders on the Must Ship On Date, not earlier.

If suppliers are shipping WDN orders in advance of the MSOD it will result in our warehouse teams having to store the goods while we await the actual delivery date scheduled with the customer. 

Same goes for Suppliers being aware of orders not shipping on time, Most of our customers receive their WDN delivery the next business day after the Must Ship By Date therefore please ensure to remind suppliers to ship at the correct dates.

Collection time windows and dates are shown in EU Logistics Ops: Supplier Collection Set-Ups for suppliers shipping on WDN Network. You can see the days Suppliers are set up for WDN collections and the time window drivers should arrive. 

Once the driver collects from our supplier, the goods will arrive back to the hub on the same date. *Scans should be visible on the order by 11pm the latest - ie. Goods showing marked as shipped. Therefore as noted previously it is extremely important that suppliers only ship on the scheduled MSOD otherwise the customer promise of delivery is affected and goods will remain in storage until date of delivery is due.


If you fail to see scans on WDN orders by 11pm please raise a ticket to LOS to investigate further.



WDN Shipping on the DHL Freight Network


Ireland

Wayfair is the final mile carrier for all LTL & White Glove items shipping to Ireland. 

To tell how a PO ships to Ireland, look in the logistics matrix for the SU:

  1. If it's just WDN there, then it will be WDN the whole journey & this is 1 label
  2. If it's just XDP there, then it's XDP for the first part of the journey & it requires 2 labels (XDP & WDN)
  3. If it's WDN and XDP there then either of the 2 options is possible, you will know which one is selected in label print

WDN Delivery Timeframe for ROI (Republic of Ireland)

It will take up to 5 business days for the parcels to be delivered once the cartons arrive into WDN's Dublin Hub -  - areas such as Dublin county, Galway City and Cork City are normally quicker.

Each area in Ireland is serviced on a specific day, there is no area serviced daily. E.G. Dublin Central South delivery fence is serviced on Mondays, Wednesdays & Fridays. 

WDN will attempt delivery only 3 times in ROI.

  • We do not pay attention to the inbound date, the outbound date is typically the evening before delivery at 8pm. This is the time the hub has to trunk the carton to the depot to ensure it's there in time for delivery.

  • Transport Ref ID is the depot which will deliver to Carton and final results “Last Updated By” is the date and time the PO Was last scanned.

From here you can see if any boxes are LIT, Stored, delivered etc.






WDN Notes we see on orders


WDN PO WAS NOT SHIPPED BY THE SUPPLIER

The WDN PO on this order was not shipped so we have nothing to deliver today.

Once the item has been received by WDN, it will be booked for the soonest possible date and time and the customer will get an email confirming this.

Please review the order notes to see if the supplier has provided new estimates.

A new EDD will only be provided when item is received by WDN!

*WDN forecast for Suppliers - When drivers arrive to collect and if they don't provide these POs then the WDN team will remove that PO from the route for the next day delivery and will add the above note to the order, stating no PO received from SU. Once an item is is received to the hub process is activated again.

Marking an order as shipped is only allowed when:

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

The team POPs First Mile Outreach Team will raise internal tickets to FMOP project where outreach to suppliers is required regarding WDN orders and processes which suppliers must adhere to. Agents will receive these tickets via DRE under CRT “WDN First Mile Supplier Outreach”  As these tickets are “Internally Raised” SUs will not have visibility on these tickets and all communication will be via outbound email to them from freightpickups@wayfair.com 


Overview

Using SOP identify the reason for outreach required and follow instructions to make outreach to the supplier. Once actioned tickets can be closed. Should the SU reply and you are unsure of next steps flag ticket in help channel for support first, under onshore POC guidance ticket may be advised to move onshore. The long team goal is to build out the SOP further based on help requests should they be raised in the channel or provided to POC directly.  



Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool

Use Case

FMOP Templates > Supplier Templates > Scroll to the bottom for the Supplier Email Templates relevant to this workflow









Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

POC

Point of contact











#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Identify Reason for Outreach via Form


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open the Ticket in ServiceHub 

  1. Open ticket in ServiceHub and see the ticket type/title appearing with “WDN First Mile Outreach.”, Label “WDHFirstMileSupplierOutreach” (Tip 1) (Visual 1.1) 
  2. To identify the required outreach area, open the attached PDF. The file will automatically open when selected.  (Visual 1.2) 
  3. Supplier details related to the request can be found under the Supplier tab.


Tip 1

These are internal requests only the SU cannot view these in Partner Home.


Visual 1.1:

Visual 1.2:

2

Information visible in the PDF 

Once PDF link is opened, you will find:

  • The specific area of support required
  • A detailed outline of the support needed
  • Any relevant supporting links
  • Reference PO numbers (Visual 2.1)

Visual 2.1:

3

Options for Support that WDN team can request from us 

Options available to WDN for requested support to us is as follows:

  • Incorrect Supplier Address
  • Supplier Does Not Ship Wayfair PO
  • Supplier only Ships LTL
  • Supplier Refusing to load Freight
  • Products Not Ready Consistently
  • Product Not Ready
  • Supplier Taking Excessive Loading Time
  • Supplier Contacting Carrier Directly
  • Supplier registering PO to incorrect warehouse
  • Supplier Loading PO’s not in Manifest
  • Internal Reference Number
  • Obstacle- Inaccessible Dock
  • Supplier Closure Confirmation
  • Supplier no longer working with Wayfair

4

Navigate to the tab to complete actions

Once you identify the required support area, proceed to the corresponding SOP tab to carry out the request. (Visual 4.1)

Visual 4.1:



Identify Reason for Outreach



Incorrect Supplier Address


mediuminfo

Wayfair  has been advised by Carrier that warehouse address is incorrect, we must outreach the Supplier to confirm their shipping address. 

A ticket has been raised to us to reach out and confirm the SU warehouse address on record. 

Once confirmed we must ensure any adjustments are made to the address to ensure smooth collections moving forwards.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Verify the Reported Address

  1. Review the ticket and locate the address information provided by the reporter. Ensure the address is clearly stated in the ticket details.
  2. Ensure that the ticket raiser provides all affected PO(s). Include these PO numbers within the communication so the supplier can verify the correct information.

2

Send Email to Supplier

  1. From the email address freightpickup@wayfair.com, send the “Incorrect Supplier Address” template to the supplier. Request confirmation of their correct address for collection. (Tip 1)
  2. Select the email template titled “Incorrect Supplier Address.” This template must be used to confirm the supplier’s address for the upcoming collection.


Tip 1

For steps on sending outbound emails refer to the Sending Outbound Email tab.



3

Update Ticket

  1. Update ticket internally stating, "First outreach completed - waiting for supplier response."
  2. Transition ticket to Waiting for Supplier status.
  3. Do not close the ticket until second outreach logic is complete. 

4

Review after 48 Hours

  1. Trigger: If no supplier response 48 hours after first oureach. The ticket will be flipped back to Waiting for Support.
  2. Send the Follow Up: Address Confirmation Required template from freightpickup@wayfair.com. 
  3. Include the supplier's SRM (exclude DESK contact) in the email if assigned.
  4. Update the ticket internally stating, "Second outreach sent - closing ticket."
  5. Mark the ticket as Done.

5

IF: SU Responds and Ticket Reopens  

  1. Review the Request 

    IF: SU Responds with Updated Address

    If the supplier replies and the ticket reopens with an updated address that differs from the one listed, escalate the ticket to T1 for further support. (Tip 2)


    IF: Confirms Address is Correct

    If the supplier reopens the case confirming the address is correct, update the ticket and close it if the addresses match our records, as it should not be sent onshore. 

    Only tickets with addresses that need updating should be sent onshore. 


  2. Onshore will then take ownership to ensure all necessary updates are completed through the appropriate onshore workflows.


Tip 2

To escalate onshore, reference .



6

orangeOnshore Actions

IF: SU Responds


  1. If the supplier responds stating that the listed address is incorrect and must be updated, begin the onshore escalation process.
  2. Tag in the #temp-firstmile-outreach Slack channel to request the address update for the supplier. Aravind is the primary point of contact for all supplier address corrections. (Tip 3)
  3. Once the request is submitted, First Mile will complete the update the same day. Monitor for confirmation in the Slack thread.
  4. After confirmation that the supplier address has been updated, mark the ticket as Done and close it.


Tip 3

If Aravind is out of office, escalate the request to Khayra or Aravind’s manager, Jacob Ober, for support in completing the update.

  5. Mark the ticket as Done.
