1256174350.title: WIMS - Large Parcel SOP (DSC2S)
1256174350.author: Former user (Deleted)
1256174350.body:
Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) 

Last Updated:



 

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

To determine if a large parcel order is on track to the customer within the estimates provided or if a dock search is needed. How to handle proof of pickup tickets and other large parcel practices

Wrapping Note

When initiating a Dock Search ticket, we should wrap the customer outreach as Internal Contacts/Transfers. See the Wrapping tab within the  for more details.



Overview

  • Ashley Furniture and Bestar do not provide BOLs; there is no need to request it.

  • Tracking, PRO, BOL numbers are not acceptable alternatives (to a signed BOL).

  • A proof of Pickup ticket should not be entered for a Castlegate or Wayfair/Warehouse order.

  • A BOL is available on the LTL carrier's website it does not need to be provided by the supplier.

  • If all cartons have been checked in and show scans at any location a BOL is not needed.

  • Do not create a dock search from a POP ticket.

  • Do not email a customer on a POP ticket.

  • All WIMS tickets are supplier facing.



Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool (Linked)

Use Case

PO HistoryInternal Wayfair tool used to track the registration, scheduled pickup and tracking of FTL POs. Also, for tracking the registration, and tracking of LTL POs shipped within the WDN

Global Visibility

 This tool should only be used when a Large Parcel item is shipped through the Wayfair Delivery Network
Load SearchInternal Wayfair tool used to change the LTL carrier by C2S



Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition

OONOut of Network carrier
HDOHome Delivery Operations
3P DAThird Party Delivery Agent
PROProgressive Rotating Number





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



General Information


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check for BOLIf information is needed, ask the supplier for a signed BOL within this ticket.

2

Update Ticket Type (If Applicable)If a Ship Status ticket is entered on an LTL order that is marked as shipped, treat it as if it was a POP ticket.

3

Request Missing Info (If Applicable)If you have a Ship Status ticket being treated as a POP ticket, use the Ship Status ticket to request the information. Do not open a separate POP ticket.

4

Monitor POP TicketsMonitor POP tickets for 2 days. Day 1 is the day it is entered, and Day 2 is whether a WIMS agent updates it or not.
5Ticket ResolutionClose WIMS tickets only after the Supplier fully answered/resolved the issue. Do not just close the ticket.
6Follow UpFollow up every day to see if there is an update.
7Provide Customer DetailsWhen an agent is responding to a customer, agents must only use the customer name and/or email address from the "Customer Billing Address" Section, which can be found in Shipping View in Support Hub.
8Handle DoreL/Can_DoreLIf you have a ticket for DoreL or Can_DoreL, please follow the 10 Day expectation for shipments to show movement.
9Update on Lack of MovementThere is no expectation to request an update until a shipment has not shown movement for at least 10 Days.
10Address Supplier ResponseIf the supplier responds to a ticket and needs assistance or guidance, please resolve if you are able or submit a ticket to the correct team to assist.
11Final Ticket UpdateThe ticket should be updated so the supplier knows of actions taken to resolve the issue and next actions needed from them.



Large Parcel Process


Follow the same process as small parcel orders, with the addition of checking PO history, Global Visibility, etc.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Track and Validate Shipment
  1. Confirm shipped and ETA dates on order page
  2. Check extranet status in Global Visibility, PO History, Load Search, etc.
  3. Confirm if the PO shipped from CastleGate, Wayfair Warehouse, Ashley Furniture or Bestar. If it did, update the ticket that a proof of ticket cannot be provided and mark ticket as Done. 


Incoming to the correct HDO/3P DA

Email customer with Update on shipment, wrap and mark ticket as Done.


Incoming to a Crossdock/Pool Point

Email customer with Update on shipment, wrap and mark ticket as Done.


Arrived at Crossdock/Pool Point

Email customer with Update on shipment, wrap and mark ticket as Done.


Arrived at the incorrect HDO

Email customer with Initiating dock search, enter dock search ticket, update initiating dock search template and mark ticket as Done.


At the HDO/3PDA, on dock for longer than 48 hours, or the HDO/3P DA had missed a previously scheduled delivery

Email customer with Initiating Dock Search, Enter dock search ticket, update ticket with Initiating dock search template and mark ticket as Done.


At the HDO/3P DA, on dock-schedule pending for less than 48 hours

Email customer with Update on shipment, wrap and mark ticket as Done


All cartons do not show scanned into network

Email customer with Looking into Delay, update ticket with BOL needed, pause for 24 hours


PO has been replaced

If the PO has been replaced, you do not need to respond to the customer, as the automation will kick in and update the customer as well as close the ticket.


PO has already been fully delivered to the customer

If the PO has already been fully delivered to the customer with no conflicting indication, refunded, returned, or has been replaced. Email customer regarding the status, wrap and mark ticket as done. 





Proof of Pickup


#

Scenarios

Actions

Visuals

1

Is the BOL Available? 


IF: Yes
  1. Attach a copy of the BOL to the dock search ticket, if one exists.
    1. The BOL can be found on the carrier's website.


IF: No
  1. Request the BOL from the supplier using the Request BOL script.
  2. Take ownership of the ticket and own through completion.
  3. Follow up as outlined below.



2

If the Supplier responds with a signed BOL
  • Close the Proof of Pickup ticket by clicking Done. (if not already done)

3

If the supplier confirms this PO did not ship:
  • Use Incomplete Script
  • Mark ticket as Done

4

If the supplier does not respond after one business day:

  • Update the POP ticket using the Need BOL script.

5

If the supplier does not respond after 2 business days:

  • Script the POP ticket with the Deemed Incomplete Due to No Response script.
  • Click Done to complete ticket.

6

If the PO gets checked in after we initiate the above process:

  • Update the POP ticket with the PO Checked in script.



OON


  • When an item ships with an Out of Network carrier, it can ship from the supplier directly to the customer, from the supplier to our crossdock/HDO/3P DA, or from our crossdock/HDO/3P DA to the customer. In many instances, we can track the PO and provide the customer an update. In the event we are not able to track a PO that shows it shipped Out of Network, we would contact the supplier following the day 1, 2 and 3 process. 
  • When a PO is shipped via an out of network carrier to our Pool Point, be sure to continue your dock search if necessary.
  • The PRO and Carrier for an out of network carrier can be found with the order details on the ticket or order management.


The OON carriers listed below are the most common out of network carriers seen on orders, it is not an all inclusive list.



ABF


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Track PO

To track a PO shipping with ABF, go to the ABF website and use the credentials found in Passwordstate to log in.    


2

Select how you will be searching for the PO

Input the PRO Number, Bill of Lading Number, or Purchase Order Number into the Quick Start Search. Check the BOL, Order Page, Open Shipments Report (OSR), or Load Search for this information.


3

View Documents

Once on the Tracking Results page, click View Documents on the top left corner.


4

Select Bill of Lading

On the Documents Page, select Bill of Lading.


5Confirm the PO is Correct

Confirm the the PO on the BOL is correct.


6Email the Customer

Once you have confirmed that the PO is correct, use the information found on the BOL and carriers website to email the customer with the status of their order. 


7Update or enter CSO Ticket

 Update or enter a CSO Ticket as needed.


8Wrap

Wrap.


9Click DoneClick Done to complete the ticket.



Averitt


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Track PO

To track a PO shipping with Averitt, go to the Averitt website and use the credentials found in Passwordstate to log in.


2

Select how you will be searching for PO

1256176186.title: WIMS - Ship Status SOP (DSC2S)
1256176186.author: Former user (Deleted)
1256176186.body:
Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S)

Last Updated:  




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

Add text here.



Overview

Add text here.




Resources & Tools



EU Specific


Resource/Tool

Use Case

Passwordstate

EU Carrier tracking websites & login details

Transportation Event History

Check shipping labels & events for a PO

Universal Parcel Tracking

Useful universal tracking page that can be used to identify carrier if not obvious from Order or Tracking







Key Definitions



EU Specific


Key Word

Definition

Lutterworth/Mallow

UK Castlegate facility

Bejot

Bejot is a crossdock facility in Poland with large parcel trucks leaving to UK or IE. The goods are either injected or collected by Bejot, sorted by location and shipped to UK/IE by DHL Freight (no pallet registration required) and final mile delivery is actioned by AIT/WDN in UK and WDN in IE.

DoD

Day of Delivery







Before You Start

Purpose: To request a shipping status for large or small parcel orders showing no movement, that are past due with no backorder date or tracking, or when the shipment was delivered incomplete.

Notes: 

  • Replacements or refunds should be issued for orders deemed as lost or incomplete unless outreach is not required per policy.
  • If the customer responds to an email with a thank you or a statement with no clear question a follow up is not required. 
  • If the customer responds to an email with a clear question a response should be provided to the best of your ability or transferred to CSO. 
  • When an agent is responding to a customer, agents must only use the customer name and/or email address from the 'Customer Billing Address' Section, which can be found in Shipping View in Support Hub. 
  • If service adds an update that reopens a closed ticket and the information has already been provided and is readily available the ticket can be closed with no further outreach. 
    • Associates may close out the ticket with the "Customer Service Reopen" template 
  • An order is considered as a backorder if any date on the order has not passed. i.e. if the ship date has already passed but the delivery date is still in the future. When using the backorder template use the latest available date. 
  • If the customer reopens a ticket and a new ticket is needed for automation purposes close the original and run WIMS for a new ticket. Automation only runs on new tickets and the customer will be auto emailed the day 1 email. 
  • If the customer responds, reopening the ticket and all expectations have already been provided to the customer with all the information backorder date, tracking, how to track, etc. with template “Customer wants more information” template and close ticket.
    • If the customer continues to reply back, update ticket with “CT Responded, no response needed” template and close. Use best judgement to send to CSO or reply back 
  • Backorder is not clear on MyAccount so an email should be sent informing the customer of the correct date
  • If a dock search is needed use label “SendToE2E”. The label will copy the ticket to E2E, update and close the ticket. No further action will be needed. 
  • When applying the SendtoCSO label to tickets, a component must be added in order to close the ticket; as these are not monitored, agents can select any component they would like.

When to send to CSO: 

  • Address Change
  • Customer requests a discount or a free return
  • Orders that have an Outstanding Balance on the order (visible because there will be a banner indicating the OSB) need to be transferred to CSO if the customer is unable to settle through their My Account, if they paid using their Wayfair Credit Card, or need help adding a new card to their account.
  • BlackHawk, Samsung, GE, OON that can’t be tracked, etc. 


Supplier Requesting Email: For instances when suppliers request that WIMS contact a separate contact through email or phone to obtain the ship status of the purchase order, WIMS should proceed with requesting the ship status through the ticket and utilize the Invalid Request-Day 1 template.





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Process



Before You Start


mediuminfo

Complete these steps prior to proceeding with any processes outlined ahead


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check order page to determine if ticket or CT outreach from C2S is needed or appropriate
  1. Delivered, Canceled, Refunded, Returned or Replaced: If the ticket is reopened and  the PO is delivered, canceled, refunded, or replaced, email the CT with any tracking and/or delivery updates & close ticket.
    1. If the ticket is not reopened change the status to Waiting on Supplier and wait for automation to clear the alert
  2. RMT: There are no special rules for RMT. We cannot run the wizard so if any wizard is needed or customer replies back in after the ticket has been closed, reopen the RMT ticket and close the ship status ticket. 
  3. Platinum +: Platinum tickets close automatically. It is Platinum responsibility to contact the carrier’s/supplier’s outside of tickets. Sometimes tickets are reopened. Provide as much information as possible as a ticket update and close the ticket. 
  4. CAT / Wayfair Registry (The Knot) / VIP: Provide as much information as possible as an internal update and complete the ticket.
  5. CARE: Please check to see if the CARE ticket is closed/open. If closed, we will continue outreach as needed. If not closed, provide as much information as possible as an internal update and complete ticket.

2

Supplier Specific 
  • Asir - Please see additional ticket templates for Asir templates. Asir is one of the only supplier’s that has a contract to ship from Istanbul and is not a scam supplier
  • Samsung  - Bannered Large appliance. Send to CSO, mark ticket as done
  • GE - Bannered Large appliance Send to CSO, mark ticket as done
  • Any supplier bannered Large Appliance. Send to CSO, mark ticket as done
  • LG - Send to CSO, mark ticket as done
  • Masterbrand - Send to CSO, mark ticket is done and update CSO ticket with Masterbrand template
  • Blackhawk - Gift cards can be sent digitally. Update ticket with the Blackhawk template and label send to CSO, wrap internally and mark as done



Automation


Customer emails sent via GrayMatter will not be visible in the SupportHub interface, but can be accessed by using the Outbox Email Notification Tool. A comment will appear in the ticket stating the email was sent by GrayMatter.


Tool Instructions for Agents:

To view an email:

  1. Open the Outbox Email Notification Tool

Set filters:

  1. Environment: Production
  2. Email Type: Triggered
  3. Start and End Dates: Match the timeframe when the email was likely sent
  4. Expand Additional Filters > Input the Order ID
  5. Click Search
  6. Click the email row to view full content


#7f187fWhitewhite3#7f187fsolidAutomation


Automation Categories

Description

1st EmailWhen a ticket is entered the customer will be emailed automatically when the ticket is created. 
Backordered:Tickets with a PO that has been backordered by the supplier and now has a future ship date will be closed  and the customer emailed with a backorder template when automation is run. 
Canceled/RefundedTickets with a PO that has been canceled, refunded or return refund will be closed and the customer emailed with a canceled  template when automation is run. 
Has Scan DataTickets with a PO that has been scanned in network and shows movement will be closed and the customer emailed with a shipped  template when automation is run. 
Is Replaced

Tickets with a PO that has been replaced with a new PO will be closed and the customer will not be emailed when automation is run.

Note: No email is triggered as the customer does receive a system generated email when a PO is replaced.

LITTickets with a PO that has been deemed lost in transit will be closed and the customer emailed advising that the item has been deemed lost in transit, and asking if they would like a replacement or refund when automation is run. Customer is provided 2 business days to respond before we proceed with refund or replacement. 
Invalid WIMS - Not OverdueTickets with a PO that has a future ship date and are not backordered  will be closed and the customer will not be emailed when automation is run. 
Mis-MatchTickets with a PO where the must ship by date provided to the customer does not match the must ship by date provided to the supplier will be closed and the customer emailed with a backorder template when automation is run. 
Bulk CancelTickets that age 10 days and are not marked as shipped and have no update are canceled, and the customer notified through the canceled/refunded automation. 
PO Not Printed/ShippedWhen the PO is in a NOT Printed/Shipped or item is processing status a ticket needs to be entered to fix the issue. If there is an outstanding balance the ticket is assigned to CSO. Everything else a fraud ticket needs to be entered. 
IncompleteTickets that have a PO that is marked as shipped and does not show movement is considered incomplete. WIMS will manually run the wizard and use the notes, chats and current stock to determine refund or replacement. Customer will be notified of the action taken through one of the other automation buckets. 
Samples/SwatchesTickets for samples/swatches are closed automatically at time of entry addressing Wayfair Team to issue a resolution if 14 days have passed. 
DuplicateTickets that have another open ticket on them are closed automatically upon ticket entry as a duplicate. 





1st Automation


mediuminfo

If 10 days has not passed and a ticket gets assigned, change status to waiting on supplier and remove yourself from assigned



Bulk Cancel


medium

The ticket is labeled WIMS_Incomplete_bulkCXL


#

Scenario

Actions

Visuals

1

Has the supplier previously denied the cancellation?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Look at the ship date. Supplier is responsible to ship, if they denied cancellation and never shipped the order or provided an update we can request cancellation. 




2

Is the item MTO?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse
  1. Add the 'SendToCSO' label
  2. Add an update in an internal comment: "This item is MTO, please refer to associated CMT ticket."
  3. Close out the ticket under a Done/Won't Do status




3

Does the order have a tracking number?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Mark as shipped, if it’s been more than 10 days replace/refund as incomplete. If less than 10 days, label with WIMS_has_scan_data






Backordered


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Has the customer previously been told that the order is on backorder? 


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Email customer with Order backordered 2nd time template, wrap, and mark as done



IF: No

Is the item made to order? 

IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Email the customer with order backordered made to order template, wrap and mark as done



IF: No


mediumtipfalse

Email the customer with order is backordered template, wrap and mark as done





2

Has the latest date, including delivery date, past?


IF: Yes

 Check order management. Has the date in order management passed?

IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Close ticket manually with no update.  Run WIMS wizard to obtain a new ticket



IF: No


mediumtipfalse

Email the customer with appropriate backorder template, providing the date in Order Management as the backorder date, wrap and mark ticket as done




Check order page for latest date.



3

Expectations Already Set
  • If expectations have already been set, complete ticket with CT responded, no answer needed. If customer continues to reply back use Customer wants more information template

4

Is the backorder in the notes only and no automation has gone out? 


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Label WIMS_Backordered




5Is customer asking for a guarantee on a backordered item?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Email customer with template “CT Requests Guarantee on BO Delivery”, wrap and mark as done






Cancelled/Refunded


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Does the customer want to know when they can expect their refund, or advise to yes please cancel?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Update with CT Responded to Ticket, No Response Needed and close. The ticket template used by automation already sets these expectations




2

Does the customer want answers as to why their order was cancelled?


IF: Yes


Was the order cancelled by bulk automation? (i.e. cancelled in bulk by Tina Lund or other AOPS person 10 days after ticket entry)


IF: Yes

Email customer with Cancelled due to Automation script, wrap and mark as done


IF: No

Email with Cancelled by another user script, wrap and mark as done


IF: No

Order was not cancelled it was refunded due to incomplete or LIT.  Email customer with Deemed LIT/Incomplete - Refunded template, wrap and mark as done





3

Was the order cancelled due to discontinued?


IF: Yes

Email customer with template “Canceling/Refunding Discontinued”, wrap and mark as done





Has Scan Data


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Is customer concerned about the terms Good News or order on track?


IF: Yes

Has the order shipped Small Parcel? 


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Customer has already received tracking from an automated message. No further information is beneficial to them. Complete ticket with CT Responded, No Response Needed template and mark as done




IF: No

Then order has shipped Large Parcel. Does MyAccount (top number on order page) delivery date show in the future and all scans indicate no issues? 


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Original email advised customer how to track. No further information is needed. Update ticket with CT Responded, No Response Needed template and close ticket



IF: No


mediumwarningfalse

The order shows on track with no issues in PO history and not passed the dates that Wayfair’s tools show. Email customer with Update on Shipment Delay and provide relevant information, wrap and mark as done.





2

Is the order already delivered?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Update ticket with PO Canceled, Refunded, Replaced, Returned - Bannered Order template and close



IF: No


mediumwarningfalse

Continue Researching




3

Is the order on disposition or is a dock search needed?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

use Label SendtoE2E. Ticket will update and auto close




4

Is the customer in Canada and did the order ship small parcel?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Order is in transit, it is less than 14 days since last leg scan. Send customer template Canada Order, wrap and mark as done



IF: No


mediumwarningfalse

It is more than 14 days since last leg scan. Replace/refund order as LIT if movement or Incomplete if no movement and add appropriate label to close ticket




5Did the customer want to cancel but was told the order had shipped?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Email the customer with “CT Asks to Cancel Shipped Order” template, wrap and mark as done






Is Replaced


mediuminfo

Tickets with a PO that has been replaced with a new PO will be closed and the customer will not be emailed when automation is run.

Note: No email is triggered as the customer does receive a system generated email when a PO is replaced.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Is the order unshipped?


IF: Yes

Is the order late? 

IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Do not update ticket, close ticket and run WIMS wizard to open a new ticket on the new replacement PO



IF: No


mediumtipfalse

Order is not late, Email customer with Deemed LIT/Incomplete - Replaced template, wrap and mark as done




IF: No

Order is not unshipped. Does order show movement? 

IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Remove all labels and save. Add label WIMS_Has_Scan_data



IF: No


mediumwarningfalse

Order is not unshipped and does not show movement. Close ticket with no update and reopen new ticket on new PO number







LIT


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Did the customer respond within 48 hours?


IF: Yes
  • Yes - Issue a refund or replacement based on the preference the customer advised when they emailed back


IF: No
  • No - Issue a replacement unless the item is out of stock or the customer has stated in the order notes, ticket or conversation history that the customer prefers a refund



2

Is the order Tried and True?


IF: Yes
  • Yes - Issue a replacement as a refund is not an option on free orders. If the customer requested a refund label with SendToCSO, wrap internal contact and mark as done



3

Is the PO over a year old?


IF: Yes
  • Yes - Email customer with template “PO is +1YR Old”, wrap and mark as done





Invalid WIMS - Not Overdue


mediuminfo

These orders have not passed the original ship date. Make sure we are checking the order and Partner Home and using the latest date available including delivery date. Rules should not be much different than backorder rules. 



Mis-Match


mediuminfo

These orders have told the customer they will ship at a different time then what the supplier was told. Make sure we are checking the order and Partner Home and using the latest date available including delivery date. Rules should not be much different than backorder rules. 



Incomplete


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Has the item shipped Small Parcel? 


IF: Yes
  1. Check tracking number on carrier’s website
  2. Does the tracking number show movement? 


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Add label WIMS_Has_Scan_Data



IF: No


mediumwarningfalse

Read conversations, notes, ticket information and inventory. If there is nothing that indicates the order should be canceled/refunded then a replacement should be ordered. 




IF: No

Order has shipped Large Parcel. Has the order shipped in network?

IF: Yes

Check PO History, Global Vis, carrier websites, Load Search, etc

Is the order on track and not passed any of the delivery dates found in the tools?

IF: Yes

Add label Has_Scan_data


IF: No

Add Label SendtoE2E



IF: No

Order shipped out of network. Check the tools in Large Parcel section. Some supplier’s have specific rules on how to track

Does the order have a carrier and PRO number listed in the carrier information. IE UPS Freight and a tracking number.

IF: Yes

Track the order on the carrier’s direct website. 

Does the order show delivered or in transit with no indicated issues and is on track to deliver?

IF: Yes

Add Label WIMS_Has_scan_data


IF: No

Refund or replace



IF: No

If there are not other rules for the specific supplier add label SendToCSO and update the CSO ticket with “Update CSO for OON Shipments” template 





2

Is the PO over a year old?


IF: Yes

Email customer with template “PO is +1YR Old”, wrap and mark as done



3

Is the order Tried and True?


IF: Yes

You can only replace tried and true orders if the customer wants a refund add label SendToCSO



4

Has the order shipped less than 10 days ago?


IF: Yes


Tracking does not show movement but it's less than 10 days
  • Email customer with “Delays in Shipping of Up to 10 Days”, wrap and mark as done


Tracking shows a scan within the last 10 days but is delayed
  • Email the customer with “PO Shows Movement Within Last 10 Days” wrap and mark as done 






Samples/Swatches


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Has the order been marked shipped 14 days or longer?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse

Replace



IF: No


mediumwarningfalse

Email customer the Samples template, wrap and mark ticket as done






Duplicate


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Is there already an open and being worked LOM or E2E ticket for the same PO that is being monitored?


IF: Yes

Update ticket with template “Duplicate Ticket” and complete ticket



2

Is there an open ticket for the same PO already open and being worked? 


IF: Yes

Update the appropriate ticket as a duplicate and close. Duplicate WIMS are automated but if there is a reopen it may cause 2 to be open, and randomly some are missed as being flagged for duplicate





Other Reasons for Outreach


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Did we send the order to E2E and customer is now reopening ticket?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse
  • Yes - Email customer with template “Initiated Dock Search” wrap internal contact and mark ticket as done




2

Is the customer seeking a discount?


IF: Due to Inconvience


mediumtipfalse
  • Email customer with template “CT Asking for Discount”, wrap and mark ticket as done



IF: Future Order / Coupon Code


mediumtipfalse
  • Email customer with template “CT Wants Discount on Future Purchase”, wrap and mark as done



Please see additional template link for more templates on price match, discounts, exchange.



3

Is the customer asking for a price match?


IF: Within 7 Days of Purchase


mediumtipfalse
  • Email customer with template “Requests for Price Match within 7 Days of Order Being Placed - Adjust for Delivered/Not Delivered”, wrap and mark as done



IF: Outside 7 Days of Purchase


mediumtipfalse
  • Email customer with template “Requests for Price Match Outside 7 Days of Order Being Placed - Adjust for Delivered/Not Delivered”, wrap and mark as done



Please see additional template link for more templates on price match, discounts, exchanges 



4

Is the customer asking for their order to be held?


IF: Small Parcel


mediumtipfalse
  • Email customer with template “CT Asking for PO to be Held”, wrap and mark as done



IF: Large Parcel


mediumtipfalse
  • Email customer with template “Send to CSO”, wrap internally and mark as done. Add label SendToCSO




5Is customer asking for a manager?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse
  • Email customer with template “CT Asked for a Manager”, wrap and mark as done




6Have we given customer all information that we can and it has not satisfied them?


IF: Yes


mediumtipfalse
  • Email customer with template “CT Wants More Information”, wrap and mark done. After this template is sent unless the order goes late we will stop communication. Send to CSO if necessary




7Are we sending to CSO?


IF: Sending to CSO on a Reopen


mediumtipfalse
  • Email the customer with template “Send to CSO”, wrap internal and mark ticket as done



IF: Not Reopened


mediumtipfalse
  • Do not email customer, it will confuse them to be told we are looking into it and then being told we cannot assist and that someone else will reach out








Emailing the Customer


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Verify the Brand on the Order

Verify the Brand on the Order (e.g., Wayfair, Perigold, AllModern, etc.).


2

Initiate Customer Email

Initiate Customer Email:

  1. Click "Email Customer" in the Available Actions section of the ticket.
  2. If using ServiceHub, go to Outbound Contact and change the Dropdown to Email—this will redirect you to SupportHub to send the email.



3

Select the Correct Email Template

Select the Correct Email Template:

  1. Open the "Email Template" dropdown. (Visual 3)
  2. Choose the Store Brand Template that matches the order’s store.

Visual 3:

4

Retitle the Email

Retitle the Email:

  1. Format: "Information on your (Store Brand) (Order#)"


5

Send the Appropriately Branded Template

Send the Appropriately Branded Template to the Customer.




Wrapping



Process


mediuminfofalse
  • All outgoing contacts to customer’s need to be wrapped.
  • We do not wrap tickets where we do not make outreach to customers as a result of policy: Platinum, ERT, VIP, etc...
  • All wraps must be done on the ticket and not the order or after running the wizard so that they do not interfere with frontline’s metrics 
  • Wrapping sends the customer a survey. We do not want to send surveys to customers when we are transferring their ticket to E2E or CSO and these will be wrapped internally 
    • When transferring a ticket to CSO, we should wrap as Internal Contacts/Transfers. See CSO Wrap for more information.
    • When entering a ticket for a Dock Search, we should wrap our customer outreach as Internal Contacts/Transfers. See E2E Wrap for more information.
  • The system will only allow you to wrap every 15 minutes. If 2 emails are sent within 15 minute timeframe it is only expected to be wrapped once. 
  • A wrap can be completed through the Service Hub using the Wrap button at the top right of the page.


#

  4. Select Email as the Method of Contact. (Visual 2.4)

Labels



Before You Start


mediuminfofalse
  • All WIMS labels start with WIMS_ except for samples and SendToCSO
  • All Labels will automatically close tickets except for SendToCSO and WIMS_Missing_Fraud_Check, unless it is labeled as WIMS_Reopened_Needs_Review
  • If labeled with WIMS_Reopened_Needs_Review and we are providing a different label than was previously provided we will need to remove all labels, save and then relabel with appropriate label. Example: Order was backordered but has now shipped 
  • We should only have to email customer’s when the WIMS_Reopened_Needs_Review is present. The other labels should automatically appear at some point when resolution is made
  • Labels are case sensitive
  • Everything has a label. If the ticket is assigned to you and there is not any label this is an indication that 10 days has not passed



Manually Applied Labels


#7B189FWhitewhite3#7B189FsolidManually Applied Labels


Label Categories

Description

SendToCSO

This label will be manually added to any order that needs follow up by CSO. 

Note: Does not work on email tickets, if an email ticket is being worked you will need to label a previous ship status ticket or tell the customer to contact service

WIMS_Backordered

The system will use this label when the PO is backordered to automatically close the ticket. We can add WIMS_Backordered when necessary if the system didn’t pick it up or if the customer reopens and the order wasn’t previously backordered

WIMS_Cancelled

The system will use this label when the PO has been canceled, refunded or a return refund has been issued.  We can add this label when necessary if the system didn’t pick it up or if the customer reopens and it wasn’t previously labeled as WIMS_Cancelled

WIMS_Has_Scan_Data

The system will use this label when the PO has scans with the carrier.  We can add this label when necessary, the supplier shipped on their own account and we can track it or if the customer reopens and this label wasn’t previously used 

WIMS_Replaced

The system will use this label when the PO has been replaced. We can add this label when necessary, if the system didn’t pick up that the order was replaced or if the customer reopens and this label wasn’t used previously




Automatically Applied Labels


#7B189FWhitewhite3#7B189FsolidAutomatically Applied Labels


Label Categories

Description

WIMS_Incomplete_IssueRefund

The system will add this label automatically when the ticket ages 10 days to let us know to issue a resolution. There may be false incompletes in this bucket. Always check tracking. This label will not need to be manually added.

WIMS_O2D_Mismatch

The system will add this label automatically when the ship date does not match the expectations provided to the customer.This label will not need to be manually added. 

WIMS_Confirm_Pending_Cancellation

The system will add this label to the ticket when the order is in a pending cancellation state. If the ticket is over 10 days and this label exists please confirm the cancellation. The automation will then update the ticket label as WIMS_Cancelled. This label will not need to be manually added.

WIMS_IncompleteBulkCXL

The system will add this label to the ticket when the ticket has aged 10 days and needs to be canceled. This label will not need to be manually added. 

WIMS_Invalid_Not_Overdue

The system will add this label when the order has not passed it’s must ship by date. This label will not need to be manually added. 

WIMS_LIT_Provide_Resolution

The system will add this label when the order has been deemed LIT. This automation will email the customer asking them if they would like a refund or replacement. Once we have manually actioned this ticket type, automation will automatically add the appropriate label. This label will not be manually added.


WIMS_Missing_Fraud_Check

The system will add this label when the order is on hold due to missing a fraud check. Only the person working orders on hold will need to occasionally manually add this label. If you see this label and the PO has not been replaced or refunded and it has been 10 days please cancel the order.

sample

The sample label is manually added in bulk each morning by the person running automation. This is added to all Sample orders to update the ticket with the sample script and close the ticker. This label does not need to be manually added.

WIMS_Reopened_Needs_Review

System automatically adds this label when the customer responds to the ticket and it is reopen. There are occasionally false label add on this label. Remove label and add appropriate label if the customer has not responded. This label can be manually removed but will not need to be manually added.

WIMS_MTO

The system will add this label when the order is identified as a Made to Order item. This label will not need to be manually added.




EU Specific


mediumnotefalse
  • When applying the SendToCSO label to tickets, a component must be added in order to close the ticket; as these are not monitored, agents can select any component they would like.
  • Dock Search: If a LP dock search is needed use label “SendtoEUOC”. The label will copy the ticket to EUOC, update and close the ticket. No further action will be needed.





Utilizing CSO



NA Specific



Topics NOT Requiring CMT Intervention


mediumnotefalse

Topics Not Requiring CMT Intervention:

  • Holding Purchase Orders (PO)
  • Inquiries on shipment delays/arrivals
  • Requests to speak with a supervisor/manager
  • Price match or discount requests
  • Cancellations of shipped items
  • Assistance with purchase orders over a year old
  • Delivery date guarantees on backordered items
  • Updates on shipment delays



Topics Requiring CMT Intervention


mediumnotefalse

Topics Requiring CMT Intervention: 

  • An outstanding balance exists on the order
  • A different wizard (i.e., Report a Problem, Returns) needs to be run
    • Refunds example: Customer refund did not post as anticipated, wizard will need to be run
  • Unable to run the WIMS wizard





EU Specific



Topics Requiring EU CRT/CAT Intervention


mediumnotefalse

Topics Requiring EU CRT/CAT Intervention:

  • Address Change/Other wizard needs to be run
  • Customer requests a discount or a free return (exception cases)
  • Orders that have an Outstanding Balance on the order (visible because there will be a banner indicating the OSB) need to be transferred to CSO if the customer is unable to settle through their My Account, if they paid using their Wayfair Credit Card, or need help adding a new card to their account.

mediuminfo
  • SLA on these is outreach to the customer within 8 business hours / 1 business day

EU Tips & Tricks



Suppliers Shipping on their own Accounts (SSOA)


mediuminfo
  • Occasionally EU suppliers can ship domestically with their own courier. If you do not recognize the carrier name or the tracking number prefix, this Universal Parcel tracking page can be useful for identifying which carrier a Supplier may have used.
  • Supplier's own courier can sometimes be listed in their transportation matrix, which can be accessed through the order by selecting Actions > Manage Supplier > Logistics.
  • The supplier will mark orders as shipped manually and should upload tracking for their own carrier.
  • When tracking a PO with the Suppliers own courier, it is always good to see if there’s another point of reference matching that it’s a Wayfair order (if there is no contact from the Supplier or nothing in the Shipping view) such as the delivery postcode/area scanned is near or matches the customers postcode or the PO number if referenced on the couriers website. 
  • Suppliers shipping on their account will often use well known EU couriers, which can be found below.

Bejot


mediuminfo

Shipping Methods with Bejot (SUS Shipping):

There are two setup types:

  1. Injection: One shipping label per box (final mile carrier label only).
  2. Collection: Two shipping labels per box; one for first mile (Bejot) and one for the final mile carrier (in the destination country). 

Details on how these methods appear in the logistics matrix can be found in the playbook and in the Carriers SOP here.

#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Objective

Add text here.



Overview

Add text here.




Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool

Use Case

NA Partner Support Tech Issue Handbook

This guide gives an overview of how to enter and manage tech tickets within NA Partner support. This includes:

  • How to enter Tech tickets and what to include
  • How to properly escalate tickets
  • Ticket follow up and when to loop in Tier II support

C2S: Tech Ticket Entry Triage Guide

This guide will assist you in deciding if the tech ticket you're submitting, or the team you're reaching out to is the appropriate choice for resolving the supplier's issue.

GPS Known Tech Issues Dashboard

The link is configured to filter for any open tech issues that are relevant to us in the Click-to-Ship space. VCN Leads/Agents do not change the preset filters.  - VCN LINK Preset to tickets to view.



Key Definitions


Key Word

Definition













#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Ticket Updates


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Managing Multi-PO Tickets

Handling Multiple Purchase Orders (POs) per Ticket

  • 1–15 POs: If a supplier includes up to 10 POs in a single ticket, please assist them with all POs within that ticket.
  • More than 15 POs (Project Discretion): For tickets involving more than 15 POs, use your discretion to determine if a separate project is necessary.
    • Project Unnecessary (Simple Requests): If the request is simple (e.g., resending POs), a project is generally not required.
    • Project Required (Complex, High-Volume Requests): If the request is complex or involves an action like rerouting 10 or more POs, it should be entered as a project.
  • Project Procedure: If you decide to create a project, advise the supplier that the issue will be addressed separately due to the volume. Refer to for instructions on submitting a project.

2

Format: Support Greetings

Greeting Guidelines

    • For internal CS Wayfair teams: "Hi Team" or "Wayfair Team" is sufficient.
    • For HDO, 3PDA, Carrier, or Supplier teams: Use their name, including location if applicable.
      • Examples: "JW Logistics - Tulsa Team," "Linden- NJ Team," "Estes Team," or "Closet Maid Team."
    • For Customers: Utilize the billing email address and contact name, as opposed to the shipping contact information. This is to align with OTD communication when outreaching customers. 


For the preferred hierarchy when addressing the supplier team or individual, please consult the Response Structure section within the [GPS QC Rubric (DSC2S)].



3

Format: Clear Supplier Communications

  1. Essential Update Content:
    1. Standard Update: Provide a standard update reflecting the current situation, following the corresponding SOP.
    2. Empathy and Assurance: Include an appropriate empathy and assurance statement.
    3. Wayfair PO: The Wayfair PO number is required in all updates.
      1. Include the PO in the subject line and body when emailing LTL carriers.
      2. Including the PO helps distinguish the item in question in order notes without needing to open the ticket.
    4. Key Information: When available and appropriate, include the ETA, carrier, pro number, and next steps.
    5. Review Scripts: Any suggested script containing placeholders (e.g., "XXX" or other ALL CAPS text) must be updated before submitting the update.
  2. Professional Tone and Communication Standards:
    1. Tone and Content: Maintain a clearly professional tone and content for all communication.
    2. Avoid Unprofessional Language:
      1. Tone/Wording: Do not use rude, disparaging, or condescending language directed to/about any party (e.g., unnecessary bolding, all caps, or italics).
      2. Urgency: Avoid using urgency-related terms such as ASAP, as soon as possible, urgently, or quickly with regard to supplier action. Tickets are worked in the order submitted; implying one issue is more urgent than others is unprofessional.
      3. Inappropriate Comments: Refrain from unprofessional comments (e.g., cursing, explicit or violent content, politics).
      4. Text/Emojis: Do not use emojis or inappropriate text language abbreviations (e.g., "u" instead of "you," "ty" instead of "thank you"). Appropriate emojis are acceptable only if an internal party used them first and we are reciprocating; never use emojis externally.
      5. Formatting: Copy-pasted text must be edited to be reader-friendly.
  3. Documentation and Ticket Closure:
    1. Ticket Updates (SPS): All tickets must be updated by Supplier Pickup Support with all relevant information to the request before closing, unless the ticket was fully answered/resolved or is a duplicate.
    2. External Action Documentation: Document any action taken outside of the ticket, such as:
      1. Interactions with an account manager where the reasoning is not visible to the SPS department.
      2. Reaching out to another team or individual to confirm or obtain information that is not universally available.
    3. Attachments and Links:
      1. Links: All links must lead to the intended information.
      2. Documents: Relevant documents must be attached when applicable:
        1. BOL: Attach the Bill of Lading (BOL) if a PO was registered more than 24 hours before receiving the ticket request. Attaching a BOL is not necessary if the PO was registered within 24 hours.
        2. Registration Documents: Attach the registration/re-registration document when appropriate or if the update advises it was attached.

4

Add Correct Email Closings

Closing Signatures for Ticket and Email Communication

  • Carrier Pickup Delay Tickets: Always include a personal closing, such as "Thank you, Your Name."
  • FirstMile@ Emails: A signature is required. Use either "Thank you, The Wayfair Team" or your individual name.

5

Use Required Closings

A closing such as "Thank you, Your_Name". Your name is required. 




Managing Tickets and Emails in SupportHub and Service Hub


The information below outlines how to action tickets and emails in SupportHub and Service Hub. Please select the relevant tab for next steps. 



Actioning Tickets/Emails in ServiceHub


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open your queue

  1. Navigate to Service Hub.
    1. Tickets assigned via the DRE routing system with a status of "Waiting for Support" appear in the left-hand panel, sorted by oldest at the top. (Visual 1.1)

Visual 1.1:

2

Navigate the ticket

  1. Click on a ticket within the queue on the left hand panel to open it.
  2. See Visual 2.1 for an indication of where to find:
    1. Ticket number
    2. Edit ticket title
    3. SLA
    4. Status
    5. Customer Request type (Reference Correct Ticket Pathways)
    6. Order ID and PO Number
    7. Ticket Description 
    8. Submission Language
    9. Change ticket status
  3. See Visual 2.2 for an indication of where to:
    1. View attachments
    2. Add attachments
    3. View linked tickets
    4. Link tickets
    5. See further ticket updates
    6. Submit ticket updates

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

3

Review Duplicate Tickets

Handling Duplicates (Multiple Open Tickets for the Same PO):

  1. Prioritize: Work the oldest ticket and close the newer ones.
  2. Recent Updates/Escalations: Pay attention to any duplicate ticket that contains new updates or supplier escalations, and prioritize working those issues.

Closing Duplicate Tickets:

  1. Selection: Close the ticket using the "Done - Duplicate" option.
  2. Communication: When closing a duplicate, the update must clearly state the issue and provide the number of the ticket where the work will continue (see script). This ensures the supplier knows the issue has been acknowledged and is being handled. 

4

Review supporting tabs

  1. In the right-hand panel of Service Hub, you can find the tabs outlined below: (Visual 4.1)
    1. Customer: View customer account details
    2. Order: View order details, including billing and payment information, and the Order Trail 
    3. Product: View all product details including dimensions, carrier and tracking. You can also edit the Shipping information here. (Visual 4.2)
    4. Supplier: View all information available in Manage Supplier and Waystation Notes.
    5. Carrier: In production
    6. Finance: View details available in Finance view of the order
    7. Knowledge: Search option for a variety of internal topics
    8. Search: Locate order details when this isn't pulled automatically from the ticket.  
  2. The Order, Product, and Supplier tabs will be most relevant when working tickets. 
  3. Wizards can be run from the Order and Product tabs.

Visual 4.1:

Visual 4.2:

5

Respond to Ticket

  1. After you have completed the necessary action on the order, use the Add Comment option to reply to the Supplier. (Visual 5.1)
    1. Select Templates and choose the relevant category to access canned responses.
    2. Use the search bar to locate the relevant template.
    3. Select the template text to add it to your response. (Visual 5.2)
    4. Ensure to make any necessary edits to the templates so that they are tailored to the supplier’s specific inquiry
    5. Select Post Externally to share your response with the supplier. (Visual 5.3)
    6. Select Post Internally to add internal comments not shared with the supplier. (Visual 5.3)

Visual 5.1:

Visual 5.2:

Visual 5.3:

6

Respond to Email

  1. From ServiceHub, select Outbound Contact then Email (Visual 6.1). The email will open in another tab where you should take the following actions

    1. Select the preferred From Address in the dropdown. 
    2. The supplier’s email address will be prepopulated as the recipient, and can be changed if necessary.
    3. Add CC/BCCs if necessary.
    4. The email's subject will be the same as the issue's title, edit if necessary. 
    5. Type Email body. (Tip 1)
    6. Add attachments if necessary.
    7. Preview/Send email. (Visual 6.2)


    Tip 1

    Shift-Enter allows you to single space emails (double space is the default).

    When using hyperlinks, use the right arrow key to end the hyperlink (otherwise everything will be included)- blue highlight indicates what will be hyperlinked

    To utilize spell check, hold Ctrl and right click the word.


Visual 6.1:

Visual 6.2:

7

Select Resolution Status

  1. When closing your ticket, ensure you choose the correct resolution below based on the outcome or action taken (Visual 7.1):
    1. Done:
      1. Use for any tickets you have successfully completed and closed.
      2. This will be the resolution selected most often.
    2. Duplicate:
      1. Use for any tickets that are a duplicate of another existing ticket.
    3. Won't Do:
      1. Acceptable to use for spam emails, or automated (including out of office) responses. (Tip 2)
      2. This usage is rare and most often applied to Email request types.


Tip 2

This should not be applied to reopened tickets where there was a true action taken when the ticket was originally marked as Done. 

These should be reclosed as Done > Done following the guidelines outlined in the Manual Components for Reopened Tickets tab. 


Visual 7.1:

8

Update Ticket Status

  1. If a response from the supplier is required, select Change Status < Waiting for Supplier. This will move the ticket out of your ServiceHub queue. (Visual 8.1)
  2. If the ticket is complete and requires no additional action by Wayfair or the supplier, select Change Status < Done (Visual 8.2)

Visual 8.1:

Visual 8.2:

#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open your queue

  1. Navigate to ServiceHub and select a ticket within the queue to open it. (Visual 1.1)

2

Navigate the Ticket

  1. Click on a ticket within the queue on the left hand panel to open it.

  2. See Visual 2.1 for an indication of where to find:

    1. Ticket number
    2. Edit ticket title
    3. SLA
    4. Status
    5. Customer Request type (Reference Correct Ticket Pathways)
    6. Order ID and PO Number
    7. Ticket Description 
    8. Submission Language
    9. Change ticket status
  3. See Visual 2.2 and 2.3 for an indication of where to:
    1. View attachments
    2. Add attachments
    3. View linked tickets
    4. Link tickets
    5. See further ticket updates
    6. Submit ticket updates

Visual 2.1:

Visual 2.2:

Visual 2.3:

3

Review Duplicate Tickets

Handling Duplicates (Multiple Open Tickets for the Same PO):

  1. Prioritize: Work the oldest ticket and close the newer ones.
  2. Recent Updates/Escalations: Pay attention to any duplicate ticket that contains new updates or supplier escalations, and prioritize working those issues.

Closing Duplicate Tickets:

  1. Selection: Close the ticket using the "Done - Duplicate" option.
  2. Communication: When closing a duplicate, the update must clearly state the issue and provide the number of the ticket where the work will continue (see script). This ensures the supplier knows the issue has been acknowledged and is being handled. 


4

Adjust Request Type

  1. If a ticket is mistakenly transferred using the incorrect request type, first update the request type to the correct category, if necessary, and then continue processing the ticket according to the established procedure.


5

Add Response

  1. Open the text box by selecting, "Click to add comment."
  2. Write ticket update or use a canned response
    1. To use a canned response, copy and paste the chosen canned response from section in InfoHub referencing the relevant SOP. 
  3. Post update. (Visual 5.1)
    1. To share your response with the Supplier, select Share with customer.
    2. If update is for internal use only, select  Comment internally.

Visual 5.1:

6

Update Ticket Status

  1. See Visual 6.1 regarding the following status usage:
    1. If a response from the supplier is required, select Change Status > Waiting for Supplier. This will move the ticket out of your ServiceHub queue. (Visual 6.1)
  2. If the ticket is complete and requires no additional action by Wayfair or the supplier, select Change Status < Done. (Visual 6.2)

Visual 6.1:



Linking Tickets


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Open the ticket

  1. Navigate to Service Hub and select a ticket within the queue to open it. (Visual 1.1)


Visual 1.1:

2

Access Linking Tool
  1. Expand the Linked Tickets dropdown in the ticket interface and select Add. (Visual 2.1)


Visual 2.1:


3

Enter ticket ID and Select Relationship

  1. Input the ticket ID for the ticket that needs to be linked and select Validate to confirm the ticket is accessible by the system. (Visual 3.1)
  2. Then change the relationship to Relates To and select Link Ticket to finalize the connection between the tickets.

Visual 3.1:




Searching for Tickets/Emails within SupportHub


This section explains the different ways you can search within SupportHub. 

Each tab outlines a different search method. 



Quick Search


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Quick Search

  1. Enter the ticket number/issue key into the search bar on SupportHub. (Visual 1.1; Tip 1)
  2. Select the ticket.
  3. This function will search in all projects on SupportHub.


Tip 1:

This is the fastest way to search within SupportHub. 

However, it is only useful when you have the specific issue key (ticket number).


Visual 1.1:



Basic Search


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Basic Search 
  1. Click on Issues > Search for issues. (Visual 1.1; Tip 1)
  2. Use the filters to refine your search.
  3. Apply any relevant filters by selecting an option from each dropdown menu. (Visual 1.2)
  4. Add more filters by selecting more and choosing what filters you need. (Tip 2)
  5. If you know the title contains specific information, add this into "contains text". (Visual 1.3)
  6. Edit the columns as needed using the columns dropdown. (Visual 1.4)


Tip 1:

This is the most efficient way to search for a ticket/email within SupportHub.

Correct Ticket Entry Pathways

To ensure Suppliers follow the correct ticket entry points to report their issues, you must educate them on the correct process to follow in the future. 

Example Phrasing: 

"To ensure we can help you as efficiently as possible, please report similar issues by entering an Order & PO Management > Problem with an Order / Other > Update Tracking Number/Order Status ticket."

This Help Centre article outlines the available ticket entry points for the Supplier. 

Below is an overview of the issues that should be reported through each ticket entry type. 

Tip 1:

Supplier education is not required for Problem with an Order tickets where the correct pathway should have been any of the below: 

  • Update Tracking Number/Order Status
  • Product is Out of Stock
  • Cancellation Inquiry
  • Reroute PO to a Different Warehouse
  • Shipping/Carrier Question

When the Supplier selects Problem with an Order through the Get Help pathway, tickets are not categorised. Based on this, you are not required to advise the Supplier that the incorrect pathway was followed. 





Communication in Tickets


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Jargon

Avoid using non-universal acronyms to prevent potential misinterpretations internally or externally.

  • If you have questions about a word or phrase that is potentially jargon, please check the glossary or reach out to your manager.


2

Answer-First Communication

Position your main idea or call to action at the very beginning of the message to reduce back-and-forth.

  • Goal: State the reason for the request immediately.
  • Elaborate: Provide supporting details and the PO number.
  • Next Steps: Clearly advise what action is needed.

3

Empathy & Assurance

Empathy

Definition of Empathy

Polite and respectful statements that show a person's understanding and care for another individual's problems.


Examples of Empathy
  • I’m so sorry your pickup was missed a 2nd time.  I can imagine how frustrating this is waiting to get this off your dock.
  • I can completely understand your frustration.
  • I understand how frustrating this must be for you.
  • I understand how this could be confusing.


Assurance

Definition of Assurance

A statement that inspires confidence that the reader's questions and/or issues will be resolved as efficiently as possible.


Examples of Assurance
  • I can definitely get your pickup taken care of today.
  • I can certainly look into applying your rewards dollars to your order.
  • I would be more than happy to help you with that item.


Examples Combining Empathy and Assurance
  • I am so sorry this has been really frustrating for you. I'm going to take care of this.
  • I am so sorry that CARRIER didn't pick up PO XXX. I will take care of this for you.
  • I understand how frustrating it is to not have your orders picked up on time. I assure you that I will get this resolved for you.
  • I am so sorry about the situation. I will get this resolved for you.
  • I am so sorry you are experiencing trouble with CARRIER picking up PO XXX. I would be happy to resolve this issue for you.
  • I apologize for the delay in pickup. I will definitely get this resolved for you.



4

Words/Phrases to Avoid

Don’t Say

  • Unfortunately we are unable to assist with…
  • CS123456789 was just registered yesterday….
  • I have already notified the carrier to reschedule

Do Say

  • At this time we do not have a process to assist with…
  • CS123456789 was recently registered on [ENTER_DATE]....
  • I reached out to the carrier on [ENTER_DATE] to reschedule your pickup….

5

Writing Structure for Clarifying Questions

Identify Key Components and Assumptions

  • Highlight critical terms in the question that carry the main ideas or points of inquiry. Determine who or what the question is regarding (i.e., a specific product, service, or issue).
    • Example: "In your recent inquiry about the shipment for PO number XYZ, are you assuming that the expected delivery date remains the same despite the recent warehouse changes?"

Paraphrase and Reflect Back

  • Restate the question in your own words to ensure you fully understand its meaning and intent, and rephrase part of the supplier's question to show understanding and prompt clarification.
    • Example: "So, you’re asking if the new expected delivery date for the out-of-stock items is still valid, correct? Just to clarify, are you referring to the items in PO number 12345?"

Use Open-Ended Questions

  • Start with phrases like, "Can you explain..." or "Could you describe...". This invites more detailed responses.’
    • Example: "Can you explain the reason for the shipping delay on PO number 12345?"

Be Specific

  • Reference particular details from the supplier's request to maintain focus.
    • Example: "Regarding your request for shipment timeline, can you provide the expected delivery date?"

Avoid Leading Questions

  • Ensure questions do not imply a desired answer.
    • Example: Instead of asking, "You meant to say the deadline is next week, right?", use "What is the deadline for this request?"

Limit Multiple Questions

  • Stick to one or two related questions to maintain clarity.
    • Example: "What additional specifications do you require, and when do you need them by?"

Use Simple Language

  • Aim for clear and straightforward language, avoiding jargon.
    • Example: "What exactly do you need me to help you with?"

Response Preparation

  • Once the question is analyzed, outline key points to address in your response. Organizing your thoughts ensures a clear and comprehensive answer that directly addresses the supplier's concerns.
    • Example: "To answer your inquiry about the shipping status for the order, I will provide the latest updates and outline any potential delays."



General Best Practices


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Multi-tasking

Frame your ticket response while waiting for PO numbers to generate or, begin research on your next ticket while current processes are loading


2

Slack Reminders

Use Slack to set reminders for issues that require collaboration with other departments to ensure nothing is missed.


3

Information Gathering

Gather as much information as possible independently before asking for clarification to reduce back-and-forth communication.


4

Anticipate Follow-ups

Aim to answer everything effectively and anticipate possible questions from either the supplier or collaborating teams.


5

Response Completeness

Always reread the supplier's request after completing your response to ensure every concern has been addressed.


6

Slack as a Search Engine

Use Slack to search for obscure threads, especially for label print errors.


7

Mental Resets

When encountering something strange or unexpected, take a quick breath and a mental reset to clear doubt.


8

Personalized Closings

Create a unique "general use" closing statement to add a human element and build stronger relationships with partners.


9

Mindset

Remain flexible, open to change, and never hesitate to ask questions.




Tagging a Tech Issue


redtextOffshore Agent Specific

This SOP outlines the procedure for tagging supplier-raised tickets associated with known technical issues, within the VCN workflow when an open tech ticket already exists. 

The purpose of this SOP is to enable VCN agents to identify and review existing open tech issues, as well as add relevant examples to open tickets. If you come across a tech issue, please note that you are not required to raise new tech tickets. The standard process for handling tickets remains the same — if a VCN agent is unable to complete a ticket, it should be escalated to the onshore team.

This guidance is being shared with VCN to help identify potential trends that may indicate a new tech issue. If no existing tech ticket is open, continue to flag widespread issues in the leads channel for your onshore POC. For isolated cases, move the ticket onshore following the usual process.

Use this SOP to review for issues, tag your ticket and complete where applicable or move onshore.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Check for Known Issues

  1. Navigate to the GPS Known Tech Issues Dashboard. The dashboard is already preset with the appropriate filters for applicable tickets. (Visual 1.1)
  2. Search to see if a ticket for the same issue has already been raised.
  3. If a ticket has not been found, check to see if a ticket has been raised to T3 Tech support; navigate to GPSTHD on the top of the page (Visual 1.1)


If an existing ticket is found
  1. Open it and add your specific example.
  2. Ensure all required details—date, system, and impacted users—are included.
  3. Check for a linked spreadsheet to add your example to the correct tab


If an existing ticket is not found
  1. Search GPSTHD (Global Partner Support Tech Help Desk) at the top of the dashboard to see if a ticket has already been raised to Tier 3 Tech Support.

Onshore Agents:

  1. Follow the guidelines in the NA Partner Support Tech Issue Handbook, or for Partner Home issues, refer to the C2S: Tech Ticket Entry Triage Guide to create a new ticket.

Offshore Agents:

  1. If an existing ticket is not found and you are blocked, flag in channel that you may have identified a new tech issue.
    1. Exclude Cannot Print a BOL / Packing Slip or Shipping Label workarounds, these do not require a new ticket.
  2. If you are unable to resolve the issue, move the ticket onshore following the standard T0 to T1 process.


Visual 1.1:

2

Leave an Internal Comment

  1. After identifying or logging a technical issue, an internal comment must be added to the corresponding ticket within ServiceHub or SupportHub using the following exact phrasing (Visual 2.1):
    1. Related Tech issue: (Insert ticket ID here)
  2. To confirm the comment has enabled the automation, you can see the label added with the tech details (Visual 2.2)
  3. Format guidance and examples for internal comments are as follows:
    1. ProjectHub ticket (Visual 2.3):
      1. Related Tech issue: PHI-3079
    2. SupportHub ticket (Visual 2.4):
      1. Related Tech issue: PHHELP-15621
    3. INC ticket (Visual 2.5):
      1. Related Tech issue: INC4213695
    4. Slack channel (Visual 2.6):
      1. Related Tech issue: Slack multichannel-fulfillment-forum
    5. All other scenarios (Visual 2.7):
      1. Related Tech issue: Tech

Closing Auto Replies


This section explains how to prevent tickets from reopening in cases where a closure triggers an auto reply from suppliers. 

  • This setting cannot be applied in bulk; it must be updated manually for each individual ticket.
  • This configuration is a SupportHub-only feature and is not visible or editable in the ServiceHub interface.


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Adjust Ticket Settings

  1. Select the ticket hyperlink to open it in SupportHub. (Visual 1.1)
  2. Select Edit at the top of the ticket to modify the ticket settings.
  3. Select the Email tab within the edit menu and scroll down to the Pause Outbound Emails section.  
  4. Select Yes from the dropdown menu and click Update to save your changes. (Visual 1.2)

  • GPS QC Rubric (DSC2S)

Updated Q1: Response Structure

Added the following bullets:

  • Opening statements are required in the following scenarios specifically:

    • Required on the initial response to an inbound supplier ticket i.e. "Thank you for submitting this request. I can resolve this for you today...."

    • Required for a follow-up comment where we were waiting on an internal team/fix to update the Supplier i.e. "Thank you for your patience while our team reviewed this..."

    • Required if a closed ticket is reopened by a supplier i.e. "Thanks for getting back to us to confirm..."

    • For all other scenarios, an opening statement is recommended where appropriate but is not required for QA purposes.

Edited the guidance for using opening statements to:

  • Reduce the number of appeals being submitted

  • Provide more clarity for agents

Therefore we educate the SU on if this occurs again how to request SKU Qty thresholds are added to those specific SKUs."

    • Added Step 7

Canned Responses/Templates

  • Updated tab – Change Ship Method

    • Updated template – SP to LP

  • Update the process for handling supplier requests to change shipping from Small Parcel (SP) to (LTL). When customers order multiple units of a product that normally ships SP, the combined weight or dimensions may exceed FedEx limits after carton consolidation or make LTL more cost-effective. In these cases, suppliers typically submit a Change Ship Method ticket for review by Global Partner Support (GPS) requesting upgrade from SP to LTL. 

  • This only applies to the NA process - EU process on change ship remains the same.

    • Labels

      • EU Specific

Our current process to manually add the label 'SendtoEUWC', which triggers the ticket to clone over to the EUWC project, is being updated to 'SendtoEUOC'. 

Order Support & Investigations has gained ownership over several projects recently, including EUWC and EUOC. They are looking to work out of one project - EUOC - and have asked we update our clone logic to create new tickets in the EUOC project going forward. The only change to the team would be the label manually updated - we would input 'SendtoEUOC' instead of 'SendtoEUWC'.

The automation rule has not been updated yet - wanted to address the SOP first.

Search Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) Content:

Global Dropship Click to Ship (DSC2S) 

Last Updated:  




#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FSOP Introduction


Purpose


Overview

Project Work Includes, (but is not limited to):

  • Mass Reroutes
  • Mass Order Tracking Status Updates

Projects are defined by requests regarding issues that include 10 or more PO's (at individual discretion).

If you need help determining if a request should be projected, please connect with your L2 for guidance. 

**If you put a project on this list, you are responsible for monitoring it. If reasonable progress is not happening, please connect with your L2 for assistance on project delegation.



Resources & Tools


Resource/Tool

Use Case

Projects ListSpreadsheet containing current and past projects.





#7B189Fwhite2#7B189FProcess/Workflow



Review the Process


#

Steps

Actions

Visuals

1

Navigate to Projects List

Navigate to Projects List and create a new line for the project. It must include the following:

  • Date Added
  • Required Completion Date
  • Project Owner Manager
  • Supplier
  • Quick Briefing - What needs to be done
  • Approximate Affected POs
  • Project Link
  • Other Notes
